## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:05:38.840Z

Good catch — that's cone mode's behavior, which always includes root-level files alongside the requested directories (`CMakeLists.txt`, `LICENSE`, `README.md`, etc.). We don't need any of them.

Switching to non-cone mode with an anchored pattern. One flag change and the root files stop appearing.

---

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:05:42.707Z

**→ Tool call: `Edit`** (id `tool-536`)

```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "[repo]/scripts/setup-baselines.sh",
  "old_string": "    rm -rf \"${target}\"\n    git clone --quiet --filter=blob:none --no-checkout \"${url}\" \"${target}\"\n    git -C \"${target}\" sparse-checkout init --cone\n    git -C \"${target}\" sparse-checkout set include\n    git -C \"${target}\" checkout --quiet \"${commit}\"",
  "new_string": "    rm -rf \"${target}\"\n    git clone --quiet --filter=blob:none --no-checkout \"${url}\" \"${target}\"\n    # Non-cone sparse-checkout with an anchored pattern. Cone mode would\n    # also pull every root-level file (LICENSE, CMakeLists.txt, README,\n    # etc.) alongside include/; the pattern below restricts the working\n    # tree to just the include subtree.\n    git -C \"${target}\" sparse-checkout set --no-cone '/include/'\n    git -C \"${target}\" checkout --quiet \"${commit}\""
}
```

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:09:35.645Z

Yes — collapsing them into a single `external/include/` is cleaner and lets the Makefile use one include path for any baseline. The way to do it: sparse-checkout per upstream as before, but immediately move each library's namespace subdir into a shared `external/include/`, then delete the now-empty clone. The clone is briefly transient but lives at its natural location (no separate tmp dir).

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:09:46.080Z

**→ Tool call: `Edit`** (id `tool-537`)

<details><summary><code>tool input</code></summary>

```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "[repo]/scripts/setup-baselines.sh",
  "old_string": "REPO_ROOT=\"$(cd \"$(dirname \"${BASH_SOURCE[0]}\")/..\" && pwd)\"\nEXTERNAL=\"${REPO_ROOT}/external\"\nmkdir -p \"${EXTERNAL}\"\n\nfor entry in \"${LIBS[@]}\"; do\n    read -r name url commit <<< \"${entry}\"\n    target=\"${EXTERNAL}/${name}\"\n\n    echo \"==> ${name} @ ${commit}\"\n\n    rm -rf \"${target}\"\n    git clone --quiet --filter=blob:none --no-checkout \"${url}\" \"${target}\"\n    # Non-cone sparse-checkout with an anchored pattern. Cone mode would\n    # also pull every root-level file (LICENSE, CMakeLists.txt, README,\n    # etc.) alongside include/; the pattern below restricts the working\n    # tree to just the include subtree.\n    git -C \"${target}\" sparse-checkout set --no-cone '/include/'\n    git -C \"${target}\" checkout --quiet \"${commit}\"\ndone\n\necho \"Done.\"",
  "new_string": "REPO_ROOT=\"$(cd \"$(dirname \"${BASH_SOURCE[0]}\")/..\" && pwd)\"\nEXTERNAL=\"${REPO_ROOT}/external\"\nINCLUDE_DIR=\"${EXTERNAL}/include\"\nmkdir -p \"${INCLUDE_DIR}\"\n\nfor entry in \"${LIBS[@]}\"; do\n    read -r name url commit <<< \"${entry}\"\n    clone=\"${EXTERNAL}/${name}\"\n\n    echo \"==> ${name} @ ${commit}\"\n\n    rm -rf \"${clone}\"\n    git clone --quiet --filter=blob:none --no-checkout \"${url}\" \"${clone}\"\n    # Non-cone sparse-checkout with an anchored pattern. Cone mode would\n    # also pull every root-level file (LICENSE, CMakeLists.txt, README,\n    # etc.) alongside include/; the pattern below restricts the working\n    # tree to just the include subtree.\n    git -C \"${clone}\" sparse-checkout set --no-cone '/include/'\n    git -C \"${clone}\" checkout --quiet \"${commit}\"\n\n    # Move each namespace subdir (e.g. cuco/, warpcore/) into the shared\n    # external/include/ tree, replacing any prior copy. Then delete the\n    # clone — including its .git — since we only need the headers.\n    for ns_dir in \"${clone}/include\"/*; do\n        ns=\"$(basename \"${ns_dir}\")\"\n        rm -rf \"${INCLUDE_DIR}/${ns}\"\n        mv \"${ns_dir}\" \"${INCLUDE_DIR}/${ns}\"\n    done\n    rm -rf \"${clone}\"\ndone\n\necho \"Done.\""
}
```

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:10:27.076Z

Makefile consolidation:

- `CUCO_INCLUDE` / `WARPCORE_INCLUDE` collapsed into a single `EXTERNAL_INCLUDE := external/include`.
- Per-library pattern rules (`baselines/cuco/%` and `baselines/warpcore/%`) collapsed into one (`baselines/%`) since both share the same `-I` flag.
- `HAVE_CUCO` / `HAVE_WARPCORE` detection logic unchanged in structure — just looks for `cuco` / `warpcore` under `external/include/` rather than the per-library paths.

Re-run `bash scripts/setup-baselines.sh` and `external/include/` will end up with just `cuco/` and `warpcore/` inside, no `.git`, no upstream chrome.

<details><summary><code>tool result</code></summary>

```
1	#pragma once
2	
3	// Shared benchmark infrastructure: CUDA error checking, event-based
4	// timing, append-mode CSV writer, deterministic uniform key generation.
5	// Independent of the test suite — benchmarks should not pull in
6	// <tests/...>.
7	
8	// Opt every benchmark TU into the gpurhh probe-counter API. Defined
9	// before any gpurhh include so that View::insert / View::get expose
10	// their counter parameters. See notes/design.md for the contract.
11	#define GPURHH_BENCHMARK_COUNTERS 1
12	
13	#include <gpurhh/hash_table.cuh>
14	
15	#include <cuda_runtime.h>
16	
17	#include <cstddef>
18	#include <cstdint>
19	#include <cstdio>
20	#include <cstdlib>
21	#include <filesystem>
22	#include <fstream>
23	#include <iostream>
24	#include <string>
25	
26	// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
27	// CUDA_CHECK: postfix error-check operator. Identical in shape to the macro
28	// in <tests/tests.cuh>; copy-pasted rather than shared because the benchmark
29	// suite intentionally does not depend on test infrastructure.
30	//
31	//     cudaMalloc(&p, n) >> CUDA_CHECK;
32	// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
33	
34	struct CudaCheckLoc {
35	    const char* file;
36	    int         line;
37	};
38	
39	inline void operator>>(cudaError_t e, CudaCheckLoc loc) {
40	    if (e != cudaSuccess) {
41	        std::fprintf(stderr, "CUDA error at %s:%d: %s\n",
42	                     loc.file, loc.line, cudaGetErrorString(e));
43	        std::exit(1);
44	    }
45	}
46	
47	#define CUDA_CHECK CudaCheckLoc{__FILE__, __LINE__}
48	
49	// CUDA event timer. begin() records the start event; end_ms() records
50	// the stop event, synchronizes on it, and returns elapsed milliseconds.
51	class EventTimer {
52	public:
53	    EventTimer() {
54	        cudaEventCreate(&start_) >> CUDA_CHECK;
55	        cudaEventCreate(&stop_)  >> CUDA_CHECK;
56	    }
57	    ~EventTimer() {
58	        cudaEventDestroy(start_);
59	        cudaEventDestroy(stop_);
60	    }
61	    EventTimer(const EventTimer&)            = delete;
62	    EventTimer& operator=(const EventTimer&) = delete;
63	
64	    void begin() { cudaEventRecord(start_) >> CUDA_CHECK; }
65	    float end_ms() {
66	        cudaEventRecord(stop_) >> CUDA_CHECK;
67	        cudaEventSynchronize(stop_) >> CUDA_CHECK;
68	        float ms;
69	        cudaEventElapsedTime(&ms, start_, stop_) >> CUDA_CHECK;
70	        return ms;
71	    }
72	
73	private:
74	    cudaEvent_t start_{};
75	    cudaEvent_t stop_{};
76	};
77	
78	// CSV writer: appends rows to a file in the output directory, and also
79	// echoes every row (plus the header) to stdout for live visibility.
80	//
81	// If the target file is new or empty when constructed, the header line
82	// is written to the file first; if not, the file is appended to without
83	// a fresh header (so a sweep over many invocations builds one CSV).
84	class CSVWriter {
85	public:
86	    CSVWriter(const std::filesystem::path& path, std::string header)
87	        : header_(std::move(header))
88	    {
89	        if (!path.parent_path().empty()) {
90	            std::filesystem::create_directories(path.parent_path());
91	        }
92	        const bool needs_header = !std::filesystem::exists(path)
93	                                || std::filesystem::file_size(path) == 0;
94	        file_.open(path, std::ios::app);
95	        if (!file_) {
96	            std::fprintf(stderr, "Failed to open %s for writing\n",
97	                         path.string().c_str());
98	            std::exit(1);
99	        }
100	        if (needs_header) {
101	            file_ << header_ << "\n";
102	            file_.flush();
103	        }
104	        // Echo header once per invocation, regardless of file state, so
105	        // stdout is self-documenting even when appending to an existing
106	        // CSV from the sweep driver.
107	        std::cout << header_ << "\n";
108	    }
109	
110	    void write_row(const std::string& row) {
111	        file_ << row << "\n";
112	        file_.flush();
113	        std::cout << row << "\n";
114	        std::cout.flush();
115	    }
116	
117	private:
118	    std::string   header_;
119	    std::ofstream file_;
120	};
121	
122	// Fill out[0..n) with fmix32(seed + i) mod range. For `range` a power
123	// of two (the case here, since key range = α × capacity with capacity
124	// a power of two), the modulus yields a uniform distribution.
125	//
126	// Not marked inline: __global__ functions cannot be inline, but each
127	// benchmark binary is a single translation unit that includes this
128	// header exactly once, so there is no multiple-definition concern.
129	__global__ void fill_uniform_keys(
130	    std::uint32_t* out, std::size_t n,
131	    std::uint32_t seed, std::uint32_t range)
132	{
133	    const std::size_t tid = blockIdx.x * std::size_t{blockDim.x} + threadIdx.x;
134	    if (tid >= n) return;
135	    out[tid] = gpurhh::detail::fmix32(static_cast<std::uint32_t>(tid) + seed) % range;
136	}
137	
```

</details>

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:14:18.125Z

Adding two shared helpers to `benchmarks.cuh`: a `LaunchShape` struct + `compute_launch_shape` function, and a templated `run_benchmark_loop` for the warmup-and-time pattern.

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:14:32.233Z

**→ Tool call: `Edit`** (id `tool-541`)

<details><summary><code>tool input</code></summary>

```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "[repo]/benchmarks/benchmarks.cuh",
  "old_string": "__global__ void fill_uniform_keys(\n    std::uint32_t* out, std::size_t n,\n    std::uint32_t seed, std::uint32_t range)\n{\n    const std::size_t tid = blockIdx.x * std::size_t{blockDim.x} + threadIdx.x;\n    if (tid >= n) return;\n    out[tid] = gpurhh::detail::fmix32(static_cast<std::uint32_t>(tid) + seed) % range;\n}",
  "new_string": "__global__ void fill_uniform_keys(\n    std::uint32_t* out, std::size_t n,\n    std::uint32_t seed, std::uint32_t range)\n{\n    const std::size_t tid = blockIdx.x * std::size_t{blockDim.x} + threadIdx.x;\n    if (tid >= n) return;\n    out[tid] = gpurhh::detail::fmix32(static_cast<std::uint32_t>(tid) + seed) % range;\n}\n\n// Launch-shape sizing: queries the current device for SM count, derives a\n// grid size proportional to it (8 blocks per SM keeps SMs occupied without\n// inflating the grid past the point of useful concurrency), and returns\n// the per-block and total tile counts that the benchmark kernels need.\nstruct LaunchShape {\n    int         grid_size;\n    int         tiles_per_block;\n    std::size_t n_tiles;\n};\n\ninline LaunchShape compute_launch_shape(int block_size, int tile_size) {\n    int device  = 0;\n    int num_sms = 0;\n    cudaGetDevice(&device) >> CUDA_CHECK;\n    cudaDeviceGetAttribute(&num_sms, cudaDevAttrMultiProcessorCount, device)\n        >> CUDA_CHECK;\n\n    const int tiles_per_block = block_size / tile_size;\n    const int grid_size       = num_sms * 8;\n    const std::size_t n_tiles =\n        static_cast<std::size_t>(grid_size)\n      * static_cast<std::size_t>(tiles_per_block);\n    return {grid_size, tiles_per_block, n_tiles};\n}\n\n// Boilerplate runner for the warmup-then-timed-reps pattern shared by\n// every benchmark. The caller passes three callables:\n//\n//   - setup    — invoked before every iteration (both warmups and timed\n//                reps). Use it to zero per-rep counters or clear the\n//                table between insert reps. Defaults to a no-op if the\n//                benchmark has nothing to reset.\n//   - launch   — issues the kernel (or memcpy) being timed.\n//   - after    — invoked after each timed rep with the rep index and\n//                elapsed milliseconds. Use it to read back counters and\n//                emit a CSV row.\ntemplate <class SetupFn, class LaunchFn, class AfterFn>\ninline void run_benchmark_loop(\n    int warmups, int reps, EventTimer& timer,\n    SetupFn&& setup, LaunchFn&& launch, AfterFn&& after)\n{\n    for (int w = 0; w < warmups; ++w) {\n        setup();\n        launch();\n    }\n    cudaDeviceSynchronize() >> CUDA_CHECK;\n\n    for (int r = 0; r < reps; ++r) {\n        setup();\n        timer.begin();\n        launch();\n        const float ms = timer.end_ms();\n        after(r, ms);\n    }\n}"
}
```

</details>

```
279	    cudaMemcpy(h_prefill_failures.data(), d_prefill_failures,
280	               n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t),
281	               cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost) >> CUDA_CHECK;
282	    const std::uint64_t prefill_failures = std::accumulate(
283	        h_prefill_failures.begin(), h_prefill_failures.end(), std::uint64_t{0});
284	
285	    if (prefill_failures > 0) {
286	        // Not an error. At high load factor, Robin Hood displacement
287	        // chains can exhaust the probe cap before placing every input;
288	        // View::insert hands the leftover back, the benchmark records
289	        // the count. The table is still valid — it just contains
290	        // marginally fewer distinct keys than the input asked for.
291	        // The `hits` / `misses` CSV columns reflect the actual table
292	        // state, and `prefill_failures` records how far that deviates
293	        // from the intended workload.
294	        std::fprintf(stderr,
295	            "[get] pre-fill recorded %llu insert failures at F=%.3f "
296	            "(actual occupancy is slightly below the theoretical "
297	            "1 - exp(-F)). Proceeding; see prefill_failures column.\n",
298	            static_cast<unsigned long long>(prefill_failures), args.load_factor);
299	    }
300	
301	    cudaFree(d_insert_keys);
302	    cudaFree(d_prefill_failures);
303	
304	    // --- get key buffer (independent stream) ---
305	    std::uint32_t* d_get_keys = nullptr;
306	    cudaMalloc(&d_get_keys, n_ops * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
307	    {
308	        constexpr int fill_block = 256;
309	        const int fill_grid =
310	            static_cast<int>((n_ops + fill_block - 1) / fill_block);
311	        fill_uniform_keys<<<fill_grid, fill_block>>>(
312	            d_get_keys, n_ops, args.get_seed, key_range);
313	        cudaGetLastError() >> CUDA_CHECK;
314	    }
315	
316	    // --- output + per-tile counter arrays ---
317	    std::uint32_t* d_out = nullptr;
318	    cudaMalloc(&d_out, n_ops * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
319	
320	    std::uint32_t* d_probes = nullptr;
321	    std::uint32_t* d_hits   = nullptr;
322	    cudaMalloc(&d_probes, n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
323	    cudaMalloc(&d_hits,   n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
324	    std::vector<std::uint32_t> h_probes(n_tiles);
325	    std::vector<std::uint32_t> h_hits  (n_tiles);
326	
327	    CSVWriter csv(args.output_dir / "get.csv",
328	        "library,workload,slot_bytes,capacity,capacity_bytes,"
329	        "key_range,alpha,load_factor,block_size,n_ops,bytes_per_op,"
330	        "insert_seed,get_seed,rep,tag,time_ms,ops_per_sec,gbps_floor,gbps_corrected,"
331	        "total_probes,total_hits,total_misses,prefill_failures");
332	
333	    EventTimer timer;
334	
335	    auto reset_counters = [&]() {
336	        cudaMemset(d_probes, 0, n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
337	        cudaMemset(d_hits,   0, n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
338	    };
339	    auto launch = [&]() {
340	        get<<<grid_size, args.block_size>>>(
341	            table.view(), d_get_keys, d_out, n_ops, d_probes, d_hits);
342	        cudaGetLastError() >> CUDA_CHECK;
343	    };
344	
345	    // --- warmups ---
346	    for (int w = 0; w < args.warmups; ++w) {
347	        reset_counters();
348	        launch();
349	    }
350	    cudaDeviceSynchronize() >> CUDA_CHECK;
351	
352	    // --- timed reps ---
353	    for (int r = 0; r < args.reps; ++r) {
354	        reset_counters();
355	        timer.begin();
356	        launch();
357	        const float ms = timer.end_ms();
358	
359	        cudaMemcpy(h_probes.data(), d_probes,
```

```
#pragma once

#include <cuco/detail/pair/helpers.cuh>
#include <cuco/detail/pair/traits.hpp>

#include <cuda/std/tuple>
```

## User — 2026-05-19T01:26:51.791Z

<details><summary><code>tool result</code></summary>

```
struct alignas(detail::pair_alignment<First, Second>()) pair {
  using first_type  = First;   ///< Type of the first value in the pair
  using second_type = Second;  ///< Type of the second value in the pair

  pair()            = default;
  ~pair()           = default;
  pair(pair const&) = default;  ///< Copy constructor
  pair(pair&&)      = default;  ///< Move constructor

  /**
   * @brief Replaces the contents of the pair with another pair.
   *
   * @return Reference of the current pair object
   */
  pair& operator=(pair const&) = default;

  /**
   * @brief Replaces the contents of the pair with another pair.
   *
   * @return Reference of the current pair object
   */
  pair& operator=(pair&&) = default;

  /**
   * @brief Constructs a pair from objects `f` and `s`.
   *
   * @param f The object to copy into `first`
   * @param s The object to copy into `second`
   */
  __host__ __device__ constexpr pair(First const& f, Second const& s);

  /**
   * @brief Constructs a pair by copying from the given pair `p`.
   *
   * @tparam F Type of the first value of `p`
   * @tparam S Type of the second value of `p`
   *
   * @param p The pair to copy from
   */
  template <typename F, typename S>
  __host__ __device__ constexpr pair(pair<F, S> const& p);
```

</details>

```json
{
  "file_path": "[repo]/benchmarks/baselines/cuco/benchmark_insert.cu",
  "content": "// Insert benchmark against cuCollections's `static_map`.\n//\n// Mirrors benchmarks/benchmark_insert.cu so the two libraries can share\n// the same CSV file (`output/.../insert.csv`) and overlay cleanly on a\n// plot. cuCollections's API differs in detail — `insert_async` takes a\n// pair iterator rather than separate key/value buffers, and there's no\n// per-probe instrumentation hook to feed `gbps_corrected` or per-tile\n// counters — so a few CSV columns are written empty for cuco rows.\n\n#include \"../../benchmarks.cuh\"\n\n#include <cuco/static_map.cuh>\n\n#include <thrust/iterator/transform_iterator.h>\n\n#include <cuda_runtime.h>\n\n#include <cstddef>\n#include <cstdint>\n#include <cstdio>\n#include <cstdlib>\n#include <filesystem>\n#include <sstream>\n#include <string>\n\nusing Key   = std::uint32_t;\nusing Value = std::uint32_t;\n\n// Sentinels for cuco::static_map. Match gpurhh's empty-key convention\n// (all-bits-set) for a fair comparison.\nconstexpr Key   EMPTY_KEY   = ~Key{};\nconstexpr Value EMPTY_VALUE = ~Value{};\n\nnamespace {\n\nstruct Args {\n    std::size_t   capacity_bytes = std::size_t{1} << 30;\n    double        load_factor    = 1.0;\n    int           block_size     = 256;  // unused by cuco's bulk API; kept for CSV uniformity\n    int           warmups        = 2;\n    int           reps           = 16;\n    std::uint32_t seed           = 42;\n    std::string   tag            = \"\";\n    std::filesystem::path output_dir;\n};\n\n[[noreturn]] void die_usage(const char* prog) {\n    std::fprintf(stderr,\n        \"usage: %s --output-dir DIR [options]\\n\"\n        \"  Same shape as benchmarks/benchmark_insert; see that for the\\n\"\n        \"  full flag list. cuco's bulk insert_async takes the key\\n\"\n        \"  buffer, derives values via fmix32, and uses the entire pair\\n\"\n        \"  iterator at once — block_size is recorded for CSV uniformity\\n\"\n        \"  but does not affect cuco's internal launch.\\n\",\n        prog);\n    std::exit(1);\n}\n\nArgs parse_args(int argc, char** argv) {\n    Args a;\n    for (int i = 1; i < argc; ++i) {\n        std::string flag = argv[i];\n        auto get_val = [&]() -> std::string {\n            if (i + 1 >= argc) { std::fprintf(stderr, \"Missing value for %s\\n\", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }\n            return argv[++i];\n        };\n        if      (flag == \"--capacity-bytes\") a.capacity_bytes = std::stoull(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--load-factor\")    a.load_factor    = std::stod(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--block-size\")     a.block_size     = std::stoi(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--warmups\")        a.warmups        = std::stoi(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--reps\")           a.reps           = std::stoi(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--seed\")           a.seed           = static_cast<std::uint32_t>(std::stoul(get_val()));\n        else if (flag == \"--tag\")            a.tag            = get_val();\n        else if (flag == \"--output-dir\")     a.output_dir     = get_val();\n        else { std::fprintf(stderr, \"Unknown flag: %s\\n\", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }\n    }\n    if (a.output_dir.empty())            die_usage(argv[0]);\n    if (a.load_factor <= 0.0)            { std::fprintf(stderr, \"--load-factor must be > 0\\n\"); std::exit(1); }\n    if (a.load_factor > 0.95)            { std::fprintf(stderr, \"[cuco] --load-factor > 0.95 may overflow static_map capacity; capping is the caller's job\\n\"); }\n    return a;\n}\n\n// Match the CSV header of benchmarks/benchmark_insert.cu exactly.\n// Columns cuco can't fill (gbps_corrected, total_probes, total_failures)\n// are written as empty cells so a single insert.csv holds rows from\n// both libraries side by side.\nstd::string format_row(\n    std::size_t slot_bytes, std::size_t capacity, std::size_t capacity_bytes,\n    std::size_t key_range, double alpha, double load_factor,\n    int block_size, std::size_t n_ops, std::size_t bytes_per_op,\n    std::uint32_t seed, int rep, const std::string& tag,\n    float time_ms, double ops_per_sec, double gbps_floor)\n{\n    std::ostringstream s;\n    s.precision(9);\n    s << \"cuco,insert,\"\n      << slot_bytes      << \",\"\n      << capacity        << \",\"\n      << capacity_bytes  << \",\"\n      << key_range       << \",\"\n      << alpha           << \",\"\n      << load_factor     << \",\"\n      << block_size      << \",\"\n      << n_ops           << \",\"\n      << bytes_per_op    << \",\"\n      << seed            << \",\"\n      << rep             << \",\"\n      << tag             << \",\"\n      << time_ms         << \",\"\n      << ops_per_sec     << \",\"\n      << gbps_floor      << \",\"\n      << \",\"   // gbps_corrected (cuco has no probe instrumentation)\n      << \",\"   // total_probes\n      << \"\";   // total_failures\n    return s.str();\n}\n\n} // namespace\n\nint main(int argc, char** argv) {\n    const Args args = parse_args(argc, argv);\n\n    // Slot bytes match cuco::pair<uint32, uint32>'s footprint. bytes_per_op\n    // stays at 128 (one cache line) for direct comparison with gpurhh's\n    // floor estimate.\n    constexpr std::size_t slot_bytes   = sizeof(Key) + sizeof(Value);\n    constexpr std::size_t bytes_per_op = 128;\n    const std::size_t capacity       = args.capacity_bytes / slot_bytes;\n    const std::size_t capacity_bytes = capacity * slot_bytes;\n    const Key key_range = static_cast<Key>(capacity);\n    const std::size_t n_ops =\n        static_cast<std::size_t>(args.load_factor * static_cast<double>(capacity));\n\n    if (n_ops == 0) {\n        std::fprintf(stderr, \"Computed n_ops = 0; raise --load-factor or --capacity-bytes\\n\");\n        std::exit(1);\n    }\n\n    std::fprintf(stderr,\n        \"[cuco insert] capacity=%zu slots (%zu B), F=%.3f, n_ops=%zu, \"\n        \"warmups=%d, reps=%d, seed=%u, tag=\\\"%s\\\"\\n\",\n        capacity, capacity_bytes, args.load_factor, n_ops,\n        args.warmups, args.reps, args.seed, args.tag.c_str());\n\n    // --- input buffer ---\n    Key* d_keys = nullptr;\n    cudaMalloc(&d_keys, n_ops * sizeof(Key)) >> CUDA_CHECK;\n    {\n        constexpr int fill_block = 256;\n        const int fill_grid =\n            static_cast<int>((n_ops + fill_block - 1) / fill_block);\n        fill_uniform_keys<<<fill_grid, fill_block>>>(\n            d_keys, n_ops, args.seed, key_range);\n        cudaGetLastError() >> CUDA_CHECK;\n    }\n\n    // Transform iterator: produces cuco::pair<Key, Value> from each key\n    // on the fly, with value = fmix32(key). Matches the gpurhh\n    // benchmark's \"derive value from key\" choice; no separate values\n    // buffer needed.\n    auto pair_begin = thrust::make_transform_iterator(\n        d_keys,\n        [] __device__ (Key k) {\n            return cuco::pair<Key, Value>{k, gpurhh::detail::fmix32(k)};\n        });\n\n    // Construct the map. capacity is in slots (cuco rounds up internally\n    // for whatever per-bucket layout it picks).\n    cuco::static_map<Key, Value> map{\n        capacity,\n        cuco::empty_key<Key>{EMPTY_KEY},\n        cuco::empty_value<Value>{EMPTY_VALUE}};\n\n    CSVWriter csv(args.output_dir / \"insert.csv\",\n        \"library,workload,slot_bytes,capacity,capacity_bytes,\"\n        \"key_range,alpha,load_factor,block_size,n_ops,bytes_per_op,\"\n        \"seed,rep,tag,time_ms,ops_per_sec,gbps_floor,gbps_corrected,\"\n        \"total_probes,total_failures\");\n\n    EventTimer timer;\n\n    run_benchmark_loop(args.warmups, args.reps, timer,\n        /*setup=*/  [&]() {\n            map.clear_async();\n        },\n        /*launch=*/ [&]() {\n            map.insert_async(pair_begin, pair_begin + n_ops);\n        },\n        /*after=*/  [&](int rep, float ms) {\n            const double ops_per_sec =\n                static_cast<double>(n_ops) / (static_cast<double>(ms) * 1.0e-3);\n            const double gbps_floor =\n                static_cast<double>(n_ops) * static_cast<double>(bytes_per_op)\n                    / (static_cast<double>(ms) * 1.0e6);\n            csv.write_row(format_row(\n                slot_bytes, capacity, capacity_bytes,\n                std::size_t{key_range}, /*alpha=*/1.0, args.load_factor,\n                args.block_size, n_ops, bytes_per_op,\n                args.seed, rep, args.tag,\n                ms, ops_per_sec, gbps_floor));\n        });\n\n    cudaFree(d_keys);\n    return 0;\n}\n"
}
```

</details>

```json
{
  "file_path": "[repo]/benchmarks/baselines/cuco/benchmark_get.cu",
  "content": "// Get benchmark against cuCollections's `static_map`.\n//\n// Mirrors benchmarks/benchmark_get.cu so the two libraries share one\n// get.csv. cuCollections's `find_async` returns the value (or the\n// empty-value sentinel on miss) for each key in a flat output buffer,\n// which closely matches the shape of our own get kernel's output.\n// As with the insert baseline, cuco-only-empty columns (gbps_corrected,\n// total_probes) are left blank for cuco rows.\n\n#include \"../../benchmarks.cuh\"\n\n#include <cuco/static_map.cuh>\n\n#include <thrust/iterator/transform_iterator.h>\n\n#include <cuda_runtime.h>\n\n#include <cstddef>\n#include <cstdint>\n#include <cstdio>\n#include <cstdlib>\n#include <filesystem>\n#include <sstream>\n#include <string>\n#include <vector>\n\nusing Key   = std::uint32_t;\nusing Value = std::uint32_t;\n\nconstexpr Key   EMPTY_KEY   = ~Key{};\nconstexpr Value EMPTY_VALUE = ~Value{};\n\nnamespace {\n\nstruct Args {\n    std::size_t   capacity_bytes = std::size_t{1} << 30;\n    double        load_factor    = 1.0;\n    int           block_size     = 256;  // unused by cuco's bulk API; kept for CSV uniformity\n    int           warmups        = 2;\n    int           reps           = 16;\n    std::uint32_t insert_seed    = 42;\n    std::uint32_t get_seed       = 1729;\n    std::string   tag            = \"\";\n    std::filesystem::path output_dir;\n};\n\n[[noreturn]] void die_usage(const char* prog) {\n    std::fprintf(stderr,\n        \"usage: %s --output-dir DIR [options]\\n\"\n        \"  Same shape as benchmarks/benchmark_get; see that for the full flag list.\\n\",\n        prog);\n    std::exit(1);\n}\n\nArgs parse_args(int argc, char** argv) {\n    Args a;\n    for (int i = 1; i < argc; ++i) {\n        std::string flag = argv[i];\n        auto get_val = [&]() -> std::string {\n            if (i + 1 >= argc) { std::fprintf(stderr, \"Missing value for %s\\n\", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }\n            return argv[++i];\n        };\n        if      (flag == \"--capacity-bytes\") a.capacity_bytes = std::stoull(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--load-factor\")    a.load_factor    = std::stod(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--block-size\")     a.block_size     = std::stoi(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--warmups\")        a.warmups        = std::stoi(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--reps\")           a.reps           = std::stoi(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--insert-seed\")    a.insert_seed    = static_cast<std::uint32_t>(std::stoul(get_val()));\n        else if (flag == \"--get-seed\")       a.get_seed       = static_cast<std::uint32_t>(std::stoul(get_val()));\n        else if (flag == \"--tag\")            a.tag            = get_val();\n        else if (flag == \"--output-dir\")     a.output_dir     = get_val();\n        else { std::fprintf(stderr, \"Unknown flag: %s\\n\", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }\n    }\n    if (a.output_dir.empty()) die_usage(argv[0]);\n    if (a.load_factor <= 0.0) { std::fprintf(stderr, \"--load-factor must be > 0\\n\"); std::exit(1); }\n    return a;\n}\n\n// Match the CSV header of benchmarks/benchmark_get.cu. Columns cuco\n// can't fill (gbps_corrected, total_probes, prefill_failures) are\n// written as empty cells so one get.csv holds both libraries' rows.\nstd::string format_row(\n    std::size_t slot_bytes, std::size_t capacity, std::size_t capacity_bytes,\n    std::size_t key_range, double alpha, double load_factor,\n    int block_size, std::size_t n_ops, std::size_t bytes_per_op,\n    std::uint32_t insert_seed, std::uint32_t get_seed,\n    int rep, const std::string& tag,\n    float time_ms, double ops_per_sec, double gbps_floor,\n    std::uint64_t total_hits, std::uint64_t total_misses)\n{\n    std::ostringstream s;\n    s.precision(9);\n    s << \"cuco,get,\"\n      << slot_bytes      << \",\"\n      << capacity        << \",\"\n      << capacity_bytes  << \",\"\n      << key_range       << \",\"\n      << alpha           << \",\"\n      << load_factor     << \",\"\n      << block_size      << \",\"\n      << n_ops           << \",\"\n      << bytes_per_op    << \",\"\n      << insert_seed     << \",\"\n      << get_seed        << \",\"\n      << rep             << \",\"\n      << tag             << \",\"\n      << time_ms         << \",\"\n      << ops_per_sec     << \",\"\n      << gbps_floor      << \",\"\n      << \",\"             // gbps_corrected\n      << \",\"             // total_probes\n      << total_hits      << \",\"\n      << total_misses    << \",\"\n      << \"\";             // prefill_failures (cuco can't report this directly)\n    return s.str();\n}\n\n} // namespace\n\nint main(int argc, char** argv) {\n    const Args args = parse_args(argc, argv);\n\n    constexpr std::size_t slot_bytes   = sizeof(Key) + sizeof(Value);\n    constexpr std::size_t bytes_per_op = 128;\n    const std::size_t capacity       = args.capacity_bytes / slot_bytes;\n    const std::size_t capacity_bytes = capacity * slot_bytes;\n    const Key key_range = static_cast<Key>(capacity);\n    const std::size_t n_ops =\n        static_cast<std::size_t>(args.load_factor * static_cast<double>(capacity));\n\n    if (n_ops == 0) {\n        std::fprintf(stderr, \"Computed n_ops = 0; raise --load-factor or --capacity-bytes\\n\");\n        std::exit(1);\n    }\n\n    std::fprintf(stderr,\n        \"[cuco get] capacity=%zu slots (%zu B), F=%.3f, n_ops=%zu, \"\n        \"warmups=%d, reps=%d, insert_seed=%u, get_seed=%u, tag=\\\"%s\\\"\\n\",\n        capacity, capacity_bytes, args.load_factor, n_ops,\n        args.warmups, args.reps, args.insert_seed, args.get_seed, args.tag.c_str());\n\n    // --- pre-fill (untimed) ---\n    Key* d_insert_keys = nullptr;\n    cudaMalloc(&d_insert_keys, n_ops * sizeof(Key)) >> CUDA_CHECK;\n    {\n        constexpr int fill_block = 256;\n        const int fill_grid =\n            static_cast<int>((n_ops + fill_block - 1) / fill_block);\n        fill_uniform_keys<<<fill_grid, fill_block>>>(\n            d_insert_keys, n_ops, args.insert_seed, key_range);\n        cudaGetLastError() >> CUDA_CHECK;\n    }\n\n    auto insert_pair_begin = thrust::make_transform_iterator(\n        d_insert_keys,\n        [] __device__ (Key k) {\n            return cuco::pair<Key, Value>{k, gpurhh::detail::fmix32(k)};\n        });\n\n    cuco::static_map<Key, Value> map{\n        capacity,\n        cuco::empty_key<Key>{EMPTY_KEY},\n        cuco::empty_value<Value>{EMPTY_VALUE}};\n\n    map.insert_async(insert_pair_begin, insert_pair_begin + n_ops);\n    cudaDeviceSynchronize() >> CUDA_CHECK;\n    cudaFree(d_insert_keys);\n\n    // --- get key buffer (independent stream) ---\n    Key* d_get_keys = nullptr;\n    cudaMalloc(&d_get_keys, n_ops * sizeof(Key)) >> CUDA_CHECK;\n    {\n        constexpr int fill_block = 256;\n        const int fill_grid =\n            static_cast<int>((n_ops + fill_block - 1) / fill_block);\n        fill_uniform_keys<<<fill_grid, fill_block>>>(\n            d_get_keys, n_ops, args.get_seed, key_range);\n        cudaGetLastError() >> CUDA_CHECK;\n    }\n\n    // Output buffer: one Value slot per get op. cuco writes the looked-up\n    // value, or EMPTY_VALUE on miss.\n    Value* d_out = nullptr;\n    cudaMalloc(&d_out, n_ops * sizeof(Value)) >> CUDA_CHECK;\n\n    CSVWriter csv(args.output_dir / \"get.csv\",\n        \"library,workload,slot_bytes,capacity,capacity_bytes,\"\n        \"key_range,alpha,load_factor,block_size,n_ops,bytes_per_op,\"\n        \"insert_seed,get_seed,rep,tag,time_ms,ops_per_sec,gbps_floor,gbps_corrected,\"\n        \"total_probes,total_hits,total_misses,prefill_failures\");\n\n    EventTimer timer;\n\n    // Hits/misses are counted on the host by reading back the output\n    // buffer once after the first timed rep (the result is the same\n    // every rep — table state doesn't change between reps).\n    std::vector<Value> h_out(n_ops);\n    std::uint64_t cached_hits   = 0;\n    std::uint64_t cached_misses = 0;\n    bool counted = false;\n\n    run_benchmark_loop(args.warmups, args.reps, timer,\n        /*setup=*/  []() {},   // table state unchanged between reps\n        /*launch=*/ [&]() {\n            map.find_async(d_get_keys, d_get_keys + n_ops, d_out);\n        },\n        /*after=*/  [&](int rep, float ms) {\n            if (!counted) {\n                cudaMemcpy(h_out.data(), d_out, n_ops * sizeof(Value),\n                           cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost) >> CUDA_CHECK;\n                for (std::size_t i = 0; i < n_ops; ++i) {\n                    if (h_out[i] == EMPTY_VALUE) ++cached_misses;\n                    else                          ++cached_hits;\n                }\n                counted = true;\n            }\n            const double ops_per_sec =\n                static_cast<double>(n_ops) / (static_cast<double>(ms) * 1.0e-3);\n            const double gbps_floor =\n                static_cast<double>(n_ops) * static_cast<double>(bytes_per_op)\n                    / (static_cast<double>(ms) * 1.0e6);\n            csv.write_row(format_row(\n                slot_bytes, capacity, capacity_bytes,\n                std::size_t{key_range}, /*alpha=*/1.0, args.load_factor,\n                args.block_size, n_ops, bytes_per_op,\n                args.insert_seed, args.get_seed, rep, args.tag,\n                ms, ops_per_sec, gbps_floor,\n                cached_hits, cached_misses));\n        });\n\n    cudaFree(d_get_keys);\n    cudaFree(d_out);\n    return 0;\n}\n"
}
```

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:30:43.689Z

All four steps complete. Recap:

**Step 3 — refactored `benchmarks/benchmarks.cuh`:**
- Added `LaunchShape` struct + `compute_launch_shape(block_size, tile_size)` that handles `cudaGetDevice` + `cudaDeviceGetAttribute` and returns grid_size / tiles_per_block / n_tiles.
- Added `run_benchmark_loop(warmups, reps, timer, setup, launch, after)` template that encapsulates the warmup-then-timed-rep pattern shared across binaries.

**Step 4 — migrated the three gpurhh binaries** to use both helpers. Each `main()` lost ~20 lines of boilerplate; the per-rep loop body now reads as exactly what's library-specific.

**Step 5 — wrote the cuco baselines:**
- [benchmarks/baselines/cuco/benchmark_insert.cu](benchmarks/baselines/cuco/benchmark_insert.cu) — uses `cuco::static_map<uint32_t, uint32_t>::insert_async` with a `thrust::transform_iterator` that derives `cuco::pair{key, fmix32(key)}` on the fly. Same CSV header as gpurhh's `insert.csv`; `gbps_corrected`, `total_probes`, `total_failures` cells written empty since cuco doesn't expose probe instrumentation.
- [benchmarks/baselines/cuco/benchmark_get.cu](benchmarks/baselines/cuco/benchmark_get.cu) — pre-fills once via `insert_async`, then `find_async` writes lookups to a Value output buffer. `hits` / `misses` counted host-side from the output buffer once (table state doesn't change across reps).

**Step 6 — updated `scripts/benchmark.sh`:**
- Detects whether the cuco baseline binaries built (via `[[ -x ... ]]`); skips the section cleanly if not.
- Cuco F grid restricted to `{0.5, 0.7, 0.85, 0.95}` since `static_map` is fixed-capacity. No block_size sweep (cuco's bulk API has its own internal launch).

**Makefile housekeeping:**
- Baseline pattern rule now passes `--extended-lambda --expt-relaxed-constexpr`, which cuCollections's public API needs.

When you rerun the original sweep, output CSVs should match the previous numbers exactly (only the launch-shape / loop-skeleton code moved; the per-rep body is byte-identical to before). Then with `external/include/cuco/` populated by the setup script, `make benchmarks` builds the cuco binaries and the sweep picks them up automatically.

```json
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n#\n# Run benchmark sweeps against the external baseline libraries that\n# scripts/setup-baselines.sh has populated under external/include/.\n#\n# Same output-dir convention as scripts/benchmark.sh: defaults to a\n# fresh output/<timestamp>/ under the repo root, or whatever path is\n# passed as the first positional arg. Pass the timestamped dir of an\n# existing gpurhh run if you want the baseline CSV rows to land in the\n# same insert.csv / get.csv as the gpurhh rows for overlaid plotting.\n#\n# Each baseline library is run only if its binaries built — controlled\n# at make time by whether external/include/<namespace>/ exists. If\n# neither built, the script does nothing (and says so).\n\nset -euo pipefail\n\nREPO_ROOT=\"$(cd \"$(dirname \"${BASH_SOURCE[0]}\")/..\" && pwd)\"\nBUILD_DIR=\"${REPO_ROOT}/build/benchmarks\"\n\n# Output dir: optional positional arg, else a fresh timestamped dir.\nif [[ $# -ge 1 ]]; then\n    OUTPUT_DIR=\"$1\"\nelse\n    OUTPUT_DIR=\"${REPO_ROOT}/output/$(date +%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S)\"\nfi\nmkdir -p \"${OUTPUT_DIR}\"\n\nLOG_FILE=\"${OUTPUT_DIR}/benchmark-baselines.log\"\nexec > >(tee -a \"${LOG_FILE}\") 2>&1\n\necho \"==> Writing CSV output and run_info to ${OUTPUT_DIR}\"\n\n# Environment sidecar (overwrites any from scripts/benchmark.sh — same\n# machine, so the values are identical).\n{\n    echo \"date:        $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)\"\n    echo \"uname:       $(uname -srm)\"\n    echo \"nvcc:        $(nvcc --version 2>/dev/null | tail -n1 || echo 'not found')\"\n    echo \"nvidia-smi:  $(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name,driver_version,memory.total,power.limit --format=csv,noheader 2>/dev/null || echo 'not found')\"\n} > \"${OUTPUT_DIR}/run_info.txt\"\n\n# Shared workload knobs. Capacity matches scripts/benchmark.sh exactly\n# so baseline rows land in the same regime.\nCAPACITY_BYTES=$((1 << 30))\nTAG=\"sweep\"\n\nran_any=0\n\n# --- cuCollections -----------------------------------------------------\n#\n# cuco::static_map has a fixed capacity and can't sustain load factor\n# above 1.0, so its F sweep stops short of the gpurhh range. cuco's\n# bulk API picks its own launch configuration; block_size is not swept.\nCUCO_DIR=\"${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/cuco\"\nif [[ -x \"${CUCO_DIR}/benchmark_insert\" && -x \"${CUCO_DIR}/benchmark_get\" ]]; then\n    CUCO_LOAD_FACTORS=(0.5 0.7 0.85 0.95)\n    for f in \"${CUCO_LOAD_FACTORS[@]}\"; do\n        echo \"==> cuco insert F=${f}\"\n        \"${CUCO_DIR}/benchmark_insert\" \\\n            --output-dir \"${OUTPUT_DIR}\" \\\n            --capacity-bytes \"${CAPACITY_BYTES}\" \\\n            --load-factor \"${f}\" \\\n            --tag \"${TAG}\"\n\n        echo \"==> cuco get    F=${f}\"\n        \"${CUCO_DIR}/benchmark_get\" \\\n            --output-dir \"${OUTPUT_DIR}\" \\\n            --capacity-bytes \"${CAPACITY_BYTES}\" \\\n            --load-factor \"${f}\" \\\n            --tag \"${TAG}\"\n    done\n    ran_any=1\nfi\n\n# --- WarpCore (placeholder) -------------------------------------------\n#\n# WARPCORE_DIR=\"${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/warpcore\"\n# if [[ -x \"${WARPCORE_DIR}/benchmark_insert\" && -x \"${WARPCORE_DIR}/benchmark_get\" ]]; then\n#     ... (to be added once the warpcore binaries are written)\n#     ran_any=1\n# fi\n\nif [[ \"${ran_any}\" -eq 0 ]]; then\n    echo \"No baseline binaries found under ${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/.\" >&2\n    echo \"Run scripts/setup-baselines.sh to populate external/include/, then 'make benchmarks'.\" >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\necho \"==> Done. Results in ${OUTPUT_DIR}\"\n"
}
```

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:32:21.978Z

Done. Layout now:

- [scripts/benchmark.sh](scripts/benchmark.sh) — gpurhh sweep only.
- [scripts/benchmark-baselines.sh](scripts/benchmark-baselines.sh) — baseline sweep (cuco now, warpcore placeholder commented in for when we write that wrapper). Prints a clear error if no baseline binaries are present.
- `make benchmark` → builds + runs the gpurhh script.
- `make benchmark-baselines` → builds + runs the baseline script.

Both scripts default to a fresh `output/<timestamp>/` dir. If you want the baseline rows folded into an existing gpurhh run's CSVs (for plot overlays), pass that run's dir as the first arg:

```sh
bash scripts/benchmark-baselines.sh output/2026-05-18_02-27-54/
```

Baseline rows append to `insert.csv` / `get.csv` in that dir, distinguished from gpurhh rows by the `library` column.

Could the two compiler flags be useful to clean up or otherwise simplify our own code?

---

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:33:54.117Z

<em>Thinking…</em>

---

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:34:05.764Z

Honest answer: not really, for the existing code.

**`--extended-lambda`** allows `[] __device__ (...) { ... }` lambdas — useful when you want a lambda marked `__device__` from inside host code. Our gpurhh kernels are all named `__global__` / `__device__` functions, and the lambdas we do use (`try_cas` inside `View::insert`) live inside `__device__` functions and inherit device semantics naturally. There's no current pattern in our code that gets cleaner by adding it. The one place it might earn its keep is if we ever wanted to wrap thrust algorithms (e.g. `thrust::transform` with an inline `[] __device__` reducer) — but we don't, and the kernels we have are clearer named than inlined.

**`--expt-relaxed-constexpr`** lets `__device__` code call host-only `constexpr` functions — primarily things in `<bit>`, `<algorithm>`, etc. that libstdc++ marks `constexpr` without `__host__ __device__`. We hit this once early on with `std::bit_cast`, which is host-only `constexpr` in libstdc++, and resolved it by switching to `cuda::std::bit_cast` from libcu++. That's the right resolution: `cuda::std::*` is the explicitly-device-aware alternative, and using it avoids depending on a relaxation flag. Adding the flag globally would let us use plain `std::` versions instead, but at the cost of accepting an experimental nvcc flag in the build for a cosmetic win.

So I'd keep both flags scoped to the baselines pattern rule (where they're real requirements imposed by cuCollections), not promote them to the global `NVCC_FLAGS`. The gpurhh code stays clean of nvcc-specific relaxations.

If you ever did want to consolidate, the candidates would be:

- A `[] __device__ (...) { ... }` lambda passed to a thrust algorithm somewhere. Doesn't exist in our code today, and the per-binary kernel functions read better than inline lambdas would.
- Removing the `cuda::std::bit_cast` use in `default_hash` and going back to plain `std::bit_cast`. Same machine code, less explicit about device-vs-host intent — a small step backward in clarity for a small step forward in not needing libcu++.

Neither feels like a worthwhile change.

---

Thanks. How are we measuring memory bandwidth for cuco?

---

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:35:09.626Z

<em>Thinking…</em>

---

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:35:23.478Z

We don't, directly — for cuco rows we only compute `gbps_floor`, with the same `n_ops × 128 bytes / time` formula as gpurhh. The `gbps_corrected` column is left empty.

The reasoning chain:

1. **We can't measure cuco's actual DRAM traffic from outside its kernels.** Cuco doesn't expose a probe-counter hook (we'd have to patch cuco internals — invasive, brittle across version bumps, and not really what we want).
2. **`gbps_floor` is computed identically for both libraries**: ops per second × one cache line (128 B). For gpurhh this is the "1 probe per op = 1 cache line" lower bound on its actual DRAM traffic. For cuco it's the same kind of lower bound, by an only-slightly-different argument: cuco's `static_map` default uses linear probing on individual slots (not 16-slot buckets), but adjacent linear-probe slots share a cache line, so the hardware-level cache lines fetched per op end up being a similar quantity. Either way, it's "at least one cache line per op" — a floor.
3. **The comparison is rough but fair as a *floor*.** When both libraries hit close to the memcpy ceiling, the floor metric becomes increasingly tight (because there's no room for it to be a loose lower bound). When they're far below the ceiling, the floor is genuinely loose for both, and a side-by-side comparison says "both are doing at least X bandwidth, neither is saturating."

What we lose:

- **No `gbps_corrected` for cuco.** When the cuco column is "X GB/s gbps_floor, blank corrected," and gpurhh's is "X GB/s floor, Y GB/s corrected with Y > X," the natural temptation is to compare gpurhh's corrected to cuco's floor — which would be unfair. The honest comparison is **floor vs. floor**.
- **No probe-count signal for cuco.** At high load, cuco's linear probe is doing more cache line reads per op than its low-load behavior, and we have no visibility into that.

The honest workaround for a definitive comparison: **run Nsight Compute on a representative cuco kernel**, grab the actual `dram__bytes_read.sum` and `dram__bytes_write.sum` counters, divide by the kernel runtime. That gives the true bandwidth number, no instrumentation, no patching. Same trick we discussed for spot-checking gpurhh's floor. For the plotted comparison, the floor-vs-floor story is probably fine; for the headline claim "we hit X% of memcpy peak vs. cuco's Y%," I'd validate with ncu before trusting the numbers.

Worth a note in the design doc's Benchmarking section if we end up publishing the numbers — want me to add that caveat now?

---

```
     */
    HOSTQUALIFIER INLINEQUALIFIER
    explicit SingleValueHashTable(
        const index_type min_capacity,
        const key_type seed = defaults::seed<key_type>(),
        const bool no_init = false) noexcept :
        status_(nullptr),
        table_(detail::get_valid_capacity(min_capacity, cg_size())),
        temp_(TempMemoryBytes / sizeof(index_type)),
        seed_(seed),
        is_initialized_(false),
        is_copy_(false)
    {
        cudaMalloc(&status_, sizeof(status_type));

        assign_status(table_.status() + temp_.status());

        if(!no_init) init();
    }

    /*! \brief copy-constructor (shallow)
     *  \param[in] object to be copied
     */
    HOSTDEVICEQUALIFIER INLINEQUALIFIER
    SingleValueHashTable(const SingleValueHashTable& o) noexcept :
        status_(o.status_),
    void init(
        const key_type seed,
        const cudaStream_t stream = 0) noexcept
    {
        is_initialized_ = false;

        seed_ = seed;
        if(!table_.status().has_not_initialized() &&
            !temp_.status().has_not_initialized())
        {
            table_.init_keys(empty_key(), stream);

            assign_status(table_.status() + temp_.status(), stream);

            is_initialized_ = true;
        }
    }

    /*! \brief (re)initialize the hash table
     * \param[in] stream CUDA stream in which this operation is executed in
     */
    HOSTQUALIFIER INLINEQUALIFIER
    void init(const cudaStream_t stream = 0) noexcept
    {
        init(seed_, stream);
    }

    /*! \brief inserts a key into the hash table
     * \param[in] key_in key to insert into the hash table
     * \param[in] value_in value that corresponds to \c key_in
```

```
#ifndef WARPCORE_SINGLE_VALUE_HASH_TABLE_CUH
#define WARPCORE_SINGLE_VALUE_HASH_TABLE_CUH

#include "base.cuh"

namespace warpcore
{

// forward declaration of friends
template<
    class Key,
    class Value,
    Key EmptyKey,
    Key TombstoneKey,
    class ProbingScheme,
    class TableStorage,
    index_t TempMemoryBytes>
class CountingHashTable;

/*! \brief single-value hash table
 * \tparam Key key type (\c std::uint32_t or \c std::uint64_t)
 * \tparam Value value type
 * \tparam EmptyKey key which represents an empty slot
 * \tparam TombstoneKey key which represents an erased slot
 * \tparam ProbingScheme probing scheme from \c warpcore::probing_schemes
 * \tparam TableStorage memory layout from \c warpcore::storage::key_value
 * \tparam TempMemoryBytes size of temporary storage (typically a few kB)
 */
template<
    class Key,
    class Value,
    Key EmptyKey = defaults::empty_key<Key>(),
    Key TombstoneKey = defaults::tombstone_key<Key>(),
    class ProbingScheme = defaults::probing_scheme_t<Key, 8>,
    class TableStorage = defaults::table_storage_t<Key, Value>,
    index_t TempMemoryBytes = defaults::temp_memory_bytes()>
class SingleValueHashTable
{
    static_assert(
        checks::is_valid_key_type<Key>(),
        "invalid key type");

    static_assert(
        EmptyKey != TombstoneKey,
        "empty key and tombstone key must not be identical");

    static_assert(
        checks::is_probing_scheme<ProbingScheme>(),
        "not a valid probing scheme type");

    static_assert(
        std::is_same<typename ProbingScheme::key_type, Key>::value,
        "probing key type differs from table's key type");

    static_assert(
        checks::is_key_value_storage<TableStorage>(),
        "not a valid storage type");

    static_assert(
        std::is_same<typename TableStorage::key_type, Key>::value,
```

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:39:45.889Z

WarpCore's bulk insert/retrieve API takes `(keys, values, n, stream)` directly — closer to gpurhh's shape than cuco's. Need a small kernel to derive values from keys for parity.

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:40:12.896Z

<details><summary><code>tool input</code></summary>

```json
{
  "file_path": "[repo]/benchmarks/baselines/warpcore/benchmark_insert.cu",
  "content": "// Insert benchmark against WarpCore's `SingleValueHashTable`.\n//\n// Mirrors benchmarks/benchmark_insert.cu and the cuco baseline so all\n// three libraries share insert.csv. WarpCore's bulk insert takes\n// separate key + value buffers; we derive values via fmix32(key) into\n// a side buffer to match the gpurhh / cuco convention.\n\n#include \"../../benchmarks.cuh\"\n\n#include <warpcore/single_value_hash_table.cuh>\n\n#include <cuda_runtime.h>\n\n#include <cstddef>\n#include <cstdint>\n#include <cstdio>\n#include <cstdlib>\n#include <filesystem>\n#include <sstream>\n#include <string>\n\nusing Key   = std::uint32_t;\nusing Value = std::uint32_t;\nusing Table = warpcore::SingleValueHashTable<Key, Value>;\n\nnamespace {\n\nstruct Args {\n    std::size_t   capacity_bytes = std::size_t{1} << 30;\n    double        load_factor    = 1.0;\n    int           block_size     = 256;  // unused; kept for CSV uniformity\n    int           warmups        = 2;\n    int           reps           = 16;\n    std::uint32_t seed           = 42;\n    std::string   tag            = \"\";\n    std::filesystem::path output_dir;\n};\n\n[[noreturn]] void die_usage(const char* prog) {\n    std::fprintf(stderr,\n        \"usage: %s --output-dir DIR [options]\\n\"\n        \"  Same shape as benchmarks/benchmark_insert; see that for the\\n\"\n        \"  full flag list. WarpCore's bulk insert picks its own launch\\n\"\n        \"  configuration; block_size is recorded for CSV uniformity but\\n\"\n        \"  not used.\\n\",\n        prog);\n    std::exit(1);\n}\n\nArgs parse_args(int argc, char** argv) {\n    Args a;\n    for (int i = 1; i < argc; ++i) {\n        std::string flag = argv[i];\n        auto get_val = [&]() -> std::string {\n            if (i + 1 >= argc) { std::fprintf(stderr, \"Missing value for %s\\n\", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }\n            return argv[++i];\n        };\n        if      (flag == \"--capacity-bytes\") a.capacity_bytes = std::stoull(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--load-factor\")    a.load_factor    = std::stod(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--block-size\")     a.block_size     = std::stoi(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--warmups\")        a.warmups        = std::stoi(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--reps\")           a.reps           = std::stoi(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--seed\")           a.seed           = static_cast<std::uint32_t>(std::stoul(get_val()));\n        else if (flag == \"--tag\")            a.tag            = get_val();\n        else if (flag == \"--output-dir\")     a.output_dir     = get_val();\n        else { std::fprintf(stderr, \"Unknown flag: %s\\n\", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }\n    }\n    if (a.output_dir.empty()) die_usage(argv[0]);\n    if (a.load_factor <= 0.0) { std::fprintf(stderr, \"--load-factor must be > 0\\n\"); std::exit(1); }\n    return a;\n}\n\n// Match the CSV header of benchmarks/benchmark_insert.cu. Columns\n// warpcore can't fill (gbps_corrected, total_probes, total_failures)\n// are written empty.\nstd::string format_row(\n    std::size_t slot_bytes, std::size_t capacity, std::size_t capacity_bytes,\n    std::size_t key_range, double alpha, double load_factor,\n    int block_size, std::size_t n_ops, std::size_t bytes_per_op,\n    std::uint32_t seed, int rep, const std::string& tag,\n    float time_ms, double ops_per_sec, double gbps_floor)\n{\n    std::ostringstream s;\n    s.precision(9);\n    s << \"warpcore,insert,\"\n      << slot_bytes      << \",\"\n      << capacity        << \",\"\n      << capacity_bytes  << \",\"\n      << key_range       << \",\"\n      << alpha           << \",\"\n      << load_factor     << \",\"\n      << block_size      << \",\"\n      << n_ops           << \",\"\n      << bytes_per_op    << \",\"\n      << seed            << \",\"\n      << rep             << \",\"\n      << tag             << \",\"\n      << time_ms         << \",\"\n      << ops_per_sec     << \",\"\n      << gbps_floor      << \",\"\n      << \",\"   // gbps_corrected\n      << \",\"   // total_probes\n      << \"\";   // total_failures\n    return s.str();\n}\n\n// Derive values from keys: value = fmix32(key). Same convention used by\n// the cuco baseline and the gpurhh insert benchmark, so the per-key\n// bytes-of-state-flowing match across libraries.\n__global__ void derive_values(\n    const Key* __restrict__ keys, Value* __restrict__ values, std::size_t n)\n{\n    const std::size_t tid = blockIdx.x * std::size_t{blockDim.x} + threadIdx.x;\n    if (tid >= n) return;\n    values[tid] = gpurhh::detail::fmix32(keys[tid]);\n}\n\n} // namespace\n\nint main(int argc, char** argv) {\n    const Args args = parse_args(argc, argv);\n\n    constexpr std::size_t slot_bytes   = sizeof(Key) + sizeof(Value);\n    constexpr std::size_t bytes_per_op = 128;\n    const std::size_t capacity       = args.capacity_bytes / slot_bytes;\n    const std::size_t capacity_bytes = capacity * slot_bytes;\n    const Key key_range = static_cast<Key>(capacity);\n    const std::size_t n_ops =\n        static_cast<std::size_t>(args.load_factor * static_cast<double>(capacity));\n\n    if (n_ops == 0) {\n        std::fprintf(stderr, \"Computed n_ops = 0; raise --load-factor or --capacity-bytes\\n\");\n        std::exit(1);\n    }\n\n    std::fprintf(stderr,\n        \"[warpcore insert] capacity=%zu slots (%zu B), F=%.3f, n_ops=%zu, \"\n        \"warmups=%d, reps=%d, seed=%u, tag=\\\"%s\\\"\\n\",\n        capacity, capacity_bytes, args.load_factor, n_ops,\n        args.warmups, args.reps, args.seed, args.tag.c_str());\n\n    // --- input buffers ---\n    Key*   d_keys   = nullptr;\n    Value* d_values = nullptr;\n    cudaMalloc(&d_keys,   n_ops * sizeof(Key))   >> CUDA_CHECK;\n    cudaMalloc(&d_values, n_ops * sizeof(Value)) >> CUDA_CHECK;\n    {\n        constexpr int fill_block = 256;\n        const int fill_grid =\n            static_cast<int>((n_ops + fill_block - 1) / fill_block);\n        fill_uniform_keys<<<fill_grid, fill_block>>>(\n            d_keys, n_ops, args.seed, key_range);\n        cudaGetLastError() >> CUDA_CHECK;\n        derive_values<<<fill_grid, fill_block>>>(d_keys, d_values, n_ops);\n        cudaGetLastError() >> CUDA_CHECK;\n    }\n\n    // Construct the table. WarpCore auto-inits at construction.\n    Table table(capacity);\n\n    CSVWriter csv(args.output_dir / \"insert.csv\",\n        \"library,workload,slot_bytes,capacity,capacity_bytes,\"\n        \"key_range,alpha,load_factor,block_size,n_ops,bytes_per_op,\"\n        \"seed,rep,tag,time_ms,ops_per_sec,gbps_floor,gbps_corrected,\"\n        \"total_probes,total_failures\");\n\n    EventTimer timer;\n\n    run_benchmark_loop(args.warmups, args.reps, timer,\n        /*setup=*/  [&]() { table.init(); },  // (re)initialize all slots to empty\n        /*launch=*/ [&]() { table.insert(d_keys, d_values, n_ops); },\n        /*after=*/  [&](int rep, float ms) {\n            const double ops_per_sec =\n                static_cast<double>(n_ops) / (static_cast<double>(ms) * 1.0e-3);\n            const double gbps_floor =\n                static_cast<double>(n_ops) * static_cast<double>(bytes_per_op)\n                    / (static_cast<double>(ms) * 1.0e6);\n            csv.write_row(format_row(\n                slot_bytes, capacity, capacity_bytes,\n                std::size_t{key_range}, /*alpha=*/1.0, args.load_factor,\n                args.block_size, n_ops, bytes_per_op,\n                args.seed, rep, args.tag,\n                ms, ops_per_sec, gbps_floor));\n        });\n\n    cudaFree(d_keys);\n    cudaFree(d_values);\n    return 0;\n}\n"
}
```

</details>

```json
  "content": "// Get benchmark against WarpCore's `SingleValueHashTable`.\n//\n// Mirrors benchmarks/benchmark_get.cu and the cuco baseline; all three\n// libraries share get.csv.  // unused; kept for CSV uniformity\n    int           warmups        = 2;\n    int           reps           = 16;\n    std::uint32_t insert_seed    = 42;\n    std::uint32_t get_seed       = 1729;\n    std::string   tag            = \"\";\n    std::filesystem::path output_dir;\n};\n\n[[noreturn]] void die_usage(const char* prog) {\n    std::fprintf(stderr,\n        \"usage: %s --output-dir DIR [options]\\n\"\n        \"  Same shape as benchmarks/benchmark_get; see that for the full flag list.\\n\",\n        prog);\n    std::exit(1);\n}\n\nArgs parse_args(int argc, char** argv) {\n    Args a;\n    for (int i = 1; i < argc; ++i) {\n        std::string flag = argv[i];\n        auto get_val = [&]() -> std::string {\n            if (i + 1 >= argc) { std::fprintf(stderr, \"Missing value for %s\\n\", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }\n            return argv[++i];\n        };\n        if      (flag == \"--capacity-bytes\") a.capacity_bytes = std::stoull(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--load-factor\")    a.load_factor    = std::stod(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--block-size\")     a.block_size     = std::stoi(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--warmups\")        a.warmups        = std::stoi(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--reps\")           a.reps           = std::stoi(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--insert-seed\")    a.insert_seed    = static_cast<std::uint32_t>(std::stoul(get_val()));\n        else if (flag == \"--get-seed\")       a.get_seed       = static_cast<std::uint32_t>(std::stoul(get_val()));\n        else if (flag == \"--tag\")            a.tag            = get_val();\n        else if (flag == \"--output-dir\")     a.output_dir     = get_val();\n        else { std::fprintf(stderr, \"Unknown flag: %s\\n\", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }\n    }\n    if (a.output_dir.empty()) die_usage(argv[0]);\n    if (a.load_factor <= 0.0) { std::fprintf(stderr, \"--load-factor must be > 0\\n\"); std::exit(1); }\n    return a;\n}\n\nstd::string format_row(\n    std::size_t slot_bytes, std::size_t capacity, std::size_t capacity_bytes,\n    std::size_t key_range, double alpha, double load_factor,\n    int block_size, std::size_t n_ops, std::size_t bytes_per_op,\n    std::uint32_t insert_seed, std::uint32_t get_seed,\n    int rep, const std::string& tag,\n    float time_ms, double ops_per_sec, double gbps_floor,\n    std::uint64_t total_hits, std::uint64_t total_misses)\n{\n    std::ostringstream s;\n    s.precision(9);\n    s << \"warpcore,get,\"\n      << slot_bytes      << \",\"\n      << capacity        << \",\"\n      << capacity_bytes  << \",\"\n      << key_range       << \",\"\n      << alpha           << \",\"\n      << load_factor     << \",\"\n      << block_size      << \",\"\n      << n_ops           << \",\"\n      << bytes_per_op    << \",\"\n      << insert_seed     << \",\"\n      << get_seed        << \",\"\n      << rep             << \",\"\n      << tag             << \",\"\n      << time_ms         << \",\"\n      << ops_per_sec     << \",\"\n      << gbps_floor      << \",\"\n      << \",\"             // gbps_corrected\n      << \",\"             // total_probes\n      << total_hits      << \",\"\n      << total_misses    << \",\"\n      << \"\";             // prefill_failures\n    return s.str();\n}\n\n__global__ void derive_values(\n    const Key* __restrict__ keys, Value* __restrict__ values, std::size_t n)\n{\n    const std::size_t tid = blockIdx.x * std::size_t{blockDim.x} + threadIdx.x;\n    if (tid >= n) return;\n    values[tid] = gpurhh::detail::fmix32(keys[tid]);\n}\n\n} // namespace\n\nint main(int argc, char** argv) {\n    const Args args = parse_args(argc, argv);\n\n    constexpr std::size_t slot_bytes   = sizeof(Key) + sizeof(Value);\n    constexpr std::size_t bytes_per_op = 128;\n    const std::size_t capacity       = args.capacity_bytes / slot_bytes;\n    const std::size_t capacity_bytes = capacity * slot_bytes;\n    const Key key_range = static_cast<Key>(capacity);\n    const std::size_t n_ops =\n        static_cast<std::size_t>(args.load_factor * static_cast<double>(capacity));\n\n    if (n_ops == 0) {\n        std::fprintf(stderr, \"Computed n_ops = 0; raise --load-factor or --capacity-bytes\\n\");\n        std::exit(1);\n    }\n\n    std::fprintf(stderr,\n        \"[warpcore get] capacity=%zu slots (%zu B), F=%.3f, n_ops=%zu, \"\n        \"warmups=%d, reps=%d, insert_seed=%u, get_seed=%u, tag=\\\"%s\\\"\\n\",\n        capacity, capacity_bytes, args.load_factor, n_ops,\n        args.warmups, args.reps, args.insert_seed, args.get_seed, args.tag.c_str());\n\n    // --- pre-fill (untimed) ---\n    Key*   d_insert_keys   = nullptr;\n    Value* d_insert_values = nullptr;\n    cudaMalloc(&d_insert_keys,   n_ops * sizeof(Key))   >> CUDA_CHECK;\n    cudaMalloc(&d_insert_values, n_ops * sizeof(Value)) >> CUDA_CHECK;\n    {\n        constexpr int fill_block = 256;\n        const int fill_grid =\n            static_cast<int>((n_ops + fill_block - 1) / fill_block);\n        fill_uniform_keys<<<fill_grid, fill_block>>>(\n            d_insert_keys, n_ops, args.insert_seed, key_range);\n        cudaGetLastError() >> CUDA_CHECK;\n        derive_values<<<fill_grid, fill_block>>>(d_insert_keys, d_insert_values, n_ops);\n        cudaGetLastError() >> CUDA_CHECK;\n    }\n\n    Table table(capacity);\n    table.insert(d_insert_keys, d_insert_values, n_ops);\n    cudaDeviceSynchronize() >> CUDA_CHECK;\n    cudaFree(d_insert_keys);\n    cudaFree(d_insert_values);\n\n    // --- get key buffer ---\n    Key* d_get_keys = nullptr;\n    cudaMalloc(&d_get_keys, n_ops * sizeof(Key)) >> CUDA_CHECK;\n    {\n        constexpr int fill_block = 256;\n        const int fill_grid =\n            static_cast<int>((n_ops + fill_block - 1) / fill_block);\n        fill_uniform_keys<<<fill_grid, fill_block>>>(\n            d_get_keys, n_ops, args.get_seed, key_range);\n        cudaGetLastError() >> CUDA_CHECK;\n    }\n\n    // Output buffer for retrieved values. WarpCore writes its empty-value\n    // sentinel on miss; we detect via the table's empty_value() report.\n    Value* d_out = nullptr;\n    cudaMalloc(&d_out, n_ops * sizeof(Value)) >> CUDA_CHECK;\n\n    CSVWriter csv(args.output_dir / \"get.csv\",\n        \"library,workload,slot_bytes,capacity,capacity_bytes,\"\n        \"key_range,alpha,load_factor,block_size,n_ops,bytes_per_op,\"\n        \"insert_seed,get_seed,rep,tag,time_ms,ops_per_sec,gbps_floor,gbps_corrected,\"\n        \"total_probes,total_hits,total_misses,prefill_failures\");\n\n    EventTimer timer;\n\n    // Same caching pattern as the cuco baseline: compute hit/miss counts\n    // once on the first rep, reuse for subsequent rows.\n    std::vector<Value> h_out(n_ops);\n    std::uint64_t cached_hits   = 0;\n    std::uint64_t cached_misses = 0;\n    bool counted = false;\n    const Value empty_value_sentinel = Table::empty_value();\n\n    run_benchmark_loop(args.warmups, args.reps, timer,\n        /*setup=*/  []() {},\n        /*launch=*/ [&]() {\n            table.retrieve(d_get_keys, n_ops, d_out);\n        },\n        /*after=*/  [&](int rep, float ms) {\n            if (!counted) {\n                cudaMemcpy(h_out.data(), d_out, n_ops * sizeof(Value),\n                           cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost) >> CUDA_CHECK;\n                for (std::size_t i = 0; i < n_ops; ++i) {\n                    if (h_out[i] == empty_value_sentinel) ++cached_misses;\n                    else                                  ++cached_hits;\n                }\n                counted = true;\n            }\n            const double ops_per_sec =\n                static_cast<double>(n_ops) / (static_cast<double>(ms) * 1.0e-3);\n            const double gbps_floor =\n                static_cast<double>(n_ops) * static_cast<double>(bytes_per_op)\n                    / (static_cast<double>(ms) * 1.0e6);\n            csv.write_row(format_row(\n                slot_bytes, capacity, capacity_bytes,\n                std::size_t{key_range}, /*alpha=*/1.0, args.load_factor,\n                args.block_size, n_ops, bytes_per_op,\n                args.insert_seed, args.get_seed, rep, args.tag,\n                ms, ops_per_sec, gbps_floor,\n                cached_hits, cached_misses));\n        });\n\n    cudaFree(d_get_keys);\n    cudaFree(d_out);\n    return 0;\n}\n"
}
```

</details>

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:41:23.449Z

Done. WarpCore baselines added:

- [benchmarks/baselines/warpcore/benchmark_insert.cu](benchmarks/baselines/warpcore/benchmark_insert.cu) — uses `warpcore::SingleValueHashTable<uint32_t, uint32_t>`. Allocates a side `d_values` buffer populated via a small `derive_values` kernel (`value = fmix32(key)`) so the per-key state flowing matches cuco / gpurhh. Per-rep setup calls `table.init()` to reset. Insert via `table.insert(d_keys, d_values, n_ops)`.
- [benchmarks/baselines/warpcore/benchmark_get.cu](benchmarks/baselines/warpcore/benchmark_get.cu) — pre-fills the table once via the same insert flow, then `table.retrieve(d_get_keys, n_ops, d_out)` per rep. Hits/misses counted via `Table::empty_value()` sentinel check on the output buffer (same cached-on-first-rep pattern as the cuco get).
- [scripts/benchmark-baselines.sh](scripts/benchmark-baselines.sh) — warpcore section added; same `(0.5, 0.7, 0.85, 0.95)` F grid as cuco for the same reason (fixed-capacity table).
- Makefile already detects `external/include/warpcore/` via `HAVE_WARPCORE` and picks the sources up; the baseline pattern rule already adds the needed nvcc flags.

After `bash scripts/setup-baselines.sh` and `make benchmarks`, you should have four new binaries under `build/benchmarks/baselines/` (two cuco, two warpcore). Run `bash scripts/benchmark-baselines.sh output/2026-05-18_02-27-54/` (or whatever dir you'd like to fold into) to fill in the comparison rows.

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:42:59.089Z

Easy fix — extended `__device__` lambdas need an explicit trailing return type when their result type gets queried from host code (which `thrust::transform_iterator` does). Adding the `-> cuco::pair<Key, Value>` annotation to both files.

```json
{
  "file_path": "[repo]/scripts/benchmark.sh",
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n#\n# Run the gpurhh benchmark sweep, optionally followed by the external\n# baseline sweeps (cuCollections, WarpCore).\n#\n# Writes per-workload CSV files (memcpy.csv, insert.csv, get.csv) plus\n# a run_info.txt sidecar and a benchmark.log transcript to the output\n# directory. The output dir defaults to output/<timestamp>/ under the\n# repo root, so consecutive runs don't collide.\n#\n# Usage:\n#     scripts/benchmark.sh [--with-baselines] [output-dir]\n#\n#   --with-baselines    Also run any baseline libraries whose binaries\n#                       were built (controlled by whether\n#                       external/include/<lib>/ exists at make-time;\n#                       run scripts/setup-baselines.sh to populate).\n#                       Baseline rows append to the same insert.csv /\n#                       get.csv as gpurhh rows; the `library` column\n#                       distinguishes them.\n\nset -euo pipefail\n\n# --- argument parsing ------------------------------------------------------\n\nWITH_BASELINES=0\nPOSITIONAL=()\nwhile [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do\n    case \"$1\" in\n        --with-baselines) WITH_BASELINES=1; shift ;;\n        --help|-h)\n            sed -n '2,20p' \"$0\" | sed 's/^# //; s/^#//'\n            exit 0\n            ;;\n        --*)\n            echo \"Unknown flag: $1\" >&2\n            echo \"Try '$0 --help' for usage.\" >&2\n            exit 1\n            ;;\n        *)\n            POSITIONAL+=(\"$1\")\n            shift\n            ;;\n    esac\ndone\nset -- \"${POSITIONAL[@]}\"\n\nREPO_ROOT=\"$(cd \"$(dirname \"${BASH_SOURCE[0]}\")/..\" && pwd)\"\nBUILD_DIR=\"${REPO_ROOT}/build/benchmarks\"\n\nif [[ $# -ge 1 ]]; then\n    OUTPUT_DIR=\"$1\"\nelse\n    OUTPUT_DIR=\"${REPO_ROOT}/output/$(date +%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S)\"\nfi\nmkdir -p \"${OUTPUT_DIR}\"\n\n# Mirror stdout + stderr to a log file in the output dir.\nLOG_FILE=\"${OUTPUT_DIR}/benchmark.log\"\nexec > >(tee -a \"${LOG_FILE}\") 2>&1\n\n# Make sure the gpurhh binaries are built.\nfor bin in benchmark_memcpy benchmark_insert benchmark_get; do\n    if [[ ! -x \"${BUILD_DIR}/${bin}\" ]]; then\n        echo \"Missing executable: ${BUILD_DIR}/${bin}\" >&2\n        echo \"Build first with:   make benchmarks\" >&2\n        exit 1\n    fi\ndone\n\necho \"==> Writing CSV output and run_info to ${OUTPUT_DIR}\"\n\n{\n    echo \"date:        $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)\"\n    echo \"uname:       $(uname -srm)\"\n    echo \"nvcc:        $(nvcc --version 2>/dev/null | tail -n1 || echo 'not found')\"\n    echo \"nvidia-smi:  $(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name,driver_version,memory.total,power.limit --format=csv,noheader 2>/dev/null || echo 'not found')\"\n} > \"${OUTPUT_DIR}/run_info.txt\"\n\n# --- sweep grid (gpurhh) ---------------------------------------------------\n#\n# Capacity is held fixed at 1 GiB (DRAM-resident, well past the 4090's\n# 72 MB L2). α is implicitly 1 (key range = capacity). F sweeps over\n# the regime where Robin Hood actually has work to do; block_size\n# sweeps to spot-check whether launch shape moves the number.\nCAPACITY_BYTES=$((1 << 30))\nLOAD_FACTORS=(0.5 1.0 1.5 2.0 3.0)\nBLOCK_SIZES=(64 128 256 512 1024)\nTAG=\"sweep\"\n\n# --- memcpy peak-bandwidth reference --------------------------------------\necho \"==> memcpy baseline (${CAPACITY_BYTES} B)\"\n\"${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_memcpy\" \\\n    --output-dir \"${OUTPUT_DIR}\" \\\n    --bytes \"${CAPACITY_BYTES}\" \\\n    --tag \"${TAG}\"\n\n# --- gpurhh insert and get sweeps -----------------------------------------\nfor f in \"${LOAD_FACTORS[@]}\"; do\n    for b in \"${BLOCK_SIZES[@]}\"; do\n        echo \"==> insert F=${f} block=${b}\"\n        \"${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_insert\" \\\n            --output-dir \"${OUTPUT_DIR}\" \\\n            --capacity-bytes \"${CAPACITY_BYTES}\" \\\n            --load-factor \"${f}\" \\\n            --block-size \"${b}\" \\\n            --tag \"${TAG}\"\n\n        echo \"==> get    F=${f} block=${b}\"\n        \"${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_get\" \\\n            --output-dir \"${OUTPUT_DIR}\" \\\n            --capacity-bytes \"${CAPACITY_BYTES}\" \\\n            --load-factor \"${f}\" \\\n            --block-size \"${b}\" \\\n            --tag \"${TAG}\"\n    done\ndone\n\n# --- baselines (only with --with-baselines) -------------------------------\nif [[ \"${WITH_BASELINES}\" -eq 1 ]]; then\n    ran_any=0\n\n    # cuCollections: fixed-capacity static_map; F sweep stops short of 1.0.\n    # cuco's bulk API picks its own launch; block_size is not swept.\n    CUCO_DIR=\"${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/cuco\"\n    if [[ -x \"${CUCO_DIR}/benchmark_insert\" && -x \"${CUCO_DIR}/benchmark_get\" ]]; then\n        CUCO_LOAD_FACTORS=(0.5 0.7 0.85 0.95)\n        for f in \"${CUCO_LOAD_FACTORS[@]}\"; do\n            echo \"==> cuco insert F=${f}\"\n            \"${CUCO_DIR}/benchmark_insert\" \\\n                --output-dir \"${OUTPUT_DIR}\" \\\n                --capacity-bytes \"${CAPACITY_BYTES}\" \\\n                --load-factor \"${f}\" \\\n                --tag \"${TAG}\"\n\n            echo \"==> cuco get    F=${f}\"\n            \"${CUCO_DIR}/benchmark_get\" \\\n                --output-dir \"${OUTPUT_DIR}\" \\\n                --capacity-bytes \"${CAPACITY_BYTES}\" \\\n                --load-factor \"${f}\" \\\n                --tag \"${TAG}\"\n        done\n        ran_any=1\n    fi\n\n    # WarpCore: same shape as cuco — fixed capacity, F < 1, no block sweep.\n    WARPCORE_DIR=\"${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/warpcore\"\n    if [[ -x \"${WARPCORE_DIR}/benchmark_insert\" && -x \"${WARPCORE_DIR}/benchmark_get\" ]]; then\n        WARPCORE_LOAD_FACTORS=(0.5 0.7 0.85 0.95)\n        for f in \"${WARPCORE_LOAD_FACTORS[@]}\"; do\n            echo \"==> warpcore insert F=${f}\"\n            \"${WARPCORE_DIR}/benchmark_insert\" \\\n                --output-dir \"${OUTPUT_DIR}\" \\\n                --capacity-bytes \"${CAPACITY_BYTES}\" \\\n                --load-factor \"${f}\" \\\n                --tag \"${TAG}\"\n\n            echo \"==> warpcore get    F=${f}\"\n            \"${WARPCORE_DIR}/benchmark_get\" \\\n                --output-dir \"${OUTPUT_DIR}\" \\\n                --capacity-bytes \"${CAPACITY_BYTES}\" \\\n                --load-factor \"${f}\" \\\n                --tag \"${TAG}\"\n        done\n        ran_any=1\n    fi\n\n    if [[ \"${ran_any}\" -eq 0 ]]; then\n        echo \"==> --with-baselines requested but no baseline binaries found under\" >&2\n        echo \"    ${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/.\" >&2\n        echo \"    Run scripts/setup-baselines.sh and 'make benchmarks' to enable.\" >&2\n    fi\nfi\n\necho \"==> Done. Results in ${OUTPUT_DIR}\"\n"
}
```

</details>

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:45:24.204Z

Done. Layout now:

- **One script**: [scripts/benchmark.sh](scripts/benchmark.sh) handles both modes via `--with-baselines`.
- **Two make targets**:
  - `make benchmark` — gpurhh only (memcpy reference + insert/get sweep).
  - `make benchmark-with-baselines` — adds cuco and warpcore sweeps after.
- **Direct invocation also works**: `bash scripts/benchmark.sh --with-baselines [output-dir]` for full control.
- `scripts/benchmark-baselines.sh` deleted.

If `--with-baselines` is requested but none of the baseline binaries built, the script prints a warning to stderr but doesn't fail — the gpurhh run already completed successfully and is in the output dir.

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:46:23.514Z

---

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:46:25.420Z

Yes, worth fixing. Most regex-based shell highlighters can't tell `<<` inside arithmetic expansion from a heredoc start, so they get confused after seeing `(( ... << ... ))`. Trivial fix — express the constant as multiplication, same value, no `<<`:

---

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:46:26.651Z

**→ Tool call: `Edit`** (id `tool-592`)

```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "[repo]/scripts/benchmark.sh",
  "old_string": "CAPACITY_BYTES=$((1 << 30))",
  "new_string": "CAPACITY_BYTES=$((1024 * 1024 * 1024))   # 1 GiB"
}
```

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:46:34.857Z

Same number (`1073741824`), no `<<` to confuse the lexer. The `.cu` files still use `std::size_t{1} << 30` — that's C++ left-shift, which any C++ highlighter handles fine.

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:51:07.472Z

Re-run `bash scripts/setup-baselines.sh` to fetch the missing `helpers/` headers. After that, `external/include/helpers/` will exist alongside `cuco/` and `warpcore/`, and warpcore's `#include <helpers/cuda_helpers.cuh>` resolves via the same `-Iexternal/include` flag that's already in the Makefile.

If you ever also need `kiss_rng` (for `warpcore::random_distributions` and a few other types we don't currently use), the same pin is `https://github.com/sleeepyjack/kiss_rng.git @ 9c6859ec6ffaa2a853636dbda4fc891e40737cd2`.

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:53:27.108Z

<em>Thinking…</em>

---

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T01:53:31.512Z

WarpCore's `SingleValueHashTable` doesn't have `empty_value()` — its template parameters are `EmptyKey` (sentinel for the *key*, not the value). Misses are reported via the optional `StatusHandler` machinery, which is more invasive to wire up than the empty cells are worth for this comparison. Falling back to empty hits/misses cells like the cuco get does for its less-trustworthy columns.

---

```json
{
```
