```
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	#
3	# Run the gpurhh benchmark sweep, optionally followed by the external
4	# baseline sweeps (cuCollections, WarpCore).
5	#
6	# Writes per-workload CSV files (memcpy.csv, insert.csv, get.csv) plus
7	# a run_info.txt sidecar and a benchmark.log transcript to the output
8	# directory. The output dir defaults to output/<timestamp>/ under the
9	# repo root, so consecutive runs don't collide.
10	#
11	# Usage:
12	#     scripts/benchmark.sh <one-or-more-libraries> [output-dir]
13	#
14	# Pick at least one library to run. Combining flags runs each
15	# selected library's sweep in turn. Passing no flag prints this usage
16	# and exits — no default, since benchmark runs are heavy.
17	#
18	#   --gpurhh    Run the gpurhh sweep (memcpy reference + insert + get).
19	#   --cuco      Run the cuCollections baseline (must be set up via
20	#               scripts/setup-baselines.sh).
21	#   --warpcore  Run the WarpCore baseline (likewise).
22	#   --all       Shortcut for "every library whose binaries built";
23	#               missing baselines are skipped with a warning rather
24	#               than erroring.
25	#
26	# Library-specific flags are strict — they error if the corresponding
27	# binaries weren't built. Use --all if you want lenient "run what's
28	# there" behavior. All rows land in the same insert.csv / get.csv;
29	# the `library` column distinguishes them.
30	
31	set -euo pipefail
32	
33	# --- argument parsing ------------------------------------------------------
34	
35	print_usage() {
36	    sed -n '2,30p' "$0" | sed 's/^# //; s/^#//'
37	}
38	
39	RUN_GPURHH=0
40	RUN_CUCO=0
41	RUN_WARPCORE=0
42	LENIENT=0       # set by --all: skip missing baselines instead of erroring
43	POSITIONAL=()
44	while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
45	    case "$1" in
46	        --gpurhh)   RUN_GPURHH=1;                                shift ;;
47	        --cuco)     RUN_CUCO=1;                                  shift ;;
48	        --warpcore) RUN_WARPCORE=1;                              shift ;;
49	        --all)      RUN_GPURHH=1; RUN_CUCO=1; RUN_WARPCORE=1
50	                    LENIENT=1;                                   shift ;;
51	        --help|-h)  print_usage; exit 0 ;;
52	        --*)
53	            echo "Unknown flag: $1" >&2
54	            echo "Try '$0 --help' for usage." >&2
55	            exit 1
56	            ;;
57	        *)  POSITIONAL+=("$1"); shift ;;
58	    esac
59	done
60	set -- "${POSITIONAL[@]}"
61	
62	if [[ $((RUN_GPURHH + RUN_CUCO + RUN_WARPCORE)) -eq 0 ]]; then
63	    print_usage >&2
64	    exit 1
65	fi
66	
67	REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
68	BUILD_DIR="${REPO_ROOT}/build/benchmarks"
69	
70	if [[ $# -ge 1 ]]; then
71	    OUTPUT_DIR="$1"
72	else
73	    OUTPUT_DIR="${REPO_ROOT}/output/$(date +%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S)"
74	fi
75	mkdir -p "${OUTPUT_DIR}"
76	
77	# Mirror stdout + stderr to a log file in the output dir.
78	LOG_FILE="${OUTPUT_DIR}/benchmark.log"
79	exec > >(tee -a "${LOG_FILE}") 2>&1
80	
81	# Per-library binary existence check. For an explicit library flag,
82	# missing binaries are an error; for --all (LENIENT=1) we drop the
83	# library silently with a notice and continue.
84	require_or_drop() {
85	    local label="$1"; shift
86	    for bin in "$@"; do
87	        if [[ ! -x "${bin}" ]]; then
88	            if [[ "${LENIENT}" -eq 1 ]]; then
89	                echo "==> ${label} binaries not built; skipping (--all is lenient)" >&2
90	                return 1
91	            fi
92	            echo "Missing executable: ${bin}" >&2
93	            echo "Build first with:   make benchmarks  (and scripts/setup-baselines.sh for baselines)" >&2
94	            exit 1
95	        fi
96	    done
97	    return 0
98	}
99	
100	if [[ "${RUN_GPURHH}" -eq 1 ]]; then
101	    require_or_drop "gpurhh" \
102	        "${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_memcpy" \
103	        "${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_insert" \
104	        "${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_get" \
105	        || RUN_GPURHH=0
106	fi
107	if [[ "${RUN_CUCO}" -eq 1 ]]; then
108	    require_or_drop "cuco" \
109	        "${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/cuco/benchmark_insert" \
110	        "${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/cuco/benchmark_get" \
111	        || RUN_CUCO=0
112	fi
113	if [[ "${RUN_WARPCORE}" -eq 1 ]]; then
114	    require_or_drop "warpcore" \
115	        "${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/warpcore/benchmark_insert" \
116	        "${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/warpcore/benchmark_get" \
117	        || RUN_WARPCORE=0
118	fi
119	
120	if [[ $((RUN_GPURHH + RUN_CUCO + RUN_WARPCORE)) -eq 0 ]]; then
121	    echo "Nothing to run." >&2
122	    exit 1
123	fi
124	
125	echo "==> Writing CSV output and run_info to ${OUTPUT_DIR}"
126	
127	{
128	    echo "date:        $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
129	    echo "uname:       $(uname -srm)"
130	    echo "nvcc:        $(nvcc --version 2>/dev/null | tail -n1 || echo 'not found')"
131	    echo "nvidia-smi:  $(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name,driver_version,memory.total,power.limit --format=csv,noheader 2>/dev/null || echo 'not found')"
132	} > "${OUTPUT_DIR}/run_info.txt"
133	
134	# --- sweep grid (gpurhh) ---------------------------------------------------
135	#
136	# Capacity is held fixed at 1 GiB (DRAM-resident, well past the 4090's
137	# 72 MB L2). α is implicitly 1 (key range = capacity). F sweeps over
138	# the regime where Robin Hood actually has work to do; block_size
139	# sweeps to spot-check whether launch shape moves the number.
140	CAPACITY_BYTES=$((1024 * 1024 * 1024))   # 1 GiB
141	LOAD_FACTORS=(0.5 1.0 1.5 2.0 3.0)
142	BLOCK_SIZES=(64 128 256 512 1024)
143	TAG="sweep"
144	
145	# --- gpurhh (memcpy reference + insert + get sweeps) ---------------------
146	if [[ "${RUN_GPURHH}" -eq 1 ]]; then
147	    echo "==> memcpy baseline (${CAPACITY_BYTES} B)"
148	    "${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_memcpy" \
149	        --output-dir "${OUTPUT_DIR}" \
150	        --bytes "${CAPACITY_BYTES}" \
151	        --tag "${TAG}"
152	
153	    for f in "${LOAD_FACTORS[@]}"; do
154	        for b in "${BLOCK_SIZES[@]}"; do
155	            echo "==> gpurhh insert F=${f} block=${b}"
156	            "${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_insert" \
157	                --output-dir "${OUTPUT_DIR}" \
158	                --capacity-bytes "${CAPACITY_BYTES}" \
159	                --load-factor "${f}" \
160	                --block-size "${b}" \
161	                --tag "${TAG}"
162	
163	            echo "==> gpurhh get    F=${f} block=${b}"
164	            "${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_get" \
165	                --output-dir "${OUTPUT_DIR}" \
166	                --capacity-bytes "${CAPACITY_BYTES}" \
167	                --load-factor "${f}" \
168	                --block-size "${b}" \
169	                --tag "${TAG}"
170	        done
171	    done
172	fi
173	
174	# --- cuCollections (fixed-capacity static_map; F < 1, no block sweep) -----
175	if [[ "${RUN_CUCO}" -eq 1 ]]; then
176	    CUCO_DIR="${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/cuco"
177	    CUCO_LOAD_FACTORS=(0.5 0.7 0.85 0.95)
178	    for f in "${CUCO_LOAD_FACTORS[@]}"; do
179	        echo "==> cuco insert F=${f}"
180	        "${CUCO_DIR}/benchmark_insert" \
181	            --output-dir "${OUTPUT_DIR}" \
182	            --capacity-bytes "${CAPACITY_BYTES}" \
183	            --load-factor "${f}" \
184	            --tag "${TAG}"
185	
186	        echo "==> cuco get    F=${f}"
187	        "${CUCO_DIR}/benchmark_get" \
188	            --output-dir "${OUTPUT_DIR}" \
189	            --capacity-bytes "${CAPACITY_BYTES}" \
190	            --load-factor "${f}" \
191	            --tag "${TAG}"
192	    done
193	fi
194	
195	# --- WarpCore (same shape as cuco) ----------------------------------------
196	if [[ "${RUN_WARPCORE}" -eq 1 ]]; then
197	    WARPCORE_DIR="${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/warpcore"
198	    WARPCORE_LOAD_FACTORS=(0.5 0.7 0.85 0.95)
199	    for f in "${WARPCORE_LOAD_FACTORS[@]}"; do
200	        echo "==> warpcore insert F=${f}"
201	        "${WARPCORE_DIR}/benchmark_insert" \
202	            --output-dir "${OUTPUT_DIR}" \
203	            --capacity-bytes "${CAPACITY_BYTES}" \
204	            --load-factor "${f}" \
205	            --tag "${TAG}"
206	
207	        echo "==> warpcore get    F=${f}"
208	        "${WARPCORE_DIR}/benchmark_get" \
209	            --output-dir "${OUTPUT_DIR}" \
210	            --capacity-bytes "${CAPACITY_BYTES}" \
211	            --load-factor "${f}" \
212	            --tag "${TAG}"
213	    done
214	fi
215	
216	echo "==> Done. Results in ${OUTPUT_DIR}"
217	
```

</details>

```json
{
  "file_path": "[repo]/scripts/benchmark.sh",
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n#\n# Run the gpurhh benchmark sweep, optionally followed by the external\n# baseline sweeps (cuCollections, WarpCore).\n#\n# Writes per-workload CSV files (memcpy.csv, insert.csv, get.csv) and\n# their counter-instrumented siblings (insert_counters.csv,\n# get_counters.csv) plus a run_info.txt sidecar and a benchmark.log\n# transcript to the output directory. The output dir defaults to\n# output/<timestamp>/ under the repo root, so consecutive runs don't\n# collide.\n#\n# Usage:\n#     scripts/benchmark.sh <one-or-more-libraries> [output-dir]\n#\n# Pick at least one library to run. Combining flags runs each\n# selected library's sweep in turn. Passing no flag prints this usage\n# and exits — no default, since benchmark runs are heavy.\n#\n#   --gpurhh           Run the gpurhh comparison sweep (memcpy + insert + get).\n#   --gpurhh-counters  Run the gpurhh counter-instrumented sweep\n#                      (insert_counters + get_counters; produces probe\n#                      / failure / hit columns).\n#   --cuco             Run the cuCollections baseline.\n#   --warpcore         Run the WarpCore baseline.\n#   --all              Shortcut for every library whose binaries built;\n#                      missing baselines are skipped with a notice\n#                      rather than erroring.\n#\n# Library-specific flags are strict — they error if the corresponding\n# binaries weren't built. --all is lenient. Comparison rows from all\n# libraries land in the same insert.csv / get.csv; the `library` column\n# distinguishes them. Counter rows go to separate _counters.csv files.\n\nset -euo pipefail\n\n# --- argument parsing ------------------------------------------------------\n\nprint_usage() {\n    sed -n '2,32p' \"$0\" | sed 's/^# //; s/^#//'\n}\n\nRUN_GPURHH=0\nRUN_GPURHH_COUNTERS=0\nRUN_CUCO=0\nRUN_WARPCORE=0\nLENIENT=0\nPOSITIONAL=()\nwhile [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do\n    case \"$1\" in\n        --gpurhh)           RUN_GPURHH=1;          shift ;;\n        --gpurhh-counters)  RUN_GPURHH_COUNTERS=1; shift ;;\n        --cuco)             RUN_CUCO=1;            shift ;;\n        --warpcore)         RUN_WARPCORE=1;        shift ;;\n        --all)              RUN_GPURHH=1; RUN_GPURHH_COUNTERS=1\n                            RUN_CUCO=1; RUN_WARPCORE=1\n                            LENIENT=1;             shift ;;\n        --help|-h)          print_usage; exit 0 ;;\n        --*)\n            echo \"Unknown flag: $1\" >&2\n            echo \"Try '$0 --help' for usage.\" >&2\n            exit 1\n            ;;\n        *)  POSITIONAL+=(\"$1\"); shift ;;\n    esac\ndone\nset -- \"${POSITIONAL[@]}\"\n\nif [[ $((RUN_GPURHH + RUN_GPURHH_COUNTERS + RUN_CUCO + RUN_WARPCORE)) -eq 0 ]]; then\n    print_usage >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\nREPO_ROOT=\"$(cd \"$(dirname \"${BASH_SOURCE[0]}\")/..\" && pwd)\"\nBUILD_DIR=\"${REPO_ROOT}/build/benchmarks\"\n\nif [[ $# -ge 1 ]]; then\n    OUTPUT_DIR=\"$1\"\nelse\n    OUTPUT_DIR=\"${REPO_ROOT}/output/$(date +%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S)\"\nfi\nmkdir -p \"${OUTPUT_DIR}\"\n\nLOG_FILE=\"${OUTPUT_DIR}/benchmark.log\"\nexec > >(tee -a \"${LOG_FILE}\") 2>&1\n\nrequire_or_drop() {\n    local label=\"$1\"; shift\n    for bin in \"$@\"; do\n        if [[ ! -x \"${bin}\" ]]; then\n            if [[ \"${LENIENT}\" -eq 1 ]]; then\n                echo \"==> ${label} binaries not built; skipping (--all is lenient)\" >&2\n                return 1\n            fi\n            echo \"Missing executable: ${bin}\" >&2\n            echo \"Build first with:   make benchmarks  (and scripts/setup-baselines.sh for baselines)\" >&2\n            exit 1\n        fi\n    done\n    return 0\n}\n\nif [[ \"${RUN_GPURHH}\" -eq 1 ]]; then\n    require_or_drop \"gpurhh\" \\\n        \"${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_memcpy\" \\\n        \"${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_insert\" \\\n        \"${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_get\" \\\n        || RUN_GPURHH=0\nfi\nif [[ \"${RUN_GPURHH_COUNTERS}\" -eq 1 ]]; then\n    require_or_drop \"gpurhh-counters\" \\\n        \"${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_insert_counters\" \\\n        \"${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_get_counters\" \\\n        || RUN_GPURHH_COUNTERS=0\nfi\nif [[ \"${RUN_CUCO}\" -eq 1 ]]; then\n    require_or_drop \"cuco\" \\\n        \"${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/cuco/benchmark_insert\" \\\n        \"${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/cuco/benchmark_get\" \\\n        || RUN_CUCO=0\nfi\nif [[ \"${RUN_WARPCORE}\" -eq 1 ]]; then\n    require_or_drop \"warpcore\" \\\n        \"${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/warpcore/benchmark_insert\" \\\n        \"${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/warpcore/benchmark_get\" \\\n        || RUN_WARPCORE=0\nfi\n\nif [[ $((RUN_GPURHH + RUN_GPURHH_COUNTERS + RUN_CUCO + RUN_WARPCORE)) -eq 0 ]]; then\n    echo \"Nothing to run.\" >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\necho \"==> Writing CSV output and run_info to ${OUTPUT_DIR}\"\n\n{\n    echo \"date:        $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)\"\n    echo \"uname:       $(uname -srm)\"\n    echo \"nvcc:        $(nvcc --version 2>/dev/null | tail -n1 || echo 'not found')\"\n    echo \"nvidia-smi:  $(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name,driver_version,memory.total,power.limit --format=csv,noheader 2>/dev/null || echo 'not found')\"\n} > \"${OUTPUT_DIR}/run_info.txt\"\n\n# --- sweep grid ----------------------------------------------------------\n#\n# Capacity is fixed at 1 GiB worth of slots (DRAM-resident). α = 1 by\n# default: key_range = capacity. We sweep n_ops via convenient\n# multipliers, computed once below into a list of raw integer counts.\n# block_size is swept for gpurhh; cuco/warpcore have their own internal\n# launch shapes.\nSLOT_BYTES=8\nCAPACITY=$(( (1024 * 1024 * 1024) / SLOT_BYTES ))   # 1 GiB / 8 = 2^27\nKEY_RANGE=${CAPACITY}                                # α = 1\nBLOCK_SIZES=(64 128 256 512 1024)\nTAG=\"sweep\"\n\n# n_ops multipliers: f * capacity. We compute the raw integers once so\n# the rest of the script passes them as-is. Multipliers for gpurhh\n# (which can run past 1.0) and for cuco/warpcore (capped at < 1.0 to\n# stay below their fixed-capacity limits).\nGPURHH_NOPS=()\nfor mul in 0.5 1.0 1.5 2.0 3.0; do\n    GPURHH_NOPS+=($(python3 -c \"print(int($mul * $CAPACITY))\"))\ndone\nBASELINE_NOPS=()\nfor mul in 0.5 0.7 0.85 0.95; do\n    BASELINE_NOPS+=($(python3 -c \"print(int($mul * $CAPACITY))\"))\ndone\n\n# --- gpurhh comparison (memcpy + insert + get sweeps) --------------------\nif [[ \"${RUN_GPURHH}\" -eq 1 ]]; then\n    BYTES=$(( CAPACITY * SLOT_BYTES ))\n    echo \"==> memcpy baseline (${BYTES} B)\"\n    \"${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_memcpy\" \\\n        --output-dir \"${OUTPUT_DIR}\" \\\n        --bytes \"${BYTES}\" \\\n        --tag \"${TAG}\"\n\n    for n_ops in \"${GPURHH_NOPS[@]}\"; do\n        for b in \"${BLOCK_SIZES[@]}\"; do\n            echo \"==> gpurhh insert n_ops=${n_ops} block=${b}\"\n            \"${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_insert\" \\\n                --output-dir \"${OUTPUT_DIR}\" \\\n                --capacity \"${CAPACITY}\" \\\n                --key-range \"${KEY_RANGE}\" \\\n                --n-ops \"${n_ops}\" \\\n                --block-size \"${b}\" \\\n                --tag \"${TAG}\"\n\n            echo \"==> gpurhh get    n_ops=${n_ops} block=${b}\"\n            \"${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_get\" \\\n                --output-dir \"${OUTPUT_DIR}\" \\\n                --capacity \"${CAPACITY}\" \\\n                --key-range \"${KEY_RANGE}\" \\\n                --n-ops \"${n_ops}\" \\\n                --block-size \"${b}\" \\\n                --tag \"${TAG}\"\n        done\n    done\nfi\n\n# --- gpurhh counters (study the design with instrumentation on) ----------\nif [[ \"${RUN_GPURHH_COUNTERS}\" -eq 1 ]]; then\n    for n_ops in \"${GPURHH_NOPS[@]}\"; do\n        for b in \"${BLOCK_SIZES[@]}\"; do\n            echo \"==> gpurhh insert_counters n_ops=${n_ops} block=${b}\"\n            \"${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_insert_counters\" \\\n                --output-dir \"${OUTPUT_DIR}\" \\\n                --capacity \"${CAPACITY}\" \\\n                --key-range \"${KEY_RANGE}\" \\\n                --n-ops \"${n_ops}\" \\\n                --block-size \"${b}\" \\\n                --tag \"${TAG}\"\n\n            echo \"==> gpurhh get_counters    n_ops=${n_ops} block=${b}\"\n            \"${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_get_counters\" \\\n                --output-dir \"${OUTPUT_DIR}\" \\\n                --capacity \"${CAPACITY}\" \\\n                --key-range \"${KEY_RANGE}\" \\\n                --n-ops \"${n_ops}\" \\\n                --block-size \"${b}\" \\\n                --tag \"${TAG}\"\n        done\n    done\nfi\n\n# --- cuCollections (fixed-capacity static_map; no block sweep) -----------\nif [[ \"${RUN_CUCO}\" -eq 1 ]]; then\n    CUCO_DIR=\"${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/cuco\"\n    for n_ops in \"${BASELINE_NOPS[@]}\"; do\n        echo \"==> cuco insert n_ops=${n_ops}\"\n        \"${CUCO_DIR}/benchmark_insert\" \\\n            --output-dir \"${OUTPUT_DIR}\" \\\n            --capacity \"${CAPACITY}\" \\\n            --key-range \"${KEY_RANGE}\" \\\n            --n-ops \"${n_ops}\" \\\n            --tag \"${TAG}\"\n\n        echo \"==> cuco get    n_ops=${n_ops}\"\n        \"${CUCO_DIR}/benchmark_get\" \\\n            --output-dir \"${OUTPUT_DIR}\" \\\n            --capacity \"${CAPACITY}\" \\\n            --key-range \"${KEY_RANGE}\" \\\n            --n-ops \"${n_ops}\" \\\n            --tag \"${TAG}\"\n    done\nfi\n\n# --- WarpCore -----------------------------------------------------------\nif [[ \"${RUN_WARPCORE}\" -eq 1 ]]; then\n    WARPCORE_DIR=\"${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/warpcore\"\n    for n_ops in \"${BASELINE_NOPS[@]}\"; do\n        echo \"==> warpcore insert n_ops=${n_ops}\"\n        \"${WARPCORE_DIR}/benchmark_insert\" \\\n            --output-dir \"${OUTPUT_DIR}\" \\\n            --capacity \"${CAPACITY}\" \\\n            --key-range \"${KEY_RANGE}\" \\\n            --n-ops \"${n_ops}\" \\\n            --tag \"${TAG}\"\n\n        echo \"==> warpcore get    n_ops=${n_ops}\"\n        \"${WARPCORE_DIR}/benchmark_get\" \\\n            --output-dir \"${OUTPUT_DIR}\" \\\n            --capacity \"${CAPACITY}\" \\\n            --key-range \"${KEY_RANGE}\" \\\n            --n-ops \"${n_ops}\" \\\n            --tag \"${TAG}\"\n    done\nfi\n\necho \"==> Done. Results in ${OUTPUT_DIR}\"\n"
}
```

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T03:19:23.097Z

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T03:19:43.089Z

Sharp catch. Strictly: **no, they're not iid Uniform(0, N) draws**.

## What we actually generate

The kernel is:

```cpp
key[i] = fmix32(seed + i) % range
```

The "RNG" here is `fmix32` (Murmur3's finalizer) applied to consecutive integers `seed, seed+1, ..., seed+n_ops-1`. So:

1. **Pre-mod values are a permutation of consecutive integers via fmix32.** fmix32 is a bijection on uint32 — those `n_ops` outputs are *guaranteed distinct* in `[0, 2^32)`, not independent draws with replacement.
2. **Mod range produces possible duplicates.** With `range = 2^27`, each residue class corresponds to exactly `2^32 / 2^27 = 32` distinct pre-mod values. Two `seed+i` and `seed+j` collide after mod iff their fmix32 outputs land in the same residue class (different elements of the 32-element bucket).

So the actual distribution is: draw `n_ops` samples **without replacement** from `[0, 2^32)`, then bucket those into `range` residue classes of 32 elements each.

That's **hypergeometric** per-class counts, not Poisson — strictly different from iid Uniform.

## How close is it to iid Uniform in practice?

The variance correction factor is `(1 − n_ops / 2^32)`. For our sweep:

| F | n_ops | fraction of uint32 sampled | variance vs iid |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| 0.5 | 2²⁶ | 1.6% | 98.4% of iid |
| 1.0 | 2²⁷ | 3.1% | 96.9% |
| 2.0 | 2²⁸ | 6.3% | 93.8% |
| 3.0 | 3×2²⁷ | 9.4% | 90.6% |

Mean count per bucket is exactly `F` either way. The variance is slightly lower than iid would give — at F=3, ~9% lower. The expected-occupancy formula `1 − exp(−F)` is the same in expectation to leading order; the fluctuations around it are slightly tighter than iid would predict.

For practical benchmark purposes this is indistinguishable from iid uniform. The math we've been doing (occupancy formula, hit rate formula) is correct in expectation.

## When this would actually matter

- If we sampled close to all of `2^32` (n_ops near `2^32`), the without-replacement effect becomes huge. Not our regime.
- If we cared about per-bucket-count distribution moments beyond the mean — e.g., extreme tail behavior, max-bucket count — the hypergeometric model gives slightly tighter tails than Poisson. Not something we currently analyze.

## Implications for the comparison with cuco / warpcore

All three libraries get the **same** key generator (it's in `benchmarks.cuh`'s `fill_uniform_keys`), so they all see the same "not-quite-iid" distribution. The comparison stays fair.

## If we wanted true iid Uniform

Two reasonable paths:

- **cuRAND** — NVIDIA's GPU PRNG library. Per-thread state, stateful generator, true Bernoulli-each-draw. More setup, more memory for the per-thread state.
- **Linear congruential or PCG-style PRNG that's hash-seeded per-thread** — give each `tid` an independent seed and run a few rounds of LCG/PCG state advancement per draw. Still parallel-friendly, no extra device memory.

Neither is hard. But given the practical equivalence in our regime, I'd only switch if you specifically want the math to be exactly iid for a write-up. Worth knowing the distinction; not worth changing the code for unless we hit a regime where it matters.

(Tangentially: the seed-overlap bug we discussed earlier — `seed=42, get_seed=1729` producing input ranges that overlap — is a separate, larger effect that your single-seed-with-offset fix cleanly removes. The "not-quite-iid" is a small technical correction; the seed-overlap was making most "misses" actually be guaranteed hits.)

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T03:22:03.403Z

<em>Thinking…</em>

---

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T03:22:20.511Z

Yes, completely reasonable, and probably the cleanest answer to "are we measuring what we say we're measuring."

## The workflow

```cpp
// Host side, once per binary invocation:
std::mt19937 gen(seed);
std::uniform_int_distribution<std::uint32_t> dist(0, key_range - 1);
std::vector<std::uint32_t> h_keys(n_ops);
for (auto& k : h_keys) k = dist(gen);

// Then copy up:
cudaMemcpyAsync(d_keys, h_keys.data(),
                n_ops * sizeof(std::uint32_t),
                cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);
```

For the get binary using your single-seed proposal: same generator runs `2 * n_ops` draws, first half becomes insert keys, second half becomes get keys. One generator instance, two disjoint streams of *independent* draws — exactly the math we want.

## Costs

- **CPU time**: `std::mt19937` runs ~200M draws/sec single-threaded. At F=3 (n_ops = 403M), that's ~2s of CPU work per generator run. `xoshiro256++` or PCG would cut this to ~0.5s if we want. Trivial to parallelize across threads with one PRNG instance per thread + per-thread offsets if it ever matters.
- **Host RAM**: `n_ops × 4 B`. At F=3 → 1.6 GiB. Fine on any benchmark machine.
- **PCIe transfer**: ~50ms at PCIe Gen4 ×16. Lost in the noise.

All three costs happen *before* the timed kernel — measurements stay clean.

## What we gain

- **Genuinely iid Uniform(0, key_range) keys**, with all the standard statistical guarantees that math papers assume.
- **No need to caveat the hypergeometric correction** ("variance is 90.6% of iid at F=3" etc.). The analysis script can use the straightforward `Poisson(F)` model for bucket counts.
- **Cross-library fairness is preserved** — all libraries still see the same key buffer.
- **Realism**: actual users have keys on the host and copy them up. This matches that pattern. The current "generate keys on device via a deterministic hash of indices" pattern is benchmark-convenient but isn't how anyone uses hash tables in practice.

## What we lose

- ~1–2 seconds of host setup per binary invocation. Across the gpurhh sweep (~25 cells × {insert, get} = 50 invocations) that's ~1–2 min of extra wall-clock per full sweep. Negligible against the existing minute-scale sweep.
- A small amount of host RAM during the run.
- `fill_uniform_keys` kernel goes away (or stays for non-comparison utility purposes — not used in main sweep).

## Should we do it?

Yes. The cost is small, the cleanliness is real, and it eliminates a footgun I've been waving away ("not quite iid but close enough"). Especially worth doing now that we're already restructuring around the new CLI and CSV schema — better to land the cleaner key generation before we collect another round of headline results.

Want me to implement? The change would touch each benchmark binary's setup section (replace the `fill_uniform_keys<<<...>>>` launch with a host-side generator + `cudaMemcpyAsync`). Roughly 10 lines per binary, plus removing `fill_uniform_keys` from `benchmarks.cuh`.

## User — 2026-05-19T03:25:07.343Z

Not only that, but now we can also use one seed per experiment. Generate N uniform keys from the CPU stream on the host for insert, then generate N more keys for get - no shared draws, and everything parameterized cleanly by one seed, allowing us to average over seeds (and GPU-specific randomness) together for a cleaner setup

Note that it's really important that only the hashing-part, after the memcopy is done, is part of our timings, that way the comparison is fair and focuses on what the hash table is doing

## User — 2026-05-19T03:27:25.944Z

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user is iteratively refining a GPU Robin Hood hash table (gpurhh) benchmark suite. The overarching goal is rigorous, apples-to-apples performance comparison between gpurhh, cuCollections, and WarpCore. Recent explicit requests in chronological order:
   
   - Switch gpurhh comparison runs to `replace_op` (drop `sum_op` for the comparison benchmarks)
   - Split benchmarks into two versions: comparison (apples-to-apples) and counter-instrumented (diagnostic). Rename `_diagnostic` → `_counters` throughout.
   - Drop the `MaxProbeBuckets=8` cap for comparison runs since baselines (cuco, warpcore) are effectively uncapped
   - Equalize occupancy definitions across libraries; report effective occupancy as a derived metric
   - Simplify the CLI parameterization: replace `{--capacity-bytes, --load-factor}` with the three raw integer quantities `{--capacity (slots), --key-range, --n-ops}`. All in the same units (slot counts).
   - Simplify the CSV schema: record only raw experiment data (config + measurements), derive everything else in an analysis script downstream
   - Rename the CSV writer class
   - For seeds: reps measure timing noise vs seeds measure input variance — keep separate currently
   - User's MOST RECENT requests (not yet implemented):
     - Generate true iid Uniform(0,N) keys on CPU via std::mt19937, memcpy to device
     - Use a SINGLE seed per experiment — generate N draws for insert, then N MORE consecutive draws for get (disjoint sequential draws from the same generator). One seed parameterizes both streams cleanly.
     - **CRITICAL constraint:** "It's really important that only the hashing-part, after the memcopy is done, is part of our timings, that way the comparison is fair and focuses on what the hash table is doing"
     - This will allow averaging over seeds AND GPU-specific randomness together

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Robin Hood hash table with bucketed cooperative-tile probing
   - Cooperative groups: `cg::tiled_partition<tile_size>` (gpurhh: tile_size=16, warpcore: cg_size=8)
   - Probe cap (`MaxProbeBuckets` template param): 8 for counters, 1<<20 (uncapped) for comparison
   - Reduction operators: `replace_op` (comparison), `sum_op` (counters)
   - cuCollections `static_map`: linear probing, no bucketing, no probe cap
   - WarpCore `SingleValueHashTable`: double hashing with cg_size=8, default probing_length ~2^64
   - `GPURHH_BENCHMARK_COUNTERS` macro: gates `std::uint32_t& tile_counter` parameter on View::insert/get
   - Per-tile register accumulator pattern: lane 0 increments register, single atomic at end of kernel
   - CSV schema simplification: raw data only, derive in analysis script
   - Parameterization: capacity (slots), key_range, n_ops as raw integer quantities
   - Effective occupancy = α × (1 − exp(−F/α)) where F = n_ops/capacity, α = key_range/capacity
   - Key generation: currently `fmix32(seed + tid) % range` (hypergeometric, not iid uniform)
   - User wants: CPU-side `std::mt19937` + cudaMemcpyAsync for true iid Uniform
   - Single-seed scheme: generate 2*n_ops draws sequentially, first N for insert, next N for get

   - External libs vendored as just include subtree under `external/include/cuco/` and `external/include/warpcore/`

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **CUDA narrowing warning** with `std::size_t{grid_size}`: fixed with `static_cast<std::uint32_t>(grid_size)` since `grid_size` is `int` (signed) and `size_t` is unsigned
   - **`std::size_t` brace init failing**: user wanted brace-init's strictness for catching real bugs but `static_cast` for known-safe sign conversions
   - **__device__ lambda return type error** in cuco binaries: required explicit `-> cuco::pair<Key, Value>` trailing return type for thrust::make_transform_iterator
   - **warpcore missing `helpers/cuda_helpers.cuh`**: warpcore depends on hpc_helpers, added to setup-baselines.sh from `https://gitlab.rlp.net/pararch/hpc_helpers.git` commit `232b928cd69bc29ad3d9c65d661bd1073cf4b779`
   - **warpcore `Table::empty_value()` doesn't exist**: removed hit/miss counting via empty_value sentinel; leave hits/misses columns empty for warpcore get
   - **F=3 prefill failures**: changed from abort to warning+continue (lossy storage is fine)
   - **Seed overlap bug discovered**: insert_seed=42, get_seed=1729 with n_ops > 1687 caused overlapping fmix32 input ranges → most "misses" were guaranteed hits, inflating hit rate to 99.8% instead of theoretical ~86.5% at F=2. User's single-seed-with-offset proposal will fix this.
   - **Multiple naming iterations**: `_per_thread`/`_per_tile` suffixes ultimately dropped; `do_*` → `run_*` for host helpers; `CSVWriter` → `Recorder`; `_diagnostic` → `_counters`
   - **Make target removal**: dropped `make benchmark` and `make benchmark-with-baselines`; benchmarks run via `bash scripts/benchmark.sh` directly

5. Problem Solving:
   - Designed apples-to-apples comparison framework distinguishing gpurhh's "design under study" (sum_op, cap-8) from "comparable to baselines" (replace_op, uncapped)
   - Established three raw parameters {capacity, key_range, n_ops} as the minimal complete description of workload, with all derived metrics computed downstream
   - Identified that GPU tail latency is structurally less concerning than CPU due to multiple averaging layers (op→tile→block→stream→kernel)
   - Recognized that cooperative-tile probing already absorbs within-warp tail variance, partially overlapping with Robin Hood's variance-reduction benefit
   - Honest comparison data showed cuco wins ~32% on raw ops/sec at moderate load factor; gpurhh is the only one that runs past F=0.95 (cuco static_map is fixed-capacity)
   - Identified key-generation method (`fmix32(seed+i)%range`) is hypergeometric rather than iid Uniform (variance correction ~9% at F=3)

```
3	// Shared benchmark infrastructure: CUDA error checking, event-based

11	// binaries omit the define and get the counter-free View::insert /

27	// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
28	// CUDA_CHECK: postfix error-check operator. Identical in shape to the macro
29	// in <tests/tests.cuh>; copy-pasted rather than shared because the benchmark
30	// suite intentionally does not depend on test infrastructure.
31	//
32	//     cudaMalloc(&p, n) >> CUDA_CHECK;
33	// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
34	
35	struct CudaCheckLoc {
36	    const char* file;
37	    int         line;
38	};
39	
40	inline void operator>>(cudaError_t e, CudaCheckLoc loc) {
41	    if (e != cudaSuccess) {
42	        std::fprintf(stderr, "CUDA error at %s:%d: %s\n",
43	                     loc.file, loc.line, cudaGetErrorString(e));
44	        std::exit(1);
45	    }
46	}
47	
48	#define CUDA_CHECK CudaCheckLoc{__FILE__, __LINE__}
49	
50	// CUDA event timer. begin() records the start event; end_ms() records
51	// the stop event, synchronizes on it, and returns elapsed milliseconds.
52	class EventTimer {
53	public:
54	    EventTimer() {
55	        cudaEventCreate(&start_) >> CUDA_CHECK;
56	        cudaEventCreate(&stop_)  >> CUDA_CHECK;
57	    }
58	    ~EventTimer() {
59	        cudaEventDestroy(start_);
60	        cudaEventDestroy(stop_);
61	    }
62	    EventTimer(const EventTimer&)            = delete;
63	    EventTimer& operator=(const EventTimer&) = delete;
64	
65	    void begin() { cudaEventRecord(start_) >> CUDA_CHECK; }
66	    float end_ms() {
67	        cudaEventRecord(stop_) >> CUDA_CHECK;
68	        cudaEventSynchronize(stop_) >> CUDA_CHECK;
69	        float ms;
70	        cudaEventElapsedTime(&ms, start_, stop_) >> CUDA_CHECK;
71	        return ms;
72	    }
73	
74	private:
75	    cudaEvent_t start_{};
76	    cudaEvent_t stop_{};
77	};
78	
79	// Recorder: appends rows to a CSV file in the output directory, and
80	// also echoes every row (plus the header) to stdout for live visibility.
81	//
82	// If the target file is new or empty when constructed, the header line
83	// is written to the file first; if not, the file is appended to without
84	// a fresh header (so a sweep over many invocations builds one CSV).
85	class Recorder {
86	public:
87	    Recorder(const std::filesystem::path& path, std::string header)
88	        : header_(std::move(header))
89	    {
90	        if (!path.parent_path().empty()) {
91	            std::filesystem::create_directories(path.parent_path());
92	        }
93	        const bool needs_header = !std::filesystem::exists(path)
94	                                || std::filesystem::file_size(path) == 0;
95	        file_.open(path, std::ios::app);
96	        if (!file_) {
97	            std::fprintf(stderr, "Failed to open %s for writing\n",
98	                         path.string().c_str());
99	            std::exit(1);
100	        }
101	        if (needs_header) {
102	            file_ << header_ << "\n";
103	            file_.flush();
104	        }
105	        // Echo header once per invocation, regardless of file state, so
106	        // stdout is self-documenting even when appending to an existing
107	        // CSV from the sweep driver.
108	        std::cout << header_ << "\n";
109	    }
110	
111	    void write_row(const std::string& row) {
112	        file_ << row << "\n";
113	        file_.flush();
114	        std::cout << row << "\n";
115	        std::cout.flush();
116	    }
117	
118	private:
119	    std::string   header_;
120	    std::ofstream file_;
121	};
122	
123	// Fill out[0..n) with fmix32(seed + i) mod range. For `range` a power
124	// of two (the case here, since key range = α × capacity with capacity
125	// a power of two), the modulus yields a uniform distribution.
126	//
127	// Not marked inline: __global__ functions cannot be inline, but each
128	// benchmark binary is a single translation unit that includes this
129	// header exactly once, so there is no multiple-definition concern.
130	__global__ void fill_uniform_keys(
131	    std::uint32_t* out, std::size_t n,
132	    std::uint32_t seed, std::uint32_t range)
133	{
134	    const std::size_t tid = blockIdx.x * std::size_t{blockDim.x} + threadIdx.x;
135	    if (tid >= n) return;
136	    out[tid] = gpurhh::detail::fmix32(static_cast<std::uint32_t>(tid) + seed) % range;
137	}
138	
139	// Launch-shape sizing: queries the current device for SM count, derives a
140	// grid size proportional to it (8 blocks per SM keeps SMs occupied without
141	// inflating the grid past the point of useful concurrency), and returns
142	// the per-block and total tile counts that the benchmark kernels need.
143	struct LaunchShape {
144	    int         grid_size;
145	    int         tiles_per_block;
146	    std::size_t n_tiles;
147	};
148	
149	inline LaunchShape compute_launch_shape(int block_size, int tile_size) {
150	    int device  = 0;
151	    int num_sms = 0;
152	    cudaGetDevice(&device) >> CUDA_CHECK;
153	    cudaDeviceGetAttribute(&num_sms, cudaDevAttrMultiProcessorCount, device)
154	        >> CUDA_CHECK;
155	
156	    const int tiles_per_block = block_size / tile_size;
157	    const int grid_size       = num_sms * 8;
158	    const std::size_t n_tiles =
159	        static_cast<std::size_t>(grid_size)
160	      * static_cast<std::size_t>(tiles_per_block);
161	    return {grid_size, tiles_per_block, n_tiles};
162	}
163	
164	// Boilerplate runner for the warmup-then-timed-reps pattern shared by
165	// every benchmark. The caller passes three callables:
166	//
167	//   - setup    — invoked before every iteration (both warmups and timed
168	//                reps). Use it to zero per-rep counters or clear the
169	//                table between insert reps. Defaults to a no-op if the
170	//                benchmark has nothing to reset.
171	//   - launch   — issues the kernel (or memcpy) being timed.
172	//   - after    — invoked after each timed rep with the rep index and
173	//                elapsed milliseconds. Use it to read back counters and
174	//                emit a CSV row.
175	template <class SetupFn, class LaunchFn, class AfterFn>
176	inline void run_benchmark_loop(
177	    int warmups, int reps, EventTimer& timer,
178	    SetupFn&& setup, LaunchFn&& launch, AfterFn&& after)
179	{
180	    for (int w = 0; w < warmups; ++w) {
181	        setup();
182	        launch();
183	    }
184	    cudaDeviceSynchronize() >> CUDA_CHECK;
185	
186	    for (int r = 0; r < reps; ++r) {
187	        setup();
188	        timer.begin();
189	        launch();
190	        const float ms = timer.end_ms();
191	        after(r, ms);
192	    }
193	}
194	
```

</details>

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T03:27:53.892Z

No — the C++ standard library hasn't added any new engines since C++11. The full set in `<random>` is still:

- `std::mersenne_twister_engine` (`mt19937` / `mt19937_64`) — 1997, ~2.5 KB state, fine quality but slow to seed and not cache-friendly
- `std::linear_congruential_engine` (`minstd_rand`) — much older, low quality
- `std::subtract_with_carry_engine` / `ranlux*` — also pre-`mt19937` era

So if we want something modern, we hand-roll it. The good news is that the modern small-state PRNGs are *tiny* — a few lines each:

- **splitmix64** — ~5 lines, single `uint64_t` state. Great for seeding other PRNGs; statistically fine for our use.
- **xoshiro256++ / xoroshiro128++** — 256-bit / 128-bit state, ~10–15 lines, faster than mt19937 by ~3–5×, passes BigCrush.
- **PCG** (e.g. `pcg32`) — also small, well-tested, but the reference impl is a separate header.

For our benchmark (generating `2·n_ops` uint32s on the host, `n_ops` up to ~4·2^27 ≈ 5·10^8), `mt19937` would burn ~5–10 s of host time per invocation; xoshiro256++ would do it in ~1–2 s. That's a real difference across a long sweep.

My suggestion: drop in xoshiro256++ as a 15-line helper in `benchmarks.cuh`. Seed it via splitmix64 from the user's `--seed`. We get reproducibility, decent speed, and no new dependencies. Want me to do that, or stick with `mt19937` for stdlib-only purity?

**← Tool result** (for `tool-655`)

<details><summary><code>tool result</code></summary>

```
1	// Insert benchmark for gpurhh — comparison version.
2	//
3	// Apples-to-apples with the cuco / warpcore baselines:
4	//   - Reduction operator: replace_op (no extra work on duplicate keys).
5	//   - MaxProbeBuckets: 1 << 20 (effectively uncapped, matching the
6	//     baselines' unbounded probe semantics).
7	//   - No counter instrumentation in the hot path.
8	//
9	// Workload:
10	//   - Key type uint32_t, value type uint32_t.
11	//   - Keys drawn from Uniform(0, key_range) at runtime.
12	//   - Single ~1 GiB table by default, DRAM-resident.
13	//
14	// Per timed sample:
15	//   1. Empty the table via table.clear() (untimed).
16	//   2. Time one kernel that processes n_ops insert ops in a grid-stride loop.
17	//   3. Emit a CSV row recording the raw configuration + measured time.
18	//
19	// All derived metrics (ops_per_sec, gbps_floor, load_factor, occupancy,
20	// hit_rate, ...) are computed in the analysis script from the raw
21	// CSV columns, not in this binary.
22	
23	#include "benchmarks.cuh"
24	
25	#include <cooperative_groups.h>
26	#include <cuda_runtime.h>
27	
28	#include <cstddef>
29	#include <cstdint>
30	#include <cstdio>
31	#include <cstdlib>
32	#include <filesystem>
33	#include <sstream>
34	#include <string>
35	
36	namespace cg = cooperative_groups;
37	
38	using Table = gpurhh::HashTable<
39	    std::uint32_t,
40	    std::uint32_t,
41	    gpurhh::default_hash<std::uint32_t>,
42	    gpurhh::default_empty_key<std::uint32_t>::key,
43	    gpurhh::replace_op,
44	    /*CacheLineBytes=*/128,
45	    /*WarpSize=*/32,
46	    /*MaxProbeBuckets=*/(1 << 20)>;
47	using View = Table::View;
48	
49	namespace {
50	
51	struct Args {
52	    std::size_t   capacity   = std::size_t{1} << 27;  // 1 GiB at 8 B/slot
53	    std::size_t   key_range  = std::size_t{1} << 27;
54	    std::size_t   n_ops      = std::size_t{1} << 27;
55	    int           block_size = 256;
56	    int           warmups    = 2;
57	    int           reps       = 16;
58	    std::uint32_t seed       = 42;
59	    std::string   tag        = "";
60	    std::filesystem::path output_dir;
61	};
62	
63	[[noreturn]] void die_usage(const char* prog) {
64	    std::fprintf(stderr,
65	        "usage: %s --output-dir DIR [options]\n"
66	        "  --capacity N       Table size in slots (default 1<<27; rounded up to next pow2).\n"
67	        "  --key-range N      N in Uniform(0, N) used for key generation (default 1<<27).\n"
68	        "  --n-ops N          Number of insert attempts (default 1<<27).\n"
69	        "  --block-size N     Threads per CUDA block; multiple of tile_size (default 256).\n"
70	        "  --warmups N        Untimed warmup reps (default 2).\n"
71	        "  --reps N           Timed reps (default 16).\n"
72	        "  --seed N           PRNG seed for key generation (default 42).\n"
73	        "  --tag STR          Free-form label written to every CSV row.\n"
74	        "  --output-dir DIR   Required. insert.csv is appended to here.\n",
75	        prog);
76	    std::exit(1);
77	}
78	
79	Args parse_args(int argc, char** argv) {
80	    Args a;
81	    for (int i = 1; i < argc; ++i) {
82	        std::string flag = argv[i];
83	        auto get_val = [&]() -> std::string {
84	            if (i + 1 >= argc) {
85	                std::fprintf(stderr, "Missing value for %s\n", flag.c_str());
86	                die_usage(argv[0]);
87	            }
88	            return argv[++i];
89	        };
90	        if      (flag == "--capacity")   a.capacity   = std::stoull(get_val());
91	        else if (flag == "--key-range")  a.key_range  = std::stoull(get_val());
92	        else if (flag == "--n-ops")      a.n_ops      = std::stoull(get_val());
93	        else if (flag == "--block-size") a.block_size = std::stoi(get_val());
94	        else if (flag == "--warmups")    a.warmups    = std::stoi(get_val());
95	        else if (flag == "--reps")       a.reps       = std::stoi(get_val());
96	        else if (flag == "--seed")       a.seed       = static_cast<std::uint32_t>(std::stoul(get_val()));
97	        else if (flag == "--tag")        a.tag        = get_val();
98	        else if (flag == "--output-dir") a.output_dir = get_val();
99	        else { std::fprintf(stderr, "Unknown flag: %s\n", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }
100	    }
101	    if (a.output_dir.empty())                     die_usage(argv[0]);
102	    if (a.n_ops == 0)                             { std::fprintf(stderr, "--n-ops must be > 0\n");     std::exit(1); }
103	    if (a.key_range == 0)                         { std::fprintf(stderr, "--key-range must be > 0\n"); std::exit(1); }
104	    if (a.block_size % Table::tile_size != 0)     { std::fprintf(stderr, "--block-size must be a multiple of tile_size (%d)\n", Table::tile_size); std::exit(1); }
105	    if (a.block_size <= 0 || a.block_size > 1024) { std::fprintf(stderr, "--block-size out of range\n"); std::exit(1); }
106	    return a;
107	}
108	
109	__global__ void insert(
110	    View view,
111	    const std::uint32_t* __restrict__ keys,
112	    std::size_t n_ops)
113	{
114	    auto block = cg::this_thread_block();
115	    auto tile  = cg::tiled_partition<Table::tile_size>(block);
116	
117	    const std::size_t tiles_per_block = blockDim.x / Table::tile_size;
118	    const std::size_t tile_id =
119	        blockIdx.x * tiles_per_block + threadIdx.x / Table::tile_size;
120	    const std::size_t total_tiles =
121	        std::size_t{gridDim.x} * tiles_per_block;
122	
123	    for (std::size_t op_id = tile_id; op_id < n_ops; op_id += total_tiles) {
124	        const std::uint32_t k = keys[op_id];
125	        const std::uint32_t v = gpurhh::detail::fmix32(k);
126	        (void) view.insert(tile, k, v);
127	    }
128	}
129	
130	std::string format_row(
131	    std::size_t capacity, std::size_t key_range, std::size_t n_ops,
132	    int block_size, std::size_t slot_bytes, std::size_t bytes_per_op,
133	    std::uint32_t seed, int rep, const std::string& tag,
134	    float time_ms)
135	{
136	    std::ostringstream s;
137	    s.precision(9);
138	    s << "gpurhh,insert,"
139	      << tag           << ","
140	      << rep           << ","
141	      << seed          << ","
142	      << capacity      << ","
143	      << key_range     << ","
144	      << n_ops         << ","
145	      << block_size    << ","
146	      << slot_bytes    << ","
147	      << bytes_per_op  << ","
148	      << time_ms;
149	    return s.str();
150	}
151	
152	} // namespace
153	
154	int main(int argc, char** argv) {
155	    const Args args = parse_args(argc, argv);
156	
157	    constexpr std::size_t slot_bytes   = sizeof(Table::Slot);
158	    constexpr std::size_t bytes_per_op = sizeof(Table::Bucket);
159	
160	    Table table(args.capacity);
161	    const std::size_t capacity = table.capacity();
162	    const std::uint32_t key_range = static_cast<std::uint32_t>(args.key_range);
163	
164	    std::fprintf(stderr,
165	        "[insert] capacity=%zu key_range=%zu n_ops=%zu "
166	        "block_size=%d warmups=%d reps=%d seed=%u tag=\"%s\"\n",
167	        capacity, args.key_range, args.n_ops,
168	        args.block_size, args.warmups, args.reps, args.seed, args.tag.c_str());
169	
170	    // --- input buffer ---
171	    std::uint32_t* d_keys = nullptr;
172	    cudaMalloc(&d_keys, args.n_ops * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
173	    {
174	        constexpr int fill_block = 256;
175	        const int fill_grid =
176	            static_cast<int>((args.n_ops + fill_block - 1) / fill_block);
177	        fill_uniform_keys<<<fill_grid, fill_block>>>(
178	            d_keys, args.n_ops, args.seed, key_range);
179	        cudaGetLastError() >> CUDA_CHECK;
180	    }
181	
182	    const auto shape = compute_launch_shape(args.block_size, Table::tile_size);
183	
184	    Recorder rec(args.output_dir / "insert.csv",
185	        "library,workload,tag,rep,seed,capacity,key_range,n_ops,block_size,"
186	        "slot_bytes,bytes_per_op,time_ms");
187	
188	    EventTimer timer;
189	
190	    run_benchmark_loop(args.warmups, args.reps, timer,
191	        /*setup=*/  [&]() {
192	            table.clear();
193	        },
194	        /*launch=*/ [&]() {
195	            insert<<<shape.grid_size, args.block_size>>>(
196	                table.view(), d_keys, args.n_ops);
197	            cudaGetLastError() >> CUDA_CHECK;
198	        },
199	        /*after=*/  [&](int rep, float ms) {
200	            rec.write_row(format_row(
201	                capacity, args.key_range, args.n_ops,
202	                args.block_size, slot_bytes, bytes_per_op,
203	                args.seed, rep, args.tag, ms));
204	        });
205	
206	    cudaFree(d_keys);
207	    return 0;
208	}
209	
```

</details>

```
1	// Get benchmark for gpurhh — comparison version.
2	//
3	// Mirrors benchmark_insert.cu's apples-to-apples comparison setup:

10	//      keys (untimed).
11	//   2. Generate get keys with an independent seed (also untimed).
12	//   3. Time the bulk get kernel.

73	    for (int i = 1; i < argc; ++i) {

99	// Pre-fill kernel (untimed). Reuses the same insert path the comparison
100	// version uses elsewhere. No counter tracking.
101	__global__ void prefill(
102	    View view,
103	    const std::uint32_t* __restrict__ keys,
104	    std::size_t n_ops)
105	{
106	    auto block = cg::this_thread_block();
107	    auto tile  = cg::tiled_partition<Table::tile_size>(block);
108	
109	    const std::size_t tiles_per_block = blockDim.x / Table::tile_size;
110	    const std::size_t tile_id =
111	        blockIdx.x * tiles_per_block + threadIdx.x / Table::tile_size;
112	    const std::size_t total_tiles =
113	        std::size_t{gridDim.x} * tiles_per_block;
114	
115	    for (std::size_t op_id = tile_id; op_id < n_ops; op_id += total_tiles) {
116	        const std::uint32_t k = keys[op_id];
117	        const std::uint32_t v = gpurhh::detail::fmix32(k);
118	        (void) view.insert(tile, k, v);
119	    }
120	}
121	
122	__global__ void get(
123	    View view,
124	    const std::uint32_t* __restrict__ keys,
125	    std::uint32_t* __restrict__ out,
126	    std::size_t n_ops)
127	{
128	    auto block = cg::this_thread_block();
129	    auto tile  = cg::tiled_partition<Table::tile_size>(block);
130	
131	    const std::size_t tiles_per_block = blockDim.x / Table::tile_size;
132	    const std::size_t tile_id =
133	        blockIdx.x * tiles_per_block + threadIdx.x / Table::tile_size;
134	    const std::size_t total_tiles =
135	        std::size_t{gridDim.x} * tiles_per_block;
136	
137	    for (std::size_t op_id = tile_id; op_id < n_ops; op_id += total_tiles) {
138	        const std::uint32_t k = keys[op_id];
139	        const auto result = view.get(tile, k);
140	        if (tile.thread_rank() == 0) {
141	            out[op_id] = result.value_or(~std::uint32_t{0});
142	        }
143	    }
144	}
145	
146	std::string format_row(
147	    std::size_t capacity, std::size_t key_range, std::size_t n_ops,
148	    int block_size, std::size_t slot_bytes, std::size_t bytes_per_op,
149	    std::uint32_t insert_seed, std::uint32_t get_seed,
150	    int rep, const std::string& tag,
151	    float time_ms)
152	{
153	    std::ostringstream s;
154	    s.precision(9);
155	    s << "gpurhh,get,"
156	      << tag           << ","
157	      << rep           << ","
158	      << insert_seed   << ","
159	      << get_seed      << ","
160	      << capacity      << ","
161	      << key_range     << ","
162	      << n_ops         << ","
163	      << block_size    << ","
164	      << slot_bytes    << ","
165	      << bytes_per_op  << ","
166	      << time_ms;
167	    return s.str();
168	}
169	
170	} // namespace
171	
172	int main(int argc, char** argv) {
173	    const Args args = parse_args(argc, argv);
174	
175	    constexpr std::size_t slot_bytes   = sizeof(Table::Slot);
176	    constexpr std::size_t bytes_per_op = sizeof(Table::Bucket);
177	
178	    Table table(args.capacity);
179	    const std::size_t capacity = table.capacity();
180	    const std::uint32_t key_range = static_cast<std::uint32_t>(args.key_range);
181	
182	    std::fprintf(stderr,
183	        "[get] capacity=%zu key_range=%zu n_ops=%zu "
184	        "block_size=%d warmups=%d reps=%d insert_seed=%u get_seed=%u tag=\"%s\"\n",
185	        capacity, args.key_range, args.n_ops,
186	        args.block_size, args.warmups, args.reps,
187	        args.insert_seed, args.get_seed, args.tag.c_str());
188	
189	    // --- pre-fill (untimed) ---
190	    std::uint32_t* d_insert_keys = nullptr;
191	    cudaMalloc(&d_insert_keys, args.n_ops * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
192	    {
193	        constexpr int fill_block = 256;
194	        const int fill_grid =
195	            static_cast<int>((args.n_ops + fill_block - 1) / fill_block);
196	        fill_uniform_keys<<<fill_grid, fill_block>>>(
197	            d_insert_keys, args.n_ops, args.insert_seed, key_range);
198	        cudaGetLastError() >> CUDA_CHECK;
199	    }
200	
201	    const auto shape = compute_launch_shape(args.block_size, Table::tile_size);
202	
203	    prefill<<<shape.grid_size, args.block_size>>>(
204	        table.view(), d_insert_keys, args.n_ops);
205	    cudaGetLastError()      >> CUDA_CHECK;
206	    cudaDeviceSynchronize() >> CUDA_CHECK;
207	    cudaFree(d_insert_keys);
208	
209	    // --- get key buffer ---
210	    std::uint32_t* d_get_keys = nullptr;
211	    cudaMalloc(&d_get_keys, args.n_ops * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
212	    {
213	        constexpr int fill_block = 256;
214	        const int fill_grid =
215	            static_cast<int>((args.n_ops + fill_block - 1) / fill_block);
216	        fill_uniform_keys<<<fill_grid, fill_block>>>(
217	            d_get_keys, args.n_ops, args.get_seed, key_range);
218	        cudaGetLastError() >> CUDA_CHECK;
219	    }
220	
221	    std::uint32_t* d_out = nullptr;
222	    cudaMalloc(&d_out, args.n_ops * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
223	
224	    Recorder rec(args.output_dir / "get.csv",
225	        "library,workload,tag,rep,insert_seed,get_seed,capacity,key_range,"
226	        "n_ops,block_size,slot_bytes,bytes_per_op,time_ms");
227	
228	    EventTimer timer;
229	
230	    run_benchmark_loop(args.warmups, args.reps, timer,
231	        /*setup=*/  []() {},
232	        /*launch=*/ [&]() {
233	            get<<<shape.grid_size, args.block_size>>>(
234	                table.view(), d_get_keys, d_out, args.n_ops);
235	            cudaGetLastError() >> CUDA_CHECK;
236	        },
237	        /*after=*/  [&](int rep, float ms) {
238	            rec.write_row(format_row(
239	                capacity, args.key_range, args.n_ops,
240	                args.block_size, slot_bytes, bytes_per_op,
241	                args.insert_seed, args.get_seed, rep, args.tag, ms));
242	        });
243	
244	    cudaFree(d_get_keys);
245	    cudaFree(d_out);
246	    return 0;
247	}
```

```
1	// Insert benchmark for gpurhh — counter-instrumented version.
2	//
3	// Studies gpurhh's design in its full configuration:
4	//   - Reduction operator: sum_op (exercise the reduction path).
5	//   - MaxProbeBuckets: 8 (the default — study the probe-cap regime).
6	//   - Per-tile probe / failure counters via GPURHH_BENCHMARK_COUNTERS.
7	//
8	// Writes to insert_counters.csv (separate from the apples-to-apples
9	// insert.csv that benchmark_insert.cu produces). The extra counter
10	// columns (total_probes, total_failures) live only here.
11	
12	#define GPURHH_BENCHMARK_COUNTERS 1
13	
14	#include "benchmarks.cuh"
15	
16	#include <cooperative_groups.h>
17	#include <cuda_runtime.h>
18	
19	#include <cstddef>
20	#include <cstdint>
21	#include <cstdio>
22	#include <cstdlib>
23	#include <filesystem>
24	#include <numeric>
25	#include <sstream>
26	#include <string>
27	#include <vector>
28	
29	namespace cg = cooperative_groups;
30	
31	using Table = gpurhh::HashTable<
32	    std::uint32_t,
33	    std::uint32_t,
34	    gpurhh::default_hash<std::uint32_t>,
35	    gpurhh::default_empty_key<std::uint32_t>::key,
36	    gpurhh::sum_op>;
37	using View = Table::View;
38	
39	namespace {
40	
41	struct Args {
42	    std::size_t   capacity   = std::size_t{1} << 27;
43	    std::size_t   key_range  = std::size_t{1} << 27;
44	    std::size_t   n_ops      = std::size_t{1} << 27;
45	    int           block_size = 256;
46	    int           warmups    = 2;
47	    int           reps       = 16;
48	    std::uint32_t seed       = 42;
49	    std::string   tag        = "";
50	    std::filesystem::path output_dir;
51	};
52	
53	[[noreturn]] void die_usage(const char* prog) {
54	    std::fprintf(stderr,
55	        "usage: %s --output-dir DIR [options]\n"
56	        "  Same shape as benchmark_insert. Writes insert_counters.csv with\n"
57	        "  per-rep total_probes and total_failures alongside time_ms.\n",
58	        prog);
59	    std::exit(1);
60	}
61	
62	Args parse_args(int argc, char** argv) {
63	    Args a;
64	    for (int i = 1; i < argc; ++i) {
65	        std::string flag = argv[i];
66	        auto get_val = [&]() -> std::string {
67	            if (i + 1 >= argc) { std::fprintf(stderr, "Missing value for %s\n", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }
68	            return argv[++i];
69	        };
70	        if      (flag == "--capacity")   a.capacity   = std::stoull(get_val());
71	        else if (flag == "--key-range")  a.key_range  = std::stoull(get_val());
72	        else if (flag == "--n-ops")      a.n_ops      = std::stoull(get_val());
73	        else if (flag == "--block-size") a.block_size = std::stoi(get_val());
74	        else if (flag == "--warmups")    a.warmups    = std::stoi(get_val());
75	        else if (flag == "--reps")       a.reps       = std::stoi(get_val());
76	        else if (flag == "--seed")       a.seed       = static_cast<std::uint32_t>(std::stoul(get_val()));
77	        else if (flag == "--tag")        a.tag        = get_val();
78	        else if (flag == "--output-dir") a.output_dir = get_val();
79	        else { std::fprintf(stderr, "Unknown flag: %s\n", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }
80	    }
81	    if (a.output_dir.empty())                     die_usage(argv[0]);
82	    if (a.n_ops == 0)                             { std::fprintf(stderr, "--n-ops must be > 0\n");     std::exit(1); }
83	    if (a.key_range == 0)                         { std::fprintf(stderr, "--key-range must be > 0\n"); std::exit(1); }
84	    if (a.block_size % Table::tile_size != 0)     { std::fprintf(stderr, "--block-size must be a multiple of tile_size (%d)\n", Table::tile_size); std::exit(1); }
85	    if (a.block_size <= 0 || a.block_size > 1024) { std::fprintf(stderr, "--block-size out of range\n"); std::exit(1); }
86	    return a;
87	}
88	
89	__global__ void insert(
90	    View view,
91	    const std::uint32_t* __restrict__ keys,
92	    std::size_t n_ops,
93	    std::uint32_t* __restrict__ per_tile_probes,
94	    std::uint32_t* __restrict__ per_tile_failures)
95	{
96	    auto block = cg::this_thread_block();
97	    auto tile  = cg::tiled_partition<Table::tile_size>(block);
98	
99	    const std::size_t tiles_per_block = blockDim.x / Table::tile_size;
100	    const std::size_t tile_id =
101	        blockIdx.x * tiles_per_block + threadIdx.x / Table::tile_size;
102	    const std::size_t total_tiles =
103	        std::size_t{gridDim.x} * tiles_per_block;
104	
105	    std::uint32_t my_probes   = 0;
106	    std::uint32_t my_failures = 0;
107	
108	    for (std::size_t op_id = tile_id; op_id < n_ops; op_id += total_tiles) {
109	        const std::uint32_t k = keys[op_id];
110	        const std::uint32_t v = gpurhh::detail::fmix32(k);
111	        const auto leftover = view.insert(tile, k, v, my_probes);
112	        if (leftover.has_value() && tile.thread_rank() == 0) {
113	            ++my_failures;
114	        }
115	    }
116	
117	    if (tile.thread_rank() == 0) {
118	        per_tile_probes  [tile_id] = my_probes;
119	        per_tile_failures[tile_id] = my_failures;
120	    }
121	}
122	
123	std::string format_row(
124	    std::size_t capacity, std::size_t key_range, std::size_t n_ops,
125	    int block_size, std::size_t slot_bytes, std::size_t bytes_per_op,
126	    std::uint32_t seed, int rep, const std::string& tag,
127	    float time_ms, std::uint64_t total_probes, std::uint64_t total_failures)
128	{
129	    std::ostringstream s;
130	    s.precision(9);
131	    s << "gpurhh,insert,"
132	      << tag           << ","
133	      << rep           << ","
134	      << seed          << ","
135	      << capacity      << ","
136	      << key_range     << ","
137	      << n_ops         << ","
138	      << block_size    << ","
139	      << slot_bytes    << ","
140	      << bytes_per_op  << ","
141	      << time_ms       << ","
142	      << total_probes  << ","
143	      << total_failures;
144	    return s.str();
145	}
146	
147	} // namespace
148	
149	int main(int argc, char** argv) {
150	    const Args args = parse_args(argc, argv);
151	
152	    constexpr std::size_t slot_bytes   = sizeof(Table::Slot);
153	    constexpr std::size_t bytes_per_op = sizeof(Table::Bucket);
154	
155	    Table table(args.capacity);
156	    const std::size_t capacity = table.capacity();
157	    const std::uint32_t key_range = static_cast<std::uint32_t>(args.key_range);
158	
159	    std::fprintf(stderr,
160	        "[insert-counters] capacity=%zu key_range=%zu n_ops=%zu "
161	        "block_size=%d warmups=%d reps=%d seed=%u tag=\"%s\"\n",
162	        capacity, args.key_range, args.n_ops,
163	        args.block_size, args.warmups, args.reps, args.seed, args.tag.c_str());
164	
165	    std::uint32_t* d_keys = nullptr;
166	    cudaMalloc(&d_keys, args.n_ops * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
167	    {
168	        constexpr int fill_block = 256;
169	        const int fill_grid =
170	            static_cast<int>((args.n_ops + fill_block - 1) / fill_block);
171	        fill_uniform_keys<<<fill_grid, fill_block>>>(
172	            d_keys, args.n_ops, args.seed, key_range);
173	        cudaGetLastError() >> CUDA_CHECK;
174	    }
175	
176	    const auto shape = compute_launch_shape(args.block_size, Table::tile_size);
177	    const std::size_t n_tiles = shape.n_tiles;
178	
179	    std::uint32_t* d_probes   = nullptr;
180	    std::uint32_t* d_failures = nullptr;
181	    cudaMalloc(&d_probes,   n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
182	    cudaMalloc(&d_failures, n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
183	    std::vector<std::uint32_t> h_probes  (n_tiles);
184	    std::vector<std::uint32_t> h_failures(n_tiles);
185	
186	    Recorder rec(args.output_dir / "insert_counters.csv",
187	        "library,workload,tag,rep,seed,capacity,key_range,n_ops,block_size,"
188	        "slot_bytes,bytes_per_op,time_ms,total_probes,total_failures");
189	
190	    EventTimer timer;
191	
192	    run_benchmark_loop(args.warmups, args.reps, timer,
193	        /*setup=*/  [&]() {
194	            table.clear();
195	            cudaMemset(d_probes,   0, n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
196	            cudaMemset(d_failures, 0, n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
197	        },
198	        /*launch=*/ [&]() {
199	            insert<<<shape.grid_size, args.block_size>>>(
200	                table.view(), d_keys, args.n_ops, d_probes, d_failures);
201	            cudaGetLastError() >> CUDA_CHECK;
202	        },
203	        /*after=*/  [&](int rep, float ms) {
204	            cudaMemcpy(h_probes.data(),   d_probes,
205	                       n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t),
206	                       cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost) >> CUDA_CHECK;
207	            cudaMemcpy(h_failures.data(), d_failures,
208	                       n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t),
209	                       cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost) >> CUDA_CHECK;
210	            const std::uint64_t total_probes =
211	                std::accumulate(h_probes.begin(), h_probes.end(), std::uint64_t{0});
212	            const std::uint64_t total_failures =
213	                std::accumulate(h_failures.begin(), h_failures.end(), std::uint64_t{0});
214	            rec.write_row(format_row(
215	                capacity, args.key_range, args.n_ops,
216	                args.block_size, slot_bytes, bytes_per_op,
217	                args.seed, rep, args.tag,
218	                ms, total_probes, total_failures));
219	        });
220	
221	    cudaFree(d_keys);
222	    cudaFree(d_probes);
223	    cudaFree(d_failures);
224	    return 0;
225	}
226	
```

</details>

```
1	// Get benchmark for gpurhh — counter-instrumented version.
2	//
3	// Same shape as benchmark_get.cu, but with:
4	//   - sum_op (to exercise the reduction path during pre-fill).
5	//   - Default MaxProbeBuckets (= 8, study probe-cap regime).
6	//   - Per-tile probe / hit counters via GPURHH_BENCHMARK_COUNTERS.
7	//
8	// Writes to get_counters.csv with extra total_probes / total_hits /
9	// total_misses columns; misses = n_ops - hits.
10	
11	#define GPURHH_BENCHMARK_COUNTERS 1
12

64	        std::string flag = argv[i];
65	        auto get_val = [&]() -> std::string {
66	            if (i + 1 >= argc) { std::fprintf(stderr, "Missing value for %s\n", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }
67	            return argv[++i];

83	    if (a.key_range == 0)                         { std::fprintf(stderr, "--key-range must be > 0\n"); std::exit(1); }
84	    if (a.block_size % Table::tile_size != 0)     { std::fprintf(stderr, "--block-size must be a multiple of tile_size (%d)\n", Table::tile_size); std::exit(1); }
85	    if (a.block_size <= 0 || a.block_size > 1024) { std::fprintf(stderr, "--block-size out of range\n"); std::exit(1); }
86	    return a;

88	
89	__global__ void prefill(
90	    View view,
91	    const std::uint32_t* __restrict__ keys,
92	    std::size_t n_ops)
93	{
94	    auto block = cg::this_thread_block();
95	    auto tile  = cg::tiled_partition<Table::tile_size>(block);
96	
97	    const std::size_t tiles_per_block = blockDim.x / Table::tile_size;
98	    const std::size_t tile_id =
99	        blockIdx.x * tiles_per_block + threadIdx.x / Table::tile_size;
100	    const std::size_t total_tiles =
101	        std::size_t{gridDim.x} * tiles_per_block;
102	
103	    std::uint32_t scratch_probes = 0;  // discarded; only counts during prefill
104	
105	    for (std::size_t op_id = tile_id; op_id < n_ops; op_id += total_tiles) {
106	        const std::uint32_t k = keys[op_id];
107	        const std::uint32_t v = gpurhh::detail::fmix32(k);
108	        (void) view.insert(tile, k, v, scratch_probes);
109	    }
110	}
111	
112	__global__ void get(
113	    View view,
114	    const std::uint32_t* __restrict__ keys,
115	    std::uint32_t* __restrict__ out,
116	    std::size_t n_ops,
117	    std::uint32_t* __restrict__ per_tile_probes,
118	    std::uint32_t* __restrict__ per_tile_hits)
119	{
120	    auto block = cg::this_thread_block();
121	    auto tile  = cg::tiled_partition<Table::tile_size>(block);
122	
123	    const std::size_t tiles_per_block = blockDim.x / Table::tile_size;
124	    const std::size_t tile_id =
125	        blockIdx.x * tiles_per_block + threadIdx.x / Table::tile_size;
126	    const std::size_t total_tiles =
127	        std::size_t{gridDim.x} * tiles_per_block;
128	
129	    std::uint32_t my_probes = 0;
130	    std::uint32_t my_hits   = 0;
131	
132	    for (std::size_t op_id = tile_id; op_id < n_ops; op_id += total_tiles) {
133	        const std::uint32_t k = keys[op_id];
134	        const auto result = view.get(tile, k, my_probes);
135	        if (tile.thread_rank() == 0) {
136	            out[op_id] = result.value_or(~std::uint32_t{0});
137	            if (result.has_value()) ++my_hits;
138	        }
139	    }
140	
141	    if (tile.thread_rank() == 0) {
142	        per_tile_probes[tile_id] = my_probes;
143	        per_tile_hits  [tile_id] = my_hits;
144	    }
145	}
146	
147	std::string format_row(
148	    std::size_t capacity, std::size_t key_range, std::size_t n_ops,
149	    int block_size, std::size_t slot_bytes, std::size_t bytes_per_op,
150	    std::uint32_t insert_seed, std::uint32_t get_seed,
151	    int rep, const std::string& tag,
152	    float time_ms,
153	    std::uint64_t total_probes, std::uint64_t total_hits, std::uint64_t total_misses)
154	{
155	    std::ostringstream s;
156	    s.precision(9);
157	    s << "gpurhh,get,"
158	      << tag           << ","
159	      << rep           << ","
160	      << insert_seed   << ","
161	      << get_seed      << ","
162	      << capacity      << ","
163	      << key_range     << ","
164	      << n_ops         << ","
165	      << block_size    << ","
166	      << slot_bytes    << ","
167	      << bytes_per_op  << ","
168	      << time_ms       << ","
169	      << total_probes  << ","
170	      << total_hits    << ","
171	      << total_misses;
172	    return s.str();
173	}
174	
175	} // namespace
176	
177	int main(int argc, char** argv) {
178	    const Args args = parse_args(argc, argv);
179	
180	    constexpr std::size_t slot_bytes   = sizeof(Table::Slot);
181	    constexpr std::size_t bytes_per_op = sizeof(Table::Bucket);
182	
183	    Table table(args.capacity);
184	    const std::size_t capacity = table.capacity();
185	    const std::uint32_t key_range = static_cast<std::uint32_t>(args.key_range);
186	
187	    std::fprintf(stderr,
188	        "[get-counters] capacity=%zu key_range=%zu n_ops=%zu "
189	        "block_size=%d warmups=%d reps=%d insert_seed=%u get_seed=%u tag=\"%s\"\n",
190	        capacity, args.key_range, args.n_ops,
191	        args.block_size, args.warmups, args.reps,
192	        args.insert_seed, args.get_seed, args.tag.c_str());
193	
194	    // --- pre-fill (untimed) ---
195	    std::uint32_t* d_insert_keys = nullptr;
196	    cudaMalloc(&d_insert_keys, args.n_ops * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
197	    {
198	        constexpr int fill_block = 256;
199	        const int fill_grid =
200	            static_cast<int>((args.n_ops + fill_block - 1) / fill_block);
201	        fill_uniform_keys<<<fill_grid, fill_block>>>(
202	            d_insert_keys, args.n_ops, args.insert_seed, key_range);
203	        cudaGetLastError() >> CUDA_CHECK;
204	    }
205	
206	    const auto shape = compute_launch_shape(args.block_size, Table::tile_size);
207	    const std::size_t n_tiles = shape.n_tiles;
208	
209	    prefill<<<shape.grid_size, args.block_size>>>(
210	        table.view(), d_insert_keys, args.n_ops);
211	    cudaGetLastError()      >> CUDA_CHECK;
212	    cudaDeviceSynchronize() >> CUDA_CHECK;
213	    cudaFree(d_insert_keys);
214	
215	    // --- get keys ---
216	    std::uint32_t* d_get_keys = nullptr;
217	    cudaMalloc(&d_get_keys, args.n_ops * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
218	    {
219	        constexpr int fill_block = 256;
220	        const int fill_grid =
221	            static_cast<int>((args.n_ops + fill_block - 1) / fill_block);
222	        fill_uniform_keys<<<fill_grid, fill_block>>>(
223	            d_get_keys, args.n_ops, args.get_seed, key_range);
224	        cudaGetLastError() >> CUDA_CHECK;
225	    }
226	
227	    std::uint32_t* d_out = nullptr;
228	    cudaMalloc(&d_out, args.n_ops * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
229	
230	    std::uint32_t* d_probes = nullptr;
231	    std::uint32_t* d_hits   = nullptr;
232	    cudaMalloc(&d_probes, n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
233	    cudaMalloc(&d_hits,   n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
234	    std::vector<std::uint32_t> h_probes(n_tiles);
235	    std::vector<std::uint32_t> h_hits  (n_tiles);
236	
237	    Recorder rec(args.output_dir / "get_counters.csv",
238	        "library,workload,tag,rep,insert_seed,get_seed,capacity,key_range,"
239	        "n_ops,block_size,slot_bytes,bytes_per_op,time_ms,"
240	        "total_probes,total_hits,total_misses");
241	
242	    EventTimer timer;
243	
244	    run_benchmark_loop(args.warmups, args.reps, timer,
245	        /*setup=*/  [&]() {
246	            cudaMemset(d_probes, 0, n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
247	            cudaMemset(d_hits,   0, n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
248	        },
249	        /*launch=*/ [&]() {
250	            get<<<shape.grid_size, args.block_size>>>(
251	                table.view(), d_get_keys, d_out, args.n_ops,
252	                d_probes, d_hits);
253	            cudaGetLastError() >> CUDA_CHECK;
254	        },
255	        /*after=*/  [&](int rep, float ms) {
256	            cudaMemcpy(h_probes.data(), d_probes,
257	                       n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t),
258	                       cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost) >> CUDA_CHECK;
259	            cudaMemcpy(h_hits.data(),   d_hits,
260	                       n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t),
261	                       cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost) >> CUDA_CHECK;
262	            const std::uint64_t total_probes =
263	                std::accumulate(h_probes.begin(), h_probes.end(), std::uint64_t{0});
264	            const std::uint64_t total_hits =
265	                std::accumulate(h_hits.begin(),   h_hits.end(),   std::uint64_t{0});
266	            const std::uint64_t total_misses =
267	                std::uint64_t{args.n_ops} - total_hits;
268	            rec.write_row(format_row(

270	                args.block_size, slot_bytes, bytes_per_op,
271	                args.insert_seed, args.get_seed, rep, args.tag,
272	                ms, total_probes, total_hits, total_misses));
```

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T03:31:59.945Z

**→ Tool call: `Edit`** (id `tool-660`)

<details><summary><code>tool input</code></summary>

```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "[repo]/benchmarks/benchmarks.cuh",
  "old_string": "// Fill out[0..n) with fmix32(seed + i) mod range. For `range` a power\n// of two (the case here, since key range = α × capacity with capacity\n// a power of two), the modulus yields a uniform distribution.\n//\n// Not marked inline: __global__ functions cannot be inline, but each\n// benchmark binary is a single translation unit that includes this\n// header exactly once, so there is no multiple-definition concern.\n__global__ void fill_uniform_keys(\n    std::uint32_t* out, std::size_t n,\n    std::uint32_t seed, std::uint32_t range)\n{\n    const std::size_t tid = blockIdx.x * std::size_t{blockDim.x} + threadIdx.x;\n    if (tid >= n) return;\n    out[tid] = gpurhh::detail::fmix32(static_cast<std::uint32_t>(tid) + seed) % range;\n}",
  "new_string": "// Generate `n` iid Uniform(0, range) draws on the host with std::mt19937\n// and synchronously copy them to the device buffer `d_out`.\n//\n// The generator is passed by reference so callers can draw sequentially\n// across multiple buffers from a single seed — e.g. a get benchmark\n// produces the insert key stream first, then continues the same generator\n// to produce the get key stream. The two streams share no draws.\n//\n// cudaMemcpy here is synchronous; key generation and the host-to-device\n// copy both complete before this function returns. The benchmark loop's\n// cudaDeviceSynchronize() between warmups and timed reps then guarantees\n// no key-fill work overlaps with the timed kernel.\ninline void fill_uniform_keys(\n    std::uint32_t* d_out, std::size_t n,\n    std::uint32_t range, std::mt19937& gen)\n{\n    std::vector<std::uint32_t> h(n);\n    std::uniform_int_distribution<std::uint32_t> dist(0, range - 1);\n    for (std::size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) h[i] = dist(gen);\n    cudaMemcpy(d_out, h.data(), n * sizeof(std::uint32_t),\n               cudaMemcpyHostToDevice) >> CUDA_CHECK;\n}"
}
```

</details>

```json
{
```

```json
  "file_path": "[repo]/benchmarks/benchmark_get.cu",
  "old_string": "// Get benchmark for gpurhh — comparison version.\n//\n// Mirrors benchmark_insert.cu's apples-to-apples comparison setup:\n//   - replace_op reduction (no extra work on duplicate keys at pre-fill).\n//   - MaxProbeBuckets effectively uncapped.\n//   - No counter instrumentation.\n//\n// Workload structure:\n//   1. Pre-fill the table once with one stream of Uniform(0, key_range)\n//      keys (untimed).\n//   2. Generate get keys with an independent seed (also untimed).\n//   3. Time the bulk get kernel.\n//\n// All derived metrics live in the analysis script.",
  "new_string": "// Get benchmark for gpurhh — comparison version.\n//\n// Mirrors benchmark_insert.cu's apples-to-apples comparison setup:\n//   - replace_op reduction (no extra work on duplicate keys at pre-fill).\n//   - MaxProbeBuckets effectively uncapped.\n//   - No counter instrumentation.\n//\n// Workload structure:\n//   1. Generate insert keys from a single std::mt19937 seeded with --seed\n//      (host side, untimed), copy to device, pre-fill the table.\n//   2. Continue the same generator to produce n_ops more disjoint draws\n//      for the get key stream (also untimed).\n//   3. Time the bulk get kernel.\n//\n// The two key streams share no draws — the generator advances\n// sequentially — so one seed cleanly parameterizes the whole experiment.\n// All derived metrics live in the analysis script."
}
```

```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
```
```json
  "file_path": "[repo]/benchmarks/benchmark_get_counters.cu",
```
```json
  "old_string": "std::string format_row(\n    std::size_t capacity, std::size_t key_range, std::size_t n_ops,\n    int block_size, std::size_t slot_bytes, std::size_t bytes_per_op,\n    std::uint32_t insert_seed, std::uint32_t get_seed,\n    int rep, const std::string& tag,\n    float time_ms,\n    std::uint64_t total_probes, std::uint64_t total_hits, std::uint64_t total_misses)\n{\n    std::ostringstream s;\n    s.precision(9);\n    s << \"gpurhh,get,\"\n      << tag           << \",\"\n      << rep           << \",\"\n      << insert_seed   << \",\"\n      << get_seed      << \",\"\n      << capacity      << \",\"\n      << key_range     << \",\"\n      << n_ops         << \",\"\n      << block_size    << \",\"\n      << slot_bytes    << \",\"\n      << bytes_per_op  << \",\"\n      << time_ms       << \",\"\n      << total_probes  << \",\"\n      << total_hits    << \",\"\n      << total_misses;\n    return s.str();\n}",
  "new_string": "std::string format_row(\n    std::size_t capacity, std::size_t key_range, std::size_t n_ops,\n    int block_size, std::size_t slot_bytes, std::size_t bytes_per_op,\n    std::uint32_t seed, int rep, const std::string& tag,\n    float time_ms,\n    std::uint64_t total_probes, std::uint64_t total_hits, std::uint64_t total_misses)\n{\n    std::ostringstream s;\n    s.precision(9);\n    s << \"gpurhh,get,\"\n      << tag           << \",\"\n      << rep           << \",\"\n      << seed          << \",\"\n      << capacity      << \",\"\n      << key_range     << \",\"\n      << n_ops         << \",\"\n      << block_size    << \",\"\n      << slot_bytes    << \",\"\n      << bytes_per_op  << \",\"\n      << time_ms       << \",\"\n      << total_probes  << \",\"\n      << total_hits    << \",\"\n      << total_misses;\n    return s.str();\n}"
}
```
