```
1	// Insert benchmark against cuCollections's `static_map`.
2	//
3	// Apples-to-apples with benchmarks/benchmark_insert.cu: same CSV schema,
4	// same raw configuration columns (library distinguishes rows). Derived
5	// metrics live in the analysis script, not here.
6	
7	#include "../../benchmarks.cuh"
8	
9	#include <cuco/static_map.cuh>
10	
11	#include <thrust/iterator/transform_iterator.h>
12	
13	#include <cuda_runtime.h>
14	
15	#include <cstddef>
16	#include <cstdint>
17	#include <cstdio>
18	#include <cstdlib>
19	#include <filesystem>
20	#include <sstream>
21	#include <string>
22	
23	using Key   = std::uint32_t;
24	using Value = std::uint32_t;
25	
26	constexpr Key   EMPTY_KEY   = ~Key{};
27	constexpr Value EMPTY_VALUE = ~Value{};
28	
29	namespace {
30	
31	struct Args {
32	    std::size_t   capacity   = std::size_t{1} << 27;
33	    std::size_t   key_range  = std::size_t{1} << 27;
34	    std::size_t   n_ops      = std::size_t{1} << 27;
35	    int           warmups    = 2;
36	    int           reps       = 16;
37	    std::uint32_t seed       = 42;
38	    std::string   tag        = "";
39	    std::filesystem::path output_dir;
40	};
41	
42	[[noreturn]] void die_usage(const char* prog) {
43	    std::fprintf(stderr,
44	        "usage: %s --output-dir DIR [options]\n"
45	        "  --capacity N --key-range N --n-ops N --warmups N --reps N --seed N --tag STR\n"
46	        "  Same shape as benchmarks/benchmark_insert. cuco picks its own internal\n"
47	        "  launch shape; --block-size is not a flag here.\n",
48	        prog);
49	    std::exit(1);
50	}
51	
52	Args parse_args(int argc, char** argv) {
53	    Args a;
54	    for (int i = 1; i < argc; ++i) {
55	        std::string flag = argv[i];
56	        auto get_val = [&]() -> std::string {
57	            if (i + 1 >= argc) { std::fprintf(stderr, "Missing value for %s\n", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }
58	            return argv[++i];
59	        };
60	        if      (flag == "--capacity")   a.capacity   = std::stoull(get_val());
61	        else if (flag == "--key-range")  a.key_range  = std::stoull(get_val());
62	        else if (flag == "--n-ops")      a.n_ops      = std::stoull(get_val());
63	        else if (flag == "--warmups")    a.warmups    = std::stoi(get_val());
64	        else if (flag == "--reps")       a.reps       = std::stoi(get_val());
65	        else if (flag == "--seed")       a.seed       = static_cast<std::uint32_t>(std::stoul(get_val()));
66	        else if (flag == "--tag")        a.tag        = get_val();
67	        else if (flag == "--output-dir") a.output_dir = get_val();
68	        else { std::fprintf(stderr, "Unknown flag: %s\n", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }
69	    }
70	    if (a.output_dir.empty())  die_usage(argv[0]);
71	    if (a.n_ops == 0)          { std::fprintf(stderr, "--n-ops must be > 0\n");     std::exit(1); }
72	    if (a.key_range == 0)      { std::fprintf(stderr, "--key-range must be > 0\n"); std::exit(1); }
73	    return a;
74	}
75	
76	// Matches benchmark_insert.cu's schema. block_size cell is empty for
77	// cuco (no exposed launch knob).
78	std::string format_row(
79	    std::size_t capacity, std::size_t key_range, std::size_t n_ops,
80	    std::size_t slot_bytes, std::size_t bytes_per_op,
81	    std::uint32_t seed, int rep, const std::string& tag,
82	    float time_ms)
83	{
84	    std::ostringstream s;
85	    s.precision(9);
86	    s << "cuco,insert,"
87	      << tag          << ","
88	      << rep          << ","
89	      << seed         << ","
90	      << capacity     << ","
91	      << key_range    << ","
92	      << n_ops        << ","
93	      << ","          // block_size empty for cuco
94	      << slot_bytes   << ","
95	      << bytes_per_op << ","
96	      << time_ms;
97	    return s.str();
98	}
99	
100	} // namespace
101	
102	int main(int argc, char** argv) {
103	    const Args args = parse_args(argc, argv);
104	
105	    constexpr std::size_t slot_bytes   = sizeof(Key) + sizeof(Value);
106	    constexpr std::size_t bytes_per_op = 128;
107	    const Key key_range = static_cast<Key>(args.key_range);
108	
109	    std::fprintf(stderr,
110	        "[cuco insert] capacity=%zu key_range=%zu n_ops=%zu "
111	        "warmups=%d reps=%d seed=%u tag=\"%s\"\n",
112	        args.capacity, args.key_range, args.n_ops,
113	        args.warmups, args.reps, args.seed, args.tag.c_str());
114	
115	    Key* d_keys = nullptr;
116	    cudaMalloc(&d_keys, args.n_ops * sizeof(Key)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
117	    UniformKeyGenerator gen(args.seed);
118	
119	    // Transform iterator: builds cuco::pair<Key, Value> on the fly from
120	    // each key, with value = fmix32(key). Matches the gpurhh benchmark's
121	    // value-derivation choice.
122	    auto pair_begin = thrust::make_transform_iterator(
123	        d_keys,
124	        [] __device__ (Key k) -> cuco::pair<Key, Value> {
125	            return cuco::pair<Key, Value>{k, gpurhh::detail::fmix32(k)};
126	        });
127	
128	    cuco::static_map<Key, Value> map{
129	        args.capacity,
130	        cuco::empty_key<Key>{EMPTY_KEY},
131	        cuco::empty_value<Value>{EMPTY_VALUE}};
132	
133	    Recorder rec(args.output_dir / "insert.csv",
134	        "library,workload,tag,rep,seed,capacity,key_range,n_ops,block_size,"
135	        "slot_bytes,bytes_per_op,time_ms");
136	
137	    EventTimer timer;
138	
139	    run_benchmark_loop(args.warmups, args.reps, timer,
140	        /*setup=*/  [&]() {
141	            map.clear_async();
142	            fill_uniform_keys(d_keys, args.n_ops, key_range, gen);
143	        },
144	        /*launch=*/ [&]() { map.insert_async(pair_begin, pair_begin + args.n_ops); },
145	        /*after=*/  [&](int rep, float ms) {
146	            rec.write_row(format_row(
147	                args.capacity, args.key_range, args.n_ops,
148	                slot_bytes, bytes_per_op,
149	                args.seed, rep, args.tag, ms));
150	        });
151	
152	    cudaFree(d_keys);
153	    return 0;
154	}
155	
```

</details>

2	//
3	// Apples-to-apples with benchmarks/benchmark_get.cu's get.csv schema.

6	
7	#include <cuco/static_map.cuh>
8

10	
11	#include <cuda_runtime.h>

20	
21	using Key   = std::uint32_t;
22	using Value = std::uint32_t;
23	
24	constexpr Key   EMPTY_KEY   = ~Key{};
25	constexpr Value EMPTY_VALUE = ~Value{};
26	
27	namespace {
28	
29	struct Args {
30	    std::size_t   capacity   = std::size_t{1} << 27;
31	    std::size_t   key_range  = std::size_t{1} << 27;
32	    std::size_t   n_ops      = std::size_t{1} << 27;
33	    int           warmups    = 2;
34	    int           reps       = 16;
35	    std::uint32_t seed       = 42;
36	    std::string   tag        = "";
37	    std::filesystem::path output_dir;
38	};
39	
40	[[noreturn]] void die_usage(const char* prog) {
41	    std::fprintf(stderr,
42	        "usage: %s --output-dir DIR [options]\n"
43	        "  --capacity N --key-range N --n-ops N --warmups N --reps N\n"
44	        "  --seed N --tag STR\n",
45	        prog);
46	    std::exit(1);
47	}
48	
49	Args parse_args(int argc, char** argv) {
50	    Args a;
51	    for (int i = 1; i < argc; ++i) {
52	        std::string flag = argv[i];
53	        auto get_val = [&]() -> std::string {
54	            if (i + 1 >= argc) { std::fprintf(stderr, "Missing value for %s\n", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }
55	            return argv[++i];
56	        };
57	        if      (flag == "--capacity")   a.capacity   = std::stoull(get_val());
58	        else if (flag == "--key-range")  a.key_range  = std::stoull(get_val());
59	        else if (flag == "--n-ops")      a.n_ops      = std::stoull(get_val());
60	        else if (flag == "--warmups")    a.warmups    = std::stoi(get_val());
61	        else if (flag == "--reps")       a.reps       = std::stoi(get_val());
62	        else if (flag == "--seed")       a.seed       = static_cast<std::uint32_t>(std::stoul(get_val()));
63	        else if (flag == "--tag")        a.tag        = get_val();
64	        else if (flag == "--output-dir") a.output_dir = get_val();
65	        else { std::fprintf(stderr, "Unknown flag: %s\n", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }
66	    }
67	    if (a.output_dir.empty()) die_usage(argv[0]);
68	    if (a.n_ops == 0)         { std::fprintf(stderr, "--n-ops must be > 0\n");     std::exit(1); }
69	    if (a.key_range == 0)     { std::fprintf(stderr, "--key-range must be > 0\n"); std::exit(1); }
70	    return a;
71	}
72	
73	std::string format_row(
74	    std::size_t capacity, std::size_t key_range, std::size_t n_ops,
75	    std::size_t slot_bytes, std::size_t bytes_per_op,
76	    std::uint32_t seed, int rep, const std::string& tag,
77	    float time_ms)
78	{
79	    std::ostringstream s;
80	    s.precision(9);
81	    s << "cuco,get,"
82	      << tag          << ","
83	      << rep          << ","
84	      << seed         << ","
85	      << capacity     << ","
86	      << key_range    << ","
87	      << n_ops        << ","
88	      << ","          // block_size empty for cuco
89	      << slot_bytes   << ","
90	      << bytes_per_op << ","
91	      << time_ms;
92	    return s.str();
93	}
94	
95	} // namespace
96	
97	int main(int argc, char** argv) {
98	    const Args args = parse_args(argc, argv);
99	
100	    constexpr std::size_t slot_bytes   = sizeof(Key) + sizeof(Value);
```

16	
17	using Key   = std::uint32_t;
18	using Value = std::uint32_t;
19	using Table = warpcore::SingleValueHashTable<Key, Value>;
20	
21	namespace {
22	
23	struct Args {
24	    std::size_t   capacity   = std::size_t{1} << 27;
25	    std::size_t   key_range  = std::size_t{1} << 27;
26	    std::size_t   n_ops      = std::size_t{1} << 27;
27	    int           warmups    = 2;
28	    int           reps       = 16;
29	    std::uint32_t seed       = 42;
30	    std::string   tag        = "";
31	    std::filesystem::path output_dir;
32	};
33	
34	[[noreturn]] void die_usage(const char* prog) {
35	    std::fprintf(stderr,
36	        "usage: %s --output-dir DIR [options]\n"
37	        "  --capacity N --key-range N --n-ops N --warmups N --reps N\n"
38	        "  --seed N --tag STR\n",
39	        prog);
40	    std::exit(1);
41	}
42	
43	Args parse_args(int argc, char** argv) {
44	    Args a;
45	    for (int i = 1; i < argc; ++i) {
46	        std::string flag = argv[i];
47	        auto get_val = [&]() -> std::string {
48	            if (i + 1 >= argc) { std::fprintf(stderr, "Missing value for %s\n", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }
49	            return argv[++i];
50	        };
51	        if      (flag == "--capacity")   a.capacity   = std::stoull(get_val());
52	        else if (flag == "--key-range")  a.key_range  = std::stoull(get_val());
53	        else if (flag == "--n-ops")      a.n_ops      = std::stoull(get_val());
54	        else if (flag == "--warmups")    a.warmups    = std::stoi(get_val());
55	        else if (flag == "--reps")       a.reps       = std::stoi(get_val());
56	        else if (flag == "--seed")       a.seed       = static_cast<std::uint32_t>(std::stoul(get_val()));
57	        else if (flag == "--tag")        a.tag        = get_val();
58	        else if (flag == "--output-dir") a.output_dir = get_val();
59	        else { std::fprintf(stderr, "Unknown flag: %s\n", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }
60	    }
61	    if (a.output_dir.empty()) die_usage(argv[0]);
62	    if (a.n_ops == 0)         { std::fprintf(stderr, "--n-ops must be > 0\n");     std::exit(1); }
63	    if (a.key_range == 0)     { std::fprintf(stderr, "--key-range must be > 0\n"); std::exit(1); }
64	    return a;
65	}
66	
67	std::string format_row(
68	    std::size_t capacity, std::size_t key_range, std::size_t n_ops,
69	    std::size_t slot_bytes, std::size_t bytes_per_op,
70	    std::uint32_t seed, int rep, const std::string& tag,
71	    float time_ms)
72	{
73	    std::ostringstream s;
74	    s.precision(9);
75	    s << "warpcore,get,"
76	      << tag          << ","
77	      << rep          << ","
78	      << seed         << ","
79	      << capacity     << ","
80	      << key_range    << ","
```

## User — 2026-05-19T14:27:21.414Z

**← Tool result** (for `tool-799`)

<details><summary><code>tool result</code></summary>

```
1	// Insert benchmark for gpurhh — counter-instrumented version.
2	//
3	// Studies gpurhh's design in its full configuration:
4	//   - Reduction operator: sum_op (exercise the reduction path).
5	//   - MaxProbeBuckets: 8 (the default — study the probe-cap regime).
6	//   - Per-tile probe / failure counters via GPURHH_BENCHMARK_COUNTERS.
7	//
8	// Writes to insert_counters.csv (separate from the apples-to-apples
9	// insert.csv that benchmark_insert.cu produces). The extra counter
10	// columns (total_probes, total_failures) live only here.
11	
12	#define GPURHH_BENCHMARK_COUNTERS 1
13	
14	#include "benchmarks.cuh"
15	
16	#include <cooperative_groups.h>
17	#include <cuda_runtime.h>
18	
19	#include <cstddef>
20	#include <cstdint>
21	#include <cstdio>
22	#include <cstdlib>
23	#include <filesystem>
24	#include <numeric>
25	#include <sstream>
26	#include <string>
27	#include <vector>
28	
29	namespace cg = cooperative_groups;
30	
31	using Table = gpurhh::HashTable<
32	    std::uint32_t,
33	    std::uint32_t,
34	    gpurhh::default_hash<std::uint32_t>,
35	    gpurhh::default_empty_key<std::uint32_t>::key,
36	    gpurhh::sum_op>;
37	using View = Table::View;
38	
39	namespace {
40	
41	struct Args {
42	    std::size_t   capacity   = std::size_t{1} << 27;
43	    std::size_t   key_range  = std::size_t{1} << 27;
44	    std::size_t   n_ops      = std::size_t{1} << 27;
45	    int           block_size = 256;
46	    int           warmups    = 2;
47	    int           reps       = 16;
48	    std::uint32_t seed       = 42;
49	    std::string   tag        = "";
50	    std::filesystem::path output_dir;
51	};
52	
53	[[noreturn]] void die_usage(const char* prog) {
54	    std::fprintf(stderr,
55	        "usage: %s --output-dir DIR [options]\n"
56	        "  Same shape as benchmark_insert. Writes insert_counters.csv with\n"
57	        "  per-rep total_probes and total_failures alongside time_ms.\n",
58	        prog);
59	    std::exit(1);
60	}
61	
62	Args parse_args(int argc, char** argv) {
63	    Args a;
64	    for (int i = 1; i < argc; ++i) {
65	        std::string flag = argv[i];
66	        auto get_val = [&]() -> std::string {
67	            if (i + 1 >= argc) { std::fprintf(stderr, "Missing value for %s\n", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }
68	            return argv[++i];
69	        };
70	        if      (flag == "--capacity")   a.capacity   = std::stoull(get_val());
71	        else if (flag == "--key-range")  a.key_range  = std::stoull(get_val());
72	        else if (flag == "--n-ops")      a.n_ops      = std::stoull(get_val());
73	        else if (flag == "--block-size") a.block_size = std::stoi(get_val());
74	        else if (flag == "--warmups")    a.warmups    = std::stoi(get_val());
75	        else if (flag == "--reps")       a.reps       = std::stoi(get_val());
76	        else if (flag == "--seed")       a.seed       = static_cast<std::uint32_t>(std::stoul(get_val()));
77	        else if (flag == "--tag")        a.tag        = get_val();
78	        else if (flag == "--output-dir") a.output_dir = get_val();
79	        else { std::fprintf(stderr, "Unknown flag: %s\n", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }
80	    }
81	    if (a.output_dir.empty())                     die_usage(argv[0]);
82	    if (a.n_ops == 0)                             { std::fprintf(stderr, "--n-ops must be > 0\n");     std::exit(1); }
83	    if (a.key_range == 0)                         { std::fprintf(stderr, "--key-range must be > 0\n"); std::exit(1); }
84	    if (a.block_size % Table::tile_size != 0)     { std::fprintf(stderr, "--block-size must be a multiple of tile_size (%d)\n", Table::tile_size); std::exit(1); }
85	    if (a.block_size <= 0 || a.block_size > 1024) { std::fprintf(stderr, "--block-size out of range\n"); std::exit(1); }
86	    return a;
87	}
88	
89	__global__ void insert(
90	    View view,
91	    const std::uint32_t* __restrict__ keys,
92	    std::size_t n_ops,
93	    std::uint32_t* __restrict__ per_tile_probes,
94	    std::uint32_t* __restrict__ per_tile_failures)
95	{
96	    auto block = cg::this_thread_block();
97	    auto tile  = cg::tiled_partition<Table::tile_size>(block);
98	
99	    const std::size_t tiles_per_block = blockDim.x / Table::tile_size;
100	    const std::size_t tile_id =
101	        blockIdx.x * tiles_per_block + threadIdx.x / Table::tile_size;
102	    const std::size_t total_tiles =
103	        std::size_t{gridDim.x} * tiles_per_block;
104	
105	    std::uint32_t my_probes   = 0;
106	    std::uint32_t my_failures = 0;
107	
108	    for (std::size_t op_id = tile_id; op_id < n_ops; op_id += total_tiles) {
109	        const std::uint32_t k = keys[op_id];
110	        const std::uint32_t v = gpurhh::detail::fmix32(k);
111	        const auto leftover = view.insert(tile, k, v, my_probes);
112	        if (leftover.has_value() && tile.thread_rank() == 0) {
113	            ++my_failures;
114	        }
115	    }
116	
117	    if (tile.thread_rank() == 0) {
118	        per_tile_probes  [tile_id] = my_probes;
119	        per_tile_failures[tile_id] = my_failures;
120	    }
121	}
122	
123	std::string format_row(
124	    std::size_t capacity, std::size_t key_range, std::size_t n_ops,
125	    int block_size, std::size_t slot_bytes, std::size_t bytes_per_op,
126	    std::uint32_t seed, int rep, const std::string& tag,
127	    float time_ms, std::uint64_t total_probes, std::uint64_t total_failures)
128	{
129	    std::ostringstream s;
130	    s.precision(9);
131	    s << "gpurhh,insert,"
132	      << tag           << ","
133	      << rep           << ","
134	      << seed          << ","
135	      << capacity      << ","
136	      << key_range     << ","
137	      << n_ops         << ","
138	      << block_size    << ","
139	      << slot_bytes    << ","
140	      << bytes_per_op  << ","
141	      << time_ms       << ","
142	      << total_probes  << ","
143	      << total_failures;
144	    return s.str();
145	}
146	
147	} // namespace
148	
149	int main(int argc, char** argv) {
150	    const Args args = parse_args(argc, argv);
151	
152	    constexpr std::size_t slot_bytes   = sizeof(Table::Slot);
153	    constexpr std::size_t bytes_per_op = sizeof(Table::Bucket);
154	
155	    Table table(args.capacity);
156	    const std::size_t capacity = table.capacity();
157	    const std::uint32_t key_range = static_cast<std::uint32_t>(args.key_range);
158	
159	    std::fprintf(stderr,
160	        "[insert-counters] capacity=%zu key_range=%zu n_ops=%zu "
161	        "block_size=%d warmups=%d reps=%d seed=%u tag=\"%s\"\n",
162	        capacity, args.key_range, args.n_ops,
163	        args.block_size, args.warmups, args.reps, args.seed, args.tag.c_str());
164	
165	    std::uint32_t* d_keys = nullptr;
166	    cudaMalloc(&d_keys, args.n_ops * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
167	    UniformKeyGenerator gen(args.seed);
168	
169	    const auto shape = compute_launch_shape(args.block_size, Table::tile_size);
170	    const std::size_t n_tiles = shape.n_tiles;
171	
172	    std::uint32_t* d_probes   = nullptr;
173	    std::uint32_t* d_failures = nullptr;
174	    cudaMalloc(&d_probes,   n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
175	    cudaMalloc(&d_failures, n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
176	    std::vector<std::uint32_t> h_probes  (n_tiles);
177	    std::vector<std::uint32_t> h_failures(n_tiles);
178	
179	    Recorder rec(args.output_dir / "insert_counters.csv",
180	        "library,workload,tag,rep,seed,capacity,key_range,n_ops,block_size,"
181	        "slot_bytes,bytes_per_op,time_ms,total_probes,total_failures");
182	
183	    EventTimer timer;
184	
185	    run_benchmark_loop(args.warmups, args.reps, timer,
186	        /*setup=*/  [&]() {
187	            table.clear();
188	            fill_uniform_keys(d_keys, args.n_ops, key_range, gen);
189	            cudaMemset(d_probes,   0, n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
190	            cudaMemset(d_failures, 0, n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
191	        },
192	        /*launch=*/ [&]() {
193	            insert<<<shape.grid_size, args.block_size>>>(
194	                table.view(), d_keys, args.n_ops, d_probes, d_failures);
195	            cudaGetLastError() >> CUDA_CHECK;
196	        },
197	        /*after=*/  [&](int rep, float ms) {
198	            cudaMemcpy(h_probes.data(),   d_probes,
199	                       n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t),
200	                       cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost) >> CUDA_CHECK;
201	            cudaMemcpy(h_failures.data(), d_failures,
202	                       n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t),
203	                       cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost) >> CUDA_CHECK;
204	            const std::uint64_t total_probes =
205	                std::accumulate(h_probes.begin(), h_probes.end(), std::uint64_t{0});
206	            const std::uint64_t total_failures =
207	                std::accumulate(h_failures.begin(), h_failures.end(), std::uint64_t{0});
208	            rec.write_row(format_row(
209	                capacity, args.key_range, args.n_ops,
210	                args.block_size, slot_bytes, bytes_per_op,
211	                args.seed, rep, args.tag,
212	                ms, total_probes, total_failures));
213	        });
214	
215	    cudaFree(d_keys);
216	    cudaFree(d_probes);
217	    cudaFree(d_failures);
218	    return 0;
219	}
220	
```

</details>

Writes insert_counters.csv with\\n\"\n        \"  per-rep total_probes and total_failures alongside time_ms.\\n\",\n        prog);\n    std::exit(1);\n}\n\nArgs parse_args(int argc, char** argv) {\n    Args a;\n    for (int i = 1; i < argc; ++i) {\n        std::string flag = argv[i];\n        auto get_val = [&]() -> std::string {\n            if (i + 1 >= argc) { std::fprintf(stderr, \"Missing value for %s\\n\", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }\n            return argv[++i];\n        };\n        if      (flag == \"--capacity\")   a.capacity   = std::stoull(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--key-range\")  a.key_range  = std::stoull(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--n-ops\")      a.n_ops      = std::stoull(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--block-size\") a.block_size = std::stoi(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--warmups\")    a.warmups    = std::stoi(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--reps\")       a.reps       = std::stoi(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--seed\")       a.seed       = static_cast<std::uint32_t>(std::stoul(get_val()));\n        else if (flag == \"--tag\")        a.tag        = get_val();\n        else if (flag == \"--output-dir\") a.output_dir = get_val();\n        else { std::fprintf(stderr, \"Unknown flag: %s\\n\", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }\n    }\n    if (a.output_dir.empty())                     die_usage(argv[0]);\n    if (a.n_ops == 0)                             { std::fprintf(stderr, \"--n-ops must be > 0\\n\");     std::exit(1); }\n    if (a.key_range == 0)                         { std::fprintf(stderr, \"--key-range must be > 0\\n\"); std::exit(1); }\n    if (a.block_size % Table::tile_size != 0)     { std::fprintf(stderr, \"--block-size must be a multiple of tile_size (%d)\\n\", Table::tile_size); std::exit(1); }\n    if (a.block_size <= 0 || a.block_size > 1024) { std::fprintf(stderr, \"--block-size out of range\\n\"); std::exit(1); }\n    return a;\n}",
  "new_string": "Args parse_args(int argc, char** argv) {\n    Args a;\n    ArgParser p(argv[0]);\n    p.add(\"--capacity\",   a.capacity,   \"Table size in slots\")\n     .add(\"--key-range\",  a.key_range,  \"N in Uniform(0, N) for key generation\")\n     .add(\"--n-ops\",      a.n_ops,      \"Number of insert attempts\")\n     .add(\"--block-size\", a.block_size, \"Threads per CUDA block; multiple of tile_size\")\n     .add(\"--warmups\",    a.warmups,    \"Untimed warmup reps\")\n     .add(\"--reps\",       a.reps,       \"Timed reps\")\n     .add(\"--seed\",       a.seed,       \"cuRAND seed for key generation\")\n     .add(\"--tag\",        a.tag,        \"Free-form label written to every CSV row\")\n     .add(\"--output-dir\", a.output_dir, \"Required. insert.csv is appended to here.\");\n    p.parse(argc, argv);\n\n    if (a.output_dir.empty())                     p.die_usage();\n    if (a.n_ops == 0)                             { std::fprintf(stderr, \"--n-ops must be > 0\\n\");     std::exit(1); }\n    if (a.key_range == 0)                         { std::fprintf(stderr, \"--key-range must be > 0\\n\"); std::exit(1); }\n    if (a.block_size % Table::tile_size != 0)     { std::fprintf(stderr, \"--block-size must be a multiple of tile_size (%d)\\n\", Table::tile_size); std::exit(1); }\n    if (a.block_size <= 0 || a.block_size > 1024) { std::fprintf(stderr, \"--block-size out of range\\n\"); std::exit(1); }\n    return a;\n}"
}

```
1	// Get benchmark for gpurhh — counter-instrumented version.
2	//
3	// Same shape as benchmark_get.cu, but with:
4	//   - sum_op (to exercise the reduction path during pre-fill).
5	//   - Default MaxProbeBuckets (= 8, study probe-cap regime).
6	//   - Per-tile probe / hit counters via GPURHH_BENCHMARK_COUNTERS.
7	//
8	// Writes to get_counters.csv with extra total_probes / total_hits /
9	// total_misses columns; misses = n_ops - hits.
10	
11	#define GPURHH_BENCHMARK_COUNTERS 1
12	
13	#include "benchmarks.cuh"
14	
15	#include <cooperative_groups.h>
16	#include <cuda_runtime.h>
17	
18	#include <cstddef>
19	#include <cstdint>
20	#include <cstdio>
21	#include <cstdlib>
22	#include <filesystem>
23	#include <numeric>
24	#include <sstream>
25	#include <string>
26	#include <vector>
27	
28	namespace cg = cooperative_groups;
29	
30	using Table = gpurhh::HashTable<
31	    std::uint32_t,
32	    std::uint32_t,
33	    gpurhh::default_hash<std::uint32_t>,
34	    gpurhh::default_empty_key<std::uint32_t>::key,
35	    gpurhh::sum_op>;
36	using View = Table::View;
37	
38	namespace {
39	
40	struct Args {
41	    std::size_t   capacity   = std::size_t{1} << 27;
42	    std::size_t   key_range  = std::size_t{1} << 27;
43	    std::size_t   n_ops      = std::size_t{1} << 27;
44	    int           block_size = 256;
45	    int           warmups    = 2;
46	    int           reps       = 16;
47	    std::uint32_t seed       = 42;
48	    std::string   tag        = "";
49	    std::filesystem::path output_dir;
50	};
51	
52	[[noreturn]] void die_usage(const char* prog) {
53	    std::fprintf(stderr,
54	        "usage: %s --output-dir DIR [options]\n"
55	        "  Same shape as benchmark_get. Writes get_counters.csv.\n",
56	        prog);
57	    std::exit(1);
58	}
59	
60	Args parse_args(int argc, char** argv) {
61	    Args a;
62	    for (int i = 1; i < argc; ++i) {
63	        std::string flag = argv[i];
64	        auto get_val = [&]() -> std::string {
65	            if (i + 1 >= argc) { std::fprintf(stderr, "Missing value for %s\n", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }
66	            return argv[++i];
67	        };
68	        if      (flag == "--capacity")   a.capacity   = std::stoull(get_val());
69	        else if (flag == "--key-range")  a.key_range  = std::stoull(get_val());
70	        else if (flag == "--n-ops")      a.n_ops      = std::stoull(get_val());
71	        else if (flag == "--block-size") a.block_size = std::stoi(get_val());
72	        else if (flag == "--warmups")    a.warmups    = std::stoi(get_val());
73	        else if (flag == "--reps")       a.reps       = std::stoi(get_val());
74	        else if (flag == "--seed")       a.seed       = static_cast<std::uint32_t>(std::stoul(get_val()));
75	        else if (flag == "--tag")        a.tag        = get_val();
76	        else if (flag == "--output-dir") a.output_dir = get_val();
77	        else { std::fprintf(stderr, "Unknown flag: %s\n", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }
78	    }
79	    if (a.output_dir.empty())                     die_usage(argv[0]);
80	    if (a.n_ops == 0)                             { std::fprintf(stderr, "--n-ops must be > 0\n");     std::exit(1); }
81	    if (a.key_range == 0)                         { std::fprintf(stderr, "--key-range must be > 0\n"); std::exit(1); }
82	    if (a.block_size % Table::tile_size != 0)     { std::fprintf(stderr, "--block-size must be a multiple of tile_size (%d)\n", Table::tile_size); std::exit(1); }
83	    if (a.block_size <= 0 || a.block_size > 1024) { std::fprintf(stderr, "--block-size out of range\n"); std::exit(1); }
84	    return a;
85	}
86	
87	__global__ void prefill(
88	    View view,
89	    const std::uint32_t* __restrict__ keys,
90	    std::size_t n_ops)
91	{
92	    auto block = cg::this_thread_block();
93	    auto tile  = cg::tiled_partition<Table::tile_size>(block);
94	
95	    const std::size_t tiles_per_block = blockDim.x / Table::tile_size;
96	    const std::size_t tile_id =
97	        blockIdx.x * tiles_per_block + threadIdx.x / Table::tile_size;
98	    const std::size_t total_tiles =
99	        std::size_t{gridDim.x} * tiles_per_block;
100	
101	    std::uint32_t scratch_probes = 0;  // discarded; only counts during prefill
102	
103	    for (std::size_t op_id = tile_id; op_id < n_ops; op_id += total_tiles) {
104	        const std::uint32_t k = keys[op_id];
105	        const std::uint32_t v = gpurhh::detail::fmix32(k);
106	        (void) view.insert(tile, k, v, scratch_probes);
107	    }
108	}
109	
110	__global__ void get(
111	    View view,
112	    const std::uint32_t* __restrict__ keys,
113	    std::uint32_t* __restrict__ out,
114	    std::size_t n_ops,
115	    std::uint32_t* __restrict__ per_tile_probes,
116	    std::uint32_t* __restrict__ per_tile_hits)
117	{
118	    auto block = cg::this_thread_block();
119	    auto tile  = cg::tiled_partition<Table::tile_size>(block);
120	
121	    const std::size_t tiles_per_block = blockDim.x / Table::tile_size;
122	    const std::size_t tile_id =
123	        blockIdx.x * tiles_per_block + threadIdx.x / Table::tile_size;
124	    const std::size_t total_tiles =
125	        std::size_t{gridDim.x} * tiles_per_block;
126	
127	    std::uint32_t my_probes = 0;
128	    std::uint32_t my_hits   = 0;
129	
130	    for (std::size_t op_id = tile_id; op_id < n_ops; op_id += total_tiles) {
131	        const std::uint32_t k = keys[op_id];
132	        const auto result = view.get(tile, k, my_probes);
133	        if (tile.thread_rank() == 0) {
134	            out[op_id] = result.value_or(~std::uint32_t{0});
135	            if (result.has_value()) ++my_hits;
136	        }
137	    }
138	
139	    if (tile.thread_rank() == 0) {
140	        per_tile_probes[tile_id] = my_probes;
141	        per_tile_hits  [tile_id] = my_hits;
142	    }
143	}
144	
145	std::string format_row(
146	    std::size_t capacity, std::size_t key_range, std::size_t n_ops,
147	    int block_size, std::size_t slot_bytes, std::size_t bytes_per_op,
148	    std::uint32_t seed, int rep, const std::string& tag,
149	    float time_ms,
150	    std::uint64_t total_probes, std::uint64_t total_hits, std::uint64_t total_misses)
151	{
152	    std::ostringstream s;
153	    s.precision(9);
154	    s << "gpurhh,get,"
155	      << tag           << ","
156	      << rep           << ","
157	      << seed          << ","
158	      << capacity      << ","
159	      << key_range     << ","
160	      << n_ops         << ","
161	      << block_size    << ","
162	      << slot_bytes    << ","
163	      << bytes_per_op  << ","
164	      << time_ms       << ","
165	      << total_probes  << ","
166	      << total_hits    << ","
167	      << total_misses;
168	    return s.str();
169	}
170	
171	} // namespace
172	
173	int main(int argc, char** argv) {
174	    const Args args = parse_args(argc, argv);
175	
176	    constexpr std::size_t slot_bytes   = sizeof(Table::Slot);
177	    constexpr std::size_t bytes_per_op = sizeof(Table::Bucket);
178	
179	    Table table(args.capacity);
180	    const std::size_t capacity = table.capacity();
181	    const std::uint32_t key_range = static_cast<std::uint32_t>(args.key_range);
182	
183	    std::fprintf(stderr,
184	        "[get-counters] capacity=%zu key_range=%zu n_ops=%zu "
185	        "block_size=%d warmups=%d reps=%d seed=%u tag=\"%s\"\n",
186	        capacity, args.key_range, args.n_ops,
187	        args.block_size, args.warmups, args.reps,
188	        args.seed, args.tag.c_str());
189	
190	    // Same single-generator scheme as benchmark_get.cu: n_ops draws for
191	    // the insert stream, then n_ops more draws for the get stream.
192	    UniformKeyGenerator gen(args.seed);
193	
194	    // --- pre-fill (untimed) ---
195	    std::uint32_t* d_insert_keys = nullptr;
196	    cudaMalloc(&d_insert_keys, args.n_ops * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
197	    fill_uniform_keys(d_insert_keys, args.n_ops, key_range, gen);
198	
199	    const auto shape = compute_launch_shape(args.block_size, Table::tile_size);
200	    const std::size_t n_tiles = shape.n_tiles;
201	
202	    prefill<<<shape.grid_size, args.block_size>>>(
203	        table.view(), d_insert_keys, args.n_ops);
204	    cudaGetLastError()      >> CUDA_CHECK;
205	    cudaDeviceSynchronize() >> CUDA_CHECK;
206	    cudaFree(d_insert_keys);
207	
208	    // --- get keys (refilled in setup per rep) ---
209	    std::uint32_t* d_get_keys = nullptr;
210	    cudaMalloc(&d_get_keys, args.n_ops * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
211	
212	    std::uint32_t* d_out = nullptr;
213	    cudaMalloc(&d_out, args.n_ops * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
214	
215	    std::uint32_t* d_probes = nullptr;
216	    std::uint32_t* d_hits   = nullptr;
217	    cudaMalloc(&d_probes, n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
218	    cudaMalloc(&d_hits,   n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
219	    std::vector<std::uint32_t> h_probes(n_tiles);
220	    std::vector<std::uint32_t> h_hits  (n_tiles);
221	
222	    Recorder rec(args.output_dir / "get_counters.csv",
223	        "library,workload,tag,rep,seed,capacity,key_range,"
224	        "n_ops,block_size,slot_bytes,bytes_per_op,time_ms,"
225	        "total_probes,total_hits,total_misses");
226	
227	    EventTimer timer;
228	
229	    run_benchmark_loop(args.warmups, args.reps, timer,
230	        /*setup=*/  [&]() {
231	            fill_uniform_keys(d_get_keys, args.n_ops, key_range, gen);
232	            cudaMemset(d_probes, 0, n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
233	            cudaMemset(d_hits,   0, n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t)) >> CUDA_CHECK;
234	        },
235	        /*launch=*/ [&]() {
236	            get<<<shape.grid_size, args.block_size>>>(
237	                table.view(), d_get_keys, d_out, args.n_ops,
238	                d_probes, d_hits);
239	            cudaGetLastError() >> CUDA_CHECK;
240	        },
241	        /*after=*/  [&](int rep, float ms) {
242	            cudaMemcpy(h_probes.data(), d_probes,
243	                       n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t),
244	                       cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost) >> CUDA_CHECK;
245	            cudaMemcpy(h_hits.data(),   d_hits,
246	                       n_tiles * sizeof(std::uint32_t),
247	                       cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost) >> CUDA_CHECK;
248	            const std::uint64_t total_probes =
249	                std::accumulate(h_probes.begin(), h_probes.end(), std::uint64_t{0});
250	            const std::uint64_t total_hits =
251	                std::accumulate(h_hits.begin(),   h_hits.end(),   std::uint64_t{0});
252	            const std::uint64_t total_misses =
253	                std::uint64_t{args.n_ops} - total_hits;
254	            rec.write_row(format_row(
255	                capacity, args.key_range, args.n_ops,
256	                args.block_size, slot_bytes, bytes_per_op,
257	                args.seed, rep, args.tag,
258	                ms, total_probes, total_hits, total_misses));
259	        });
260	
261	    cudaFree(d_get_keys);
262	    cudaFree(d_out);
263	    cudaFree(d_probes);
264	    cudaFree(d_hits);
265	    return 0;
266	}
267	
```

</details>

  "old_string": "[[noreturn]] void die_usage(const char* prog) {\n    std::fprintf(stderr,\n        \"usage: %s --output-dir DIR [options]\\n\"\n        \"  Same shape as benchmark_get. Writes get_counters.csv.\\n\",\n        prog);\n    std::exit(1);\n}\n\nArgs parse_args(int argc, char** argv) {\n    Args a;\n    for (int i = 1; i < argc; ++i) {\n        std::string flag = argv[i];\n        auto get_val = [&]() -> std::string {\n            if (i + 1 >= argc) { std::fprintf(stderr, \"Missing value for %s\\n\", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }\n            return argv[++i];\n        };\n        if      (flag == \"--capacity\")   a.capacity   = std::stoull(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--key-range\")  a.key_range  = std::stoull(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--n-ops\")      a.n_ops      = std::stoull(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--block-size\") a.block_size = std::stoi(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--warmups\")    a.warmups    = std::stoi(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--reps\")       a.reps       = std::stoi(get_val());\n        else if (flag == \"--seed\")       a.seed       = static_cast<std::uint32_t>(std::stoul(get_val()));\n        else if (flag == \"--tag\")        a.tag        = get_val();\n        else if (flag == \"--output-dir\") a.output_dir = get_val();\n        else { std::fprintf(stderr, \"Unknown flag: %s\\n\", flag.c_str()); die_usage(argv[0]); }\n    }\n    if (a.output_dir.empty())                     die_usage(argv[0]);\n    if (a.n_ops == 0)                             { std::fprintf(stderr, \"--n-ops must be > 0\\n\");     std::exit(1); }\n    if (a.key_range == 0)                         { std::fprintf(stderr, \"--key-range must be > 0\\n\"); std::exit(1); }\n    if (a.block_size % Table::tile_size != 0)     { std::fprintf(stderr, \"--block-size must be a multiple of tile_size (%d)\\n\", Table::tile_size); std::exit(1); }\n    if (a.block_size <= 0 || a.block_size > 1024) { std::fprintf(stderr, \"--block-size out of range\\n\"); std::exit(1); }\n    return a;\n}",
  "new_string": "Args parse_args(int argc, char** argv) {\n    Args a;\n    ArgParser p(argv[0]);\n    p.add(\"--capacity\",   a.capacity,   \"Table size in slots\")\n     .add(\"--key-range\",  a.key_range,  \"N in Uniform(0, N) for key generation\")\n     .add(\"--n-ops\",      a.n_ops,      \"Number of get attempts (also pre-fill count)\")\n     .add(\"--block-size\", a.block_size, \"Threads per CUDA block; multiple of tile_size\")\n     .add(\"--warmups\",    a.warmups,    \"Untimed warmup reps\")\n     .add(\"--reps\",       a.reps,       \"Timed reps\")\n     .add(\"--seed\",       a.seed,       \"cuRAND seed; one stream produces insert then get keys\")\n     .add(\"--tag\",        a.tag,        \"Free-form label written to every CSV row\")\n     .add(\"--output-dir\", a.output_dir, \"Required. get.csv is appended to here.\");\n    p.parse(argc, argv);\n\n    if (a.output_dir.empty())                     p.die_usage();\n    if (a.n_ops == 0)                             { std::fprintf(stderr, \"--n-ops must be > 0\\n\");     std::exit(1); }\n    if (a.key_range == 0)                         { std::fprintf(stderr, \"--key-range must be > 0\\n\"); std::exit(1); }\n    if (a.block_size % Table::tile_size != 0)     { std::fprintf(stderr, \"--block-size must be a multiple of tile_size (%d)\\n\", Table::tile_size); std::exit(1); }\n    if (a.block_size <= 0 || a.block_size > 1024) { std::fprintf(stderr, \"--block-size out of range\\n\"); std::exit(1); }\n    return a;\n}"
}
```

</details>

## User — 2026-05-19T14:29:16.051Z

<details><summary><code>tool result</code></summary>

```
1	# Makefile for gpurhh — header-only GPU Robin Hood hash table.
2	#
3	# Builds tests, examples, and benchmarks against the public header in
4	# include/. There is no library to compile (header-only). Each .cu file
5	# under tests/, examples/, and benchmarks/ becomes its own executable
6	# under build/.
7	#
8	# OPT controls optimization / debug flags. Default behavior:
9	#   - tests / examples: empty (nvcc's default; fastest to compile)
10	#   - benchmarks:       -O3 (unoptimized throughput numbers are meaningless)
11	#
12	# Override OPT to apply to every target uniformly:
13	#
14	#   make tests                          unoptimized
15	#   make tests     OPT=-O3              optimized tests
16	#   make benchmarks OPT=-O0             benchmarks unoptimized (rarely useful)
17	#   make test      OPT="-O0 -G -g"      debuggable test build
18	#
19	# Tests run via `make test`; benchmarks run via the standalone driver
20	# scripts/benchmark.sh (optionally with --with-baselines). The
21	# benchmark sweep is heavy enough that keeping its invocation out of
22	# the Makefile makes the inner-loop test target faster and clearer.
23	#
24	# EXTRA is appended to the nvcc command line on top of OPT. Useful for
25	# one-off additions (e.g. `make tests EXTRA=-lineinfo`).
26	#
27	# Common overrides:
28	#   make NVCC=/path/to/nvcc
29	#   make ARCH=sm_75
30	#   make CXX_STD=c++17
31	
32	NVCC    ?= nvcc
33	CXX_STD ?= c++20
34	ARCH    ?= sm_89
35	
36	OPT     ?=
37	EXTRA   ?=
38	
39	# Benchmark-specific OPT: -O3 if the user didn't set OPT explicitly, else
40	# whatever they passed. Lives in its own variable so the pattern rule
41	# below stays straightforward.
42	ifeq ($(strip $(OPT)),)
43	    BENCHMARK_OPT := -O3
44	else
45	    BENCHMARK_OPT := $(OPT)
46	endif
47	
48	INCLUDES   := -Iinclude
49	
50	NVCC_FLAGS       := -std=$(CXX_STD) -arch=$(ARCH) $(OPT)       $(INCLUDES) $(EXTRA)
51	NVCC_FLAGS_BENCHMARK := -std=$(CXX_STD) -arch=$(ARCH) $(BENCHMARK_OPT) $(INCLUDES) $(EXTRA)
52	
53	BUILD := build
54	
55	TEST_SRCS    := $(wildcard tests/test_*.cu)
56	TEST_BINS    := $(patsubst tests/%.cu,$(BUILD)/tests/%,$(TEST_SRCS))
57	
58	EXAMPLE_SRCS := $(wildcard examples/*.cu)
59	EXAMPLE_BINS := $(patsubst examples/%.cu,$(BUILD)/examples/%,$(EXAMPLE_SRCS))
60	
61	BENCHMARK_SRCS   := $(wildcard benchmarks/benchmark_*.cu)
62	
63	# Optional baseline libraries. Each is included only if the corresponding
64	# header subtree is present under external/include/ (which is gitignored —
65	# run scripts/setup-baselines.sh to populate, see that script for the
66	# pinned commits). When absent, the baselines silently drop out and
67	# `make benchmarks` produces just the gpurhh binaries.
68	EXTERNAL_INCLUDE := external/include
69	
70	HAVE_CUCO     := $(wildcard $(EXTERNAL_INCLUDE)/cuco)
71	HAVE_WARPCORE := $(wildcard $(EXTERNAL_INCLUDE)/warpcore)
72	
73	ifneq ($(HAVE_CUCO),)
74	    BENCHMARK_SRCS += $(wildcard benchmarks/baselines/cuco/benchmark_*.cu)
75	endif
76	ifneq ($(HAVE_WARPCORE),)
77	    BENCHMARK_SRCS += $(wildcard benchmarks/baselines/warpcore/benchmark_*.cu)
78	endif
79	
80	BENCHMARK_BINS   := $(patsubst benchmarks/%.cu,$(BUILD)/benchmarks/%,$(BENCHMARK_SRCS))
81	
82	# Treat any header change as a reason to rebuild everything. Adequate for a
83	# small project; revisit if compile times become an issue.
84	ALL_HEADERS := $(shell find include tests benchmarks -name "*.cuh" 2>/dev/null)
85	
86	.PHONY: all tests examples benchmarks test clean
87	
88	all: tests examples benchmarks
89	
90	tests:      $(TEST_BINS)
91	examples:   $(EXAMPLE_BINS)
92	benchmarks: $(BENCHMARK_BINS)
93	
94	test: $(TEST_BINS)
95		@set -e; for t in $(TEST_BINS); do echo "==> $$t"; "$$t"; done
96	
97	$(BUILD)/tests/%: tests/%.cu $(ALL_HEADERS)
98		@mkdir -p $(BUILD)/tests
99		$(NVCC) $(NVCC_FLAGS) $< -o $@
100	
101	$(BUILD)/examples/%: examples/%.cu $(ALL_HEADERS)
102		@mkdir -p $(BUILD)/examples
103		$(NVCC) $(NVCC_FLAGS) $< -o $@
104	
105	$(BUILD)/benchmarks/%: benchmarks/%.cu $(ALL_HEADERS)
106		@mkdir -p $(dir $@)
107		$(NVCC) $(NVCC_FLAGS_BENCHMARK) $< -o $@ -lcurand
108	
109	# Baseline pattern rule — more specific than the catch-all benchmark
110	# rule above, so it wins for targets under build/benchmarks/baselines/.
111	# Both cuco and warpcore share -Iexternal/include since the script
112	# consolidates them under that single tree. --extended-lambda and
113	# --expt-relaxed-constexpr are required by cuCollections's public API
114	# (and harmless for warpcore).
115	$(BUILD)/benchmarks/baselines/%: benchmarks/baselines/%.cu $(ALL_HEADERS)
116		@mkdir -p $(dir $@)
117		$(NVCC) $(NVCC_FLAGS_BENCHMARK) -I$(EXTERNAL_INCLUDE) --extended-lambda --expt-relaxed-constexpr $< -o $@ -lcurand
118	
119	clean:
120		rm -rf $(BUILD)
121	
```

</details>

Each is included only if the corresponding\n# header subtree is present under external/include/ (which is gitignored —\n# run scripts/setup-baselines.sh to populate, see that script for the\n# pinned commits). When absent, the baselines silently drop out and\n# `make benchmarks` produces just the gpurhh binaries.\nEXTERNAL_INCLUDE := external/include\n\nHAVE_CUCO     := $(wildcard $(EXTERNAL_INCLUDE)/cuco)\nHAVE_WARPCORE := $(wildcard $(EXTERNAL_INCLUDE)/warpcore)\n\nifneq ($(HAVE_CUCO),)\n    BENCHMARK_SRCS += $(wildcard benchmarks/baselines/cuco/benchmark_*.cu)\nendif\nifneq ($(HAVE_WARPCORE),)\n    BENCHMARK_SRCS += $(wildcard benchmarks/baselines/warpcore/benchmark_*.cu)\nendif\n\nBENCHMARK_BINS   := $(patsubst benchmarks/%.cu,$(BUILD)/benchmarks/%,$(BENCHMARK_SRCS))",
  "new_string": "# Two benchmark studies under benchmarks/: timing (apples-to-apples kernel\n# throughput vs. cuco / warpcore baselines) and memory (gpurhh's own\n# counter-instrumented probe/failure/hit study, no baselines).\nBENCHMARK_SRCS := \\\n    $(wildcard benchmarks/timing/benchmark_*.cu) \\\n    $(wildcard benchmarks/memory/benchmark_*.cu)\n\n# Optional baseline libraries. Each is included only if the corresponding\n# header subtree is present under external/include/ (which is gitignored —\n# run scripts/setup-baselines.sh to populate, see that script for the\n# pinned commits). When absent, the baselines silently drop out and\n# `make benchmarks` produces just the gpurhh binaries.\nEXTERNAL_INCLUDE := external/include\n\nHAVE_CUCO     := $(wildcard $(EXTERNAL_INCLUDE)/cuco)\nHAVE_WARPCORE := $(wildcard $(EXTERNAL_INCLUDE)/warpcore)\n\nifneq ($(HAVE_CUCO),)\n    BENCHMARK_SRCS += $(wildcard benchmarks/timing/baselines/cuco/benchmark_*.cu)\nendif\nifneq ($(HAVE_WARPCORE),)\n    BENCHMARK_SRCS += $(wildcard benchmarks/timing/baselines/warpcore/benchmark_*.cu)\nendif\n\nBENCHMARK_BINS   := $(patsubst benchmarks/%.cu,$(BUILD)/benchmarks/%,$(BENCHMARK_SRCS))"
}

## User — 2026-05-19T14:31:39.543Z

**← Tool result** (for `tool-813`)

<details><summary><code>tool result</code></summary>

```
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	#
3	# Run the gpurhh benchmark sweep, optionally followed by the external
4	# baseline sweeps (cuCollections, WarpCore).
5	#
6	# Writes per-workload CSV files (memcpy.csv, insert.csv, get.csv) and
7	# their counter-instrumented siblings (insert_counters.csv,
8	# get_counters.csv) plus a run_info.txt sidecar and a benchmark.log
9	# transcript to the output directory. The output dir defaults to
10	# output/<timestamp>/ under the repo root, so consecutive runs don't
11	# collide.
12	#
13	# Usage:
14	#     scripts/benchmark.sh <one-or-more-libraries> [output-dir]
15	#
16	# Pick at least one library to run. Combining flags runs each
17	# selected library's sweep in turn. Passing no flag prints this usage
18	# and exits — no default, since benchmark runs are heavy.
19	#
20	#   --gpurhh           Run the gpurhh comparison sweep (memcpy + insert + get).
21	#   --gpurhh-counters  Run the gpurhh counter-instrumented sweep
22	#                      (insert_counters + get_counters; produces probe
23	#                      / failure / hit columns).
24	#   --cuco             Run the cuCollections baseline.
25	#   --warpcore         Run the WarpCore baseline.
26	#   --all              Shortcut for every library whose binaries built;
27	#                      missing baselines are skipped with a notice
28	#                      rather than erroring.
29	#
30	# Library-specific flags are strict — they error if the corresponding
31	# binaries weren't built. --all is lenient. Comparison rows from all
32	# libraries land in the same insert.csv / get.csv; the `library` column
33	# distinguishes them. Counter rows go to separate _counters.csv files.
34	
35	set -euo pipefail
36	
37	# --- argument parsing ------------------------------------------------------
38	
39	print_usage() {
40	    sed -n '2,32p' "$0" | sed 's/^# //; s/^#//'
41	}
42	
43	RUN_GPURHH=0
44	RUN_GPURHH_COUNTERS=0
45	RUN_CUCO=0
46	RUN_WARPCORE=0
47	LENIENT=0
48	POSITIONAL=()
49	while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
50	    case "$1" in
51	        --gpurhh)           RUN_GPURHH=1;          shift ;;
52	        --gpurhh-counters)  RUN_GPURHH_COUNTERS=1; shift ;;
53	        --cuco)             RUN_CUCO=1;            shift ;;
54	        --warpcore)         RUN_WARPCORE=1;        shift ;;
55	        --all)              RUN_GPURHH=1; RUN_GPURHH_COUNTERS=1
56	                            RUN_CUCO=1; RUN_WARPCORE=1
57	                            LENIENT=1;             shift ;;
58	        --help|-h)          print_usage; exit 0 ;;
59	        --*)
60	            echo "Unknown flag: $1" >&2
61	            echo "Try '$0 --help' for usage." >&2
62	            exit 1
63	            ;;
64	        *)  POSITIONAL+=("$1"); shift ;;
65	    esac
66	done
67	set -- "${POSITIONAL[@]}"
68	
69	if [[ $((RUN_GPURHH + RUN_GPURHH_COUNTERS + RUN_CUCO + RUN_WARPCORE)) -eq 0 ]]; then
70	    print_usage >&2
71	    exit 1
72	fi
73	
74	REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
75	BUILD_DIR="${REPO_ROOT}/build/benchmarks"
76	
77	if [[ $# -ge 1 ]]; then
78	    OUTPUT_DIR="$1"
79	else
80	    OUTPUT_DIR="${REPO_ROOT}/output/$(date +%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S)"
81	fi
82	mkdir -p "${OUTPUT_DIR}"
83	
84	LOG_FILE="${OUTPUT_DIR}/benchmark.log"
85	exec > >(tee -a "${LOG_FILE}") 2>&1
86	
87	require_or_drop() {
88	    local label="$1"; shift
89	    for bin in "$@"; do
90	        if [[ ! -x "${bin}" ]]; then
91	            if [[ "${LENIENT}" -eq 1 ]]; then
92	                echo "==> ${label} binaries not built; skipping (--all is lenient)" >&2
93	                return 1
94	            fi
95	            echo "Missing executable: ${bin}" >&2
96	            echo "Build first with:   make benchmarks  (and scripts/setup-baselines.sh for baselines)" >&2
97	            exit 1
98	        fi
99	    done
100	    return 0
101	}
102	
103	if [[ "${RUN_GPURHH}" -eq 1 ]]; then
104	    require_or_drop "gpurhh" \
105	        "${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_memcpy" \
106	        "${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_insert" \
107	        "${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_get" \
108	        || RUN_GPURHH=0
109	fi
110	if [[ "${RUN_GPURHH_COUNTERS}" -eq 1 ]]; then
111	    require_or_drop "gpurhh-counters" \
112	        "${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_insert_counters" \
113	        "${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_get_counters" \
114	        || RUN_GPURHH_COUNTERS=0
115	fi
116	if [[ "${RUN_CUCO}" -eq 1 ]]; then
117	    require_or_drop "cuco" \
118	        "${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/cuco/benchmark_insert" \
119	        "${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/cuco/benchmark_get" \
120	        || RUN_CUCO=0
121	fi
122	if [[ "${RUN_WARPCORE}" -eq 1 ]]; then
123	    require_or_drop "warpcore" \
124	        "${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/warpcore/benchmark_insert" \
125	        "${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/warpcore/benchmark_get" \
126	        || RUN_WARPCORE=0
127	fi
128	
129	if [[ $((RUN_GPURHH + RUN_GPURHH_COUNTERS + RUN_CUCO + RUN_WARPCORE)) -eq 0 ]]; then
130	    echo "Nothing to run." >&2
131	    exit 1
132	fi
133	
134	echo "==> Writing CSV output and run_info to ${OUTPUT_DIR}"
135	
136	{
137	    echo "date:        $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
138	    echo "uname:       $(uname -srm)"
139	    echo "nvcc:        $(nvcc --version 2>/dev/null | tail -n1 || echo 'not found')"
140	    echo "nvidia-smi:  $(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name,driver_version,memory.total,power.limit --format=csv,noheader 2>/dev/null || echo 'not found')"
141	} > "${OUTPUT_DIR}/run_info.txt"
142	
143	# --- sweep grid ----------------------------------------------------------
144	#
145	# Capacity is fixed at 1 GiB worth of slots (DRAM-resident). α = 1 by
146	# default: key_range = capacity. We sweep n_ops via convenient
147	# multipliers, computed once below into a list of raw integer counts.
148	# block_size is swept for gpurhh; cuco/warpcore have their own internal
149	# launch shapes.
150	SLOT_BYTES=8
151	CAPACITY=$(( (1024 * 1024 * 1024) / SLOT_BYTES ))   # 1 GiB / 8 = 2^27
152	KEY_RANGE=${CAPACITY}                                # α = 1
153	BLOCK_SIZES=(64 128 256 512 1024)
154	TAG="sweep"
155	
156	# Each binary invocation runs $REPS timed reps, with the keys refilled
157	# from the continuing cuRAND stream before every rep. Statistically
158	# equivalent to $REPS independent seeds but with all CUDA / table /
159	# generator setup paid once instead of $REPS times.
160	REPS=16
161	SEED=1
162	
163	# n_ops multipliers: f * capacity. We compute the raw integers once so
164	# the rest of the script passes them as-is. Multipliers for gpurhh
165	# (which can run past 1.0) and for cuco/warpcore (capped at < 1.0 to
166	# stay below their fixed-capacity limits).
167	GPURHH_NOPS=()
168	for mul in 0.5 1.0 1.5 2.0 3.0; do
169	    GPURHH_NOPS+=($(python3 -c "print(int($mul * $CAPACITY))"))
170	done
171	BASELINE_NOPS=()
172	for mul in 0.5 0.7 0.85 0.95; do
173	    BASELINE_NOPS+=($(python3 -c "print(int($mul * $CAPACITY))"))
174	done
175	
176	# --- gpurhh comparison (memcpy + insert + get sweeps) --------------------
177	if [[ "${RUN_GPURHH}" -eq 1 ]]; then
178	    BYTES=$(( CAPACITY * SLOT_BYTES ))
179	    echo "==> memcpy baseline (${BYTES} B)"
180	    "${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_memcpy" \
181	        --output-dir "${OUTPUT_DIR}" \
182	        --bytes "${BYTES}" \
183	        --reps "${REPS}" \
184	        --seed "${SEED}" \
185	        --tag "${TAG}"
186	
187	    for n_ops in "${GPURHH_NOPS[@]}"; do
188	        for b in "${BLOCK_SIZES[@]}"; do
189	            echo "==> gpurhh insert n_ops=${n_ops} block=${b}"
190	            "${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_insert" \
191	                --output-dir "${OUTPUT_DIR}" \
192	                --capacity "${CAPACITY}" \
193	                --key-range "${KEY_RANGE}" \
194	                --n-ops "${n_ops}" \
195	                --block-size "${b}" \
196	                --reps "${REPS}" \
197	                --seed "${SEED}" \
198	                --tag "${TAG}"
199	
200	            echo "==> gpurhh get    n_ops=${n_ops} block=${b}"
201	            "${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_get" \
202	                --output-dir "${OUTPUT_DIR}" \
203	                --capacity "${CAPACITY}" \
204	                --key-range "${KEY_RANGE}" \
205	                --n-ops "${n_ops}" \
206	                --block-size "${b}" \
207	                --reps "${REPS}" \
208	                --seed "${SEED}" \
209	                --tag "${TAG}"
210	        done
211	    done
212	fi
213	
214	# --- gpurhh counters (study the design with instrumentation on) ----------
215	if [[ "${RUN_GPURHH_COUNTERS}" -eq 1 ]]; then
216	    for n_ops in "${GPURHH_NOPS[@]}"; do
217	        for b in "${BLOCK_SIZES[@]}"; do
218	            echo "==> gpurhh insert_counters n_ops=${n_ops} block=${b}"
219	            "${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_insert_counters" \
220	                --output-dir "${OUTPUT_DIR}" \
221	                --capacity "${CAPACITY}" \
222	                --key-range "${KEY_RANGE}" \
223	                --n-ops "${n_ops}" \
224	                --block-size "${b}" \
225	                --reps "${REPS}" \
226	                --seed "${SEED}" \
227	                --tag "${TAG}"
228	
229	            echo "==> gpurhh get_counters    n_ops=${n_ops} block=${b}"
230	            "${BUILD_DIR}/benchmark_get_counters" \
231	                --output-dir "${OUTPUT_DIR}" \
232	                --capacity "${CAPACITY}" \
233	                --key-range "${KEY_RANGE}" \
234	                --n-ops "${n_ops}" \
235	                --block-size "${b}" \
236	                --reps "${REPS}" \
237	                --seed "${SEED}" \
238	                --tag "${TAG}"
239	        done
240	    done
241	fi
242	
243	# --- cuCollections (fixed-capacity static_map; no block sweep) -----------
244	if [[ "${RUN_CUCO}" -eq 1 ]]; then
245	    CUCO_DIR="${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/cuco"
246	    for n_ops in "${BASELINE_NOPS[@]}"; do
247	        echo "==> cuco insert n_ops=${n_ops}"
248	        "${CUCO_DIR}/benchmark_insert" \
249	            --output-dir "${OUTPUT_DIR}" \
250	            --capacity "${CAPACITY}" \
251	            --key-range "${KEY_RANGE}" \
252	            --n-ops "${n_ops}" \
253	            --reps "${REPS}" \
254	            --seed "${SEED}" \
255	            --tag "${TAG}"
256	
257	        echo "==> cuco get    n_ops=${n_ops}"
258	        "${CUCO_DIR}/benchmark_get" \
259	            --output-dir "${OUTPUT_DIR}" \
260	            --capacity "${CAPACITY}" \
261	            --key-range "${KEY_RANGE}" \
262	            --n-ops "${n_ops}" \
263	            --reps "${REPS}" \
264	            --seed "${SEED}" \
265	            --tag "${TAG}"
266	    done
267	fi
268	
269	# --- WarpCore -----------------------------------------------------------
270	if [[ "${RUN_WARPCORE}" -eq 1 ]]; then
271	    WARPCORE_DIR="${BUILD_DIR}/baselines/warpcore"
272	    for n_ops in "${BASELINE_NOPS[@]}"; do
273	        echo "==> warpcore insert n_ops=${n_ops}"
274	        "${WARPCORE_DIR}/benchmark_insert" \
275	            --output-dir "${OUTPUT_DIR}" \
276	            --capacity "${CAPACITY}" \
277	            --key-range "${KEY_RANGE}" \
278	            --n-ops "${n_ops}" \
279	            --reps "${REPS}" \
280	            --seed "${SEED}" \
281	            --tag "${TAG}"
282	
283	        echo "==> warpcore get    n_ops=${n_ops}"
284	        "${WARPCORE_DIR}/benchmark_get" \
285	            --output-dir "${OUTPUT_DIR}" \
286	            --capacity "${CAPACITY}" \
287	            --key-range "${KEY_RANGE}" \
288	            --n-ops "${n_ops}" \
289	            --reps "${REPS}" \
290	            --seed "${SEED}" \
291	            --tag "${TAG}"
292	    done
293	fi
294	
295	echo "==> Done. Results in ${OUTPUT_DIR}"
296	
```

</details>

{
  "file_path": "[repo]/scripts/benchmark.sh",
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n#\n# Run the gpurhh benchmark sweeps and optional external baselines.\n#\n# The benchmark suite is split into two studies, each writing its own\n# CSV directory tree under the output dir:\n#\n#   timing/   apples-to-apples kernel throughput (memcpy.csv, insert.csv,\n#             get.csv). gpurhh + optional cuco / warpcore baselines all\n#             append to the same per-workload CSV; the `library` column\n#             distinguishes their rows.\n#\n#   memory/   gpurhh's counter-instrumented probe / failure / hit study\n#             (insert.csv, get.csv with extra count columns). No\n#             baselines — cuco / warpcore don't expose these counters.\n#\n# Also written: benchmark.log (transcript) and run_info.txt (host info),\n# both at the top of the output dir. Output defaults to\n# output/<timestamp>/ under the repo root, so consecutive runs don't\n# collide.\n#\n# Usage:\n#     scripts/benchmark.sh <one-or-more-flags> [output-dir]\n#\n# Pick at least one flag. Passing none prints this usage and exits.\n#\n#   --timing     Run gpurhh's timing sweep (memcpy + insert + get).\n#   --memory     Run gpurhh's memory-utilization sweep\n#                (probe / failure / hit counters).\n#   --cuco       Run the cuCollections baseline (timing only).\n#   --warpcore   Run the WarpCore baseline (timing only).\n#   --all        Shortcut for every flag above; missing binaries are\n#                skipped with a notice rather than erroring.\n#\n# Individual flags are strict — they error if the corresponding binaries\n# weren't built. --all is lenient.\n\nset -euo pipefail\n\n# --- argument parsing ------------------------------------------------------\n\nprint_usage() {\n    sed -n '2,38p' \"$0\" | sed 's/^# //; s/^#//'\n}\n\nRUN_TIMING=0\nRUN_MEMORY=0\nRUN_CUCO=0\nRUN_WARPCORE=0\nLENIENT=0\nPOSITIONAL=()\nwhile [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do\n    case \"$1\" in\n        --timing)    RUN_TIMING=1;   shift ;;\n        --memory)    RUN_MEMORY=1;   shift ;;\n        --cuco)      RUN_CUCO=1;     shift ;;\n        --warpcore)  RUN_WARPCORE=1; shift ;;\n        --all)       RUN_TIMING=1; RUN_MEMORY=1\n                     RUN_CUCO=1;   RUN_WARPCORE=1\n                     LENIENT=1;     shift ;;\n        --help|-h)   print_usage; exit 0 ;;\n        --*)\n            echo \"Unknown flag: $1\" >&2\n            echo \"Try '$0 --help' for usage.\" >&2\n            exit 1\n            ;;\n        *)  POSITIONAL+=(\"$1\"); shift ;;\n    esac\ndone\nset -- \"${POSITIONAL[@]}\"\n\nif [[ $((RUN_TIMING + RUN_MEMORY + RUN_CUCO + RUN_WARPCORE)) -eq 0 ]]; then\n    print_usage >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\nREPO_ROOT=\"$(cd \"$(dirname \"${BASH_SOURCE[0]}\")/..\" && pwd)\"\nBUILD_DIR=\"${REPO_ROOT}/build/benchmarks\"\nTIMING_BIN=\"${BUILD_DIR}/timing\"\nMEMORY_BIN=\"${BUILD_DIR}/memory\"\nCUCO_BIN=\"${TIMING_BIN}/baselines/cuco\"\nWARPCORE_BIN=\"${TIMING_BIN}/baselines/warpcore\"\n\nif [[ $# -ge 1 ]]; then\n    OUTPUT_DIR=\"$1\"\nelse\n    OUTPUT_DIR=\"${REPO_ROOT}/output/$(date +%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S)\"\nfi\nTIMING_OUT=\"${OUTPUT_DIR}/timing\"\nMEMORY_OUT=\"${OUTPUT_DIR}/memory\"\nmkdir -p \"${OUTPUT_DIR}\" \"${TIMING_OUT}\" \"${MEMORY_OUT}\"\n\nLOG_FILE=\"${OUTPUT_DIR}/benchmark.log\"\nexec > >(tee -a \"${LOG_FILE}\") 2>&1\n\nrequire_or_drop() {\n    local label=\"$1\"; shift\n    for bin in \"$@\"; do\n        if [[ ! -x \"${bin}\" ]]; then\n            if [[ \"${LENIENT}\" -eq 1 ]]; then\n                echo \"==> ${label} binaries not built; skipping (--all is lenient)\" >&2\n                return 1\n            fi\n            echo \"Missing executable: ${bin}\" >&2\n            echo \"Build first with:   make benchmarks  (and scripts/setup-baselines.sh for baselines)\" >&2\n            exit 1\n        fi\n    done\n    return 0\n}\n\nif [[ \"${RUN_TIMING}\" -eq 1 ]]; then\n    require_or_drop \"timing\" \\\n        \"${TIMING_BIN}/benchmark_memcpy\" \\\n        \"${TIMING_BIN}/benchmark_insert\" \\\n        \"${TIMING_BIN}/benchmark_get\" \\\n        || RUN_TIMING=0\nfi\nif [[ \"${RUN_MEMORY}\" -eq 1 ]]; then\n    require_or_drop \"memory\" \\\n        \"${MEMORY_BIN}/benchmark_insert\" \\\n        \"${MEMORY_BIN}/benchmark_get\" \\\n        || RUN_MEMORY=0\nfi\nif [[ \"${RUN_CUCO}\" -eq 1 ]]; then\n    require_or_drop \"cuco\" \\\n        \"${CUCO_BIN}/benchmark_insert\" \\\n        \"${CUCO_BIN}/benchmark_get\" \\\n        || RUN_CUCO=0\nfi\nif [[ \"${RUN_WARPCORE}\" -eq 1 ]]; then\n    require_or_drop \"warpcore\" \\\n        \"${WARPCORE_BIN}/benchmark_insert\" \\\n        \"${WARPCORE_BIN}/benchmark_get\" \\\n        || RUN_WARPCORE=0\nfi\n\nif [[ $((RUN_TIMING + RUN_MEMORY + RUN_CUCO + RUN_WARPCORE)) -eq 0 ]]; then\n    echo \"Nothing to run.\" >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\necho \"==> Writing CSV output and run_info to ${OUTPUT_DIR}\"\n\n{\n    echo \"date:        $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)\"\n    echo \"uname:       $(uname -srm)\"\n    echo \"nvcc:        $(nvcc --version 2>/dev/null | tail -n1 || echo 'not found')\"\n    echo \"nvidia-smi:  $(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name,driver_version,memory.total,power.limit --format=csv,noheader 2>/dev/null || echo 'not found')\"\n} > \"${OUTPUT_DIR}/run_info.txt\"\n\n# --- sweep grid ----------------------------------------------------------\n#\n# Capacity is fixed at 1 GiB worth of slots (DRAM-resident). α = 1 by\n# default: key_range = capacity. We sweep n_ops via convenient\n# multipliers, computed once below into a list of raw integer counts.\n# block_size is swept for gpurhh; cuco/warpcore have their own internal\n# launch shapes.\nSLOT_BYTES=8\nCAPACITY=$(( (1024 * 1024 * 1024) / SLOT_BYTES ))   # 1 GiB / 8 = 2^27\nKEY_RANGE=${CAPACITY}                                # α = 1\nBLOCK_SIZES=(64 128 256 512 1024)\nTAG=\"sweep\"\n\n# Each binary invocation runs $REPS timed reps, with the keys refilled\n# from the continuing cuRAND stream before every rep. Statistically\n# equivalent to $REPS independent seeds but with all CUDA / table /\n# generator setup paid once instead of $REPS times.\nREPS=16\nSEED=1\n\n# n_ops multipliers: f * capacity. We compute the raw integers once so\n# the rest of the script passes them as-is. Multipliers for gpurhh\n# (which can run past 1.0) and for cuco/warpcore (capped at < 1.0 to\n# stay below their fixed-capacity limits).\nGPURHH_NOPS=()\nfor mul in 0.5 1.0 1.5 2.0 3.0; do\n    GPURHH_NOPS+=($(python3 -c \"print(int($mul * $CAPACITY))\"))\ndone\nBASELINE_NOPS=()\nfor mul in 0.5 0.7 0.85 0.95; do\n    BASELINE_NOPS+=($(python3 -c \"print(int($mul * $CAPACITY))\"))\ndone\n\n# --- timing sweep (memcpy + insert + get for gpurhh) ---------------------\nif [[ \"${RUN_TIMING}\" -eq 1 ]]; then\n    BYTES=$(( CAPACITY * SLOT_BYTES ))\n    echo \"==> memcpy baseline (${BYTES} B)\"\n    \"${TIMING_BIN}/benchmark_memcpy\" \\\n        --output-dir \"${TIMING_OUT}\" \\\n        --bytes \"${BYTES}\" \\\n        --reps \"${REPS}\" \\\n        --seed \"${SEED}\" \\\n        --tag \"${TAG}\"\n\n    for n_ops in \"${GPURHH_NOPS[@]}\"; do\n        for b in \"${BLOCK_SIZES[@]}\"; do\n            echo \"==> timing insert n_ops=${n_ops} block=${b}\"\n            \"${TIMING_BIN}/benchmark_insert\" \\\n                --output-dir \"${TIMING_OUT}\" \\\n                --capacity \"${CAPACITY}\" \\\n                --key-range \"${KEY_RANGE}\" \\\n                --n-ops \"${n_ops}\" \\\n                --block-size \"${b}\" \\\n                --reps \"${REPS}\" \\\n                --seed \"${SEED}\" \\\n                --tag \"${TAG}\"\n\n            echo \"==> timing get    n_ops=${n_ops} block=${b}\"\n            \"${TIMING_BIN}/benchmark_get\" \\\n                --output-dir \"${TIMING_OUT}\" \\\n                --capacity \"${CAPACITY}\" \\\n                --key-range \"${KEY_RANGE}\" \\\n                --n-ops \"${n_ops}\" \\\n                --block-size \"${b}\" \\\n                --reps \"${REPS}\" \\\n                --seed \"${SEED}\" \\\n                --tag \"${TAG}\"\n        done\n    done\nfi\n\n# --- memory sweep (counter-instrumented gpurhh study) --------------------\nif [[ \"${RUN_MEMORY}\" -eq 1 ]]; then\n    for n_ops in \"${GPURHH_NOPS[@]}\"; do\n        for b in \"${BLOCK_SIZES[@]}\"; do\n            echo \"==> memory insert n_ops=${n_ops} block=${b}\"\n            \"${MEMORY_BIN}/benchmark_insert\" \\\n                --output-dir \"${MEMORY_OUT}\" \\\n                --capacity \"${CAPACITY}\" \\\n                --key-range \"${KEY_RANGE}\" \\\n                --n-ops \"${n_ops}\" \\\n                --block-size \"${b}\" \\\n                --reps \"${REPS}\" \\\n                --seed \"${SEED}\" \\\n                --tag \"${TAG}\"\n\n            echo \"==> memory get    n_ops=${n_ops} block=${b}\"\n            \"${MEMORY_BIN}/benchmark_get\" \\\n                --output-dir \"${MEMORY_OUT}\" \\\n                --capacity \"${CAPACITY}\" \\\n                --key-range \"${KEY_RANGE}\" \\\n                --n-ops \"${n_ops}\" \\\n                --block-size \"${b}\" \\\n                --reps \"${REPS}\" \\\n                --seed \"${SEED}\" \\\n                --tag \"${TAG}\"\n        done\n    done\nfi\n\n# --- cuCollections baseline (timing only) --------------------------------\nif [[ \"${RUN_CUCO}\" -eq 1 ]]; then\n    for n_ops in \"${BASELINE_NOPS[@]}\"; do\n        echo \"==> cuco insert n_ops=${n_ops}\"\n        \"${CUCO_BIN}/benchmark_insert\" \\\n            --output-dir \"${TIMING_OUT}\" \\\n            --capacity \"${CAPACITY}\" \\\n            --key-range \"${KEY_RANGE}\" \\\n            --n-ops \"${n_ops}\" \\\n            --reps \"${REPS}\" \\\n            --seed \"${SEED}\" \\\n            --tag \"${TAG}\"\n\n        echo \"==> cuco get    n_ops=${n_ops}\"\n        \"${CUCO_BIN}/benchmark_get\" \\\n            --output-dir \"${TIMING_OUT}\" \\\n            --capacity \"${CAPACITY}\" \\\n            --key-range \"${KEY_RANGE}\" \\\n            --n-ops \"${n_ops}\" \\\n            --reps \"${REPS}\" \\\n            --seed \"${SEED}\" \\\n            --tag \"${TAG}\"\n    done\nfi\n\n# --- WarpCore baseline (timing only) ------------------------------------\nif [[ \"${RUN_WARPCORE}\" -eq 1 ]]; then\n    for n_ops in \"${BASELINE_NOPS[@]}\"; do\n        echo \"==> warpcore insert n_ops=${n_ops}\"\n        \"${WARPCORE_BIN}/benchmark_insert\" \\\n            --output-dir \"${TIMING_OUT}\" \\\n            --capacity \"${CAPACITY}\" \\\n            --key-range \"${KEY_RANGE}\" \\\n            --n-ops \"${n_ops}\" \\\n            --reps \"${REPS}\" \\\n            --seed \"${SEED}\" \\\n            --tag \"${TAG}\"\n\n        echo \"==> warpcore get    n_ops=${n_ops}\"\n        \"${WARPCORE_BIN}/benchmark_get\" \\\n            --output-dir \"${TIMING_OUT}\" \\\n            --capacity \"${CAPACITY}\" \\\n            --key-range \"${KEY_RANGE}\" \\\n            --n-ops \"${n_ops}\" \\\n            --reps \"${REPS}\" \\\n            --seed \"${SEED}\" \\\n            --tag \"${TAG}\"\n    done\nfi\n\necho \"==> Done. Results in ${OUTPUT_DIR}\"\n"

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T14:33:07.112Z

Clean — `die_usage` is now the ArgParser member; `GPURHH_BENCHMARK_COUNTERS` correctly stays in the two memory binaries; no stale `_counters` references.

Summary:

- **`benchmarks/benchmarks.cuh`**: added the `ArgParser` class (~70 lines, supports `std::size_t / int / std::uint32_t / std::string / std::filesystem::path`, declarative `.add(flag, target, help)` fluent API, generates usage from registered flags, handles `--help`).
- **Directory reorg** (all via `git mv` so renames are tracked):
  ```
  benchmarks/
  ├── benchmarks.cuh
  ├── timing/{benchmark_memcpy,benchmark_insert,benchmark_get}.cu
  │   └── baselines/{cuco,warpcore}/{benchmark_insert,benchmark_get}.cu
  └── memory/{benchmark_insert,benchmark_get}.cu
  ```
- **CSV output**: the memory binaries now write `insert.csv` / `get.csv` (no `_counters` suffix). The script passes `--output-dir <ts>/timing` or `<ts>/memory` so they never collide.
- **`Makefile`**: globs `benchmarks/timing/*` and `benchmarks/memory/*`, baseline pattern rule retargeted to `build/benchmarks/timing/baselines/`.
- **`scripts/benchmark.sh`**: flags renamed `--gpurhh` → `--timing`, `--gpurhh-counters` → `--memory`. Baseline binaries land in `timing/` so their rows mix into the timing CSVs alongside gpurhh's via the `library` column.
- **Each binary**: ~25 lines of parse_args boilerplate replaced by ~10 lines of declarative `.add()` calls. `die_usage` is now the parser's member method, so no per-binary copies.

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T14:54:02.059Z

`die_usage` is inherited from Perl/sh idiom where `die "message"` means "print + exit fatally" — common in shell scripts and Perl, not idiomatic C++. I picked it up from the previous hand-rolled parsers without thinking. Cleaner options:

- `print_usage()` — explicit about what it does; `[[noreturn]]` makes the exit visible at the declaration
- `usage()` — short, very common in POSIX tools

I'd lean `print_usage()`. Want me to rename?

For the power-of-2 doc: let me find the notes and the actual assumption sites first.

## User — 2026-05-19T14:54:20.199Z

**← Tool result** (for `tool-819`)

<details><summary><code>tool result</code></summary>

```
65	
66	**Probe distance.**
67	We do *not* store probe distance in the slot.
68	It is derivable as `(slot_index - hash(key)) mod capacity`.
69	Storing it would cost bits we'd rather give to the payload, and the derivation is a single subtract once we've read the slot.
70	
71	## Capacity, hash function, load factor
72	
73	- Capacity is a power of two so `mod` becomes a mask.
74	  Users specify a target load factor (default 0.7); we round capacity up to `next_pow2(N / load_factor)`.
75	- Hash function is a template parameter; default is a finalizer-style mixer (e.g. the splitmix64/Murmur3 finalizer) that is cheap on GPU and decorrelates structured keys well.
76	- Robin Hood tolerates higher load than linear probing, but on a GPU the warp tail-latency argument pushes us back toward 0.7–0.8 rather than 0.9.
77	
78	## Reduction operators
79	
80	When `View::insert` encounters an existing entry for the same key, the new value is combined with the existing one through a `Reduction` functor — a template parameter of `HashTable`, defaulting to `gpurhh::replace_op`.
81	Stateless and trivially copyable, the functor sits inside the `View` by value:
82	
83	```cpp
84	struct replace_op {
85	    template <class T>
86	    __device__ T operator()(T /*existing*/, T incoming) const noexcept { return incoming; }
87	};
88	
89	struct sum_op {
90	    template <class T>
91	    __device__ T operator()(T existing, T incoming) const noexcept { return existing + incoming; }
92	};
93	```
94	
95	`replace_op` gives last-writer-wins semantics. `sum_op` accumulates values for repeated-key inserts — useful as a parallel histogram or counter primitive. Users can define their own functor with the same signature.
96	
97	For commutative and associative reductions like `sum_op`, the final stored value is fully deterministic regardless of how the CAS retries interleave across tiles: the result is just the reduction of all inserted values for that key. For non-commutative reductions, the outcome depends on which writers win their CAS races.
98	
99	## Parallel insertion algorithm
```

</details>

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T14:56:01.501Z

**→ Tool call: `Edit`** (id `tool-825`)

<details><summary><code>tool input</code></summary>

```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "[repo]/notes/design.md",
  "old_string": "## Capacity, hash function, load factor\n\n- Capacity is a power of two so `mod` becomes a mask.\n  Users specify a target load factor (default 0.7); we round capacity up to `next_pow2(N / load_factor)`.\n- Hash function is a template parameter; default is a finalizer-style mixer (e.g. the splitmix64/Murmur3 finalizer) that is cheap on GPU and decorrelates structured keys well.\n- Robin Hood tolerates higher load than linear probing, but on a GPU the warp tail-latency argument pushes us back toward 0.7–0.8 rather than 0.9.",
  "new_string": "## Capacity, hash function, load factor\n\n- Capacity is a power of two so `mod` becomes a mask.\n  Users specify a target load factor (default 0.7); we round capacity up to `next_pow2(N / load_factor)`.\n- Hash function is a template parameter; default is a finalizer-style mixer (e.g. the splitmix64/Murmur3 finalizer) that is cheap on GPU and decorrelates structured keys well.\n- Robin Hood tolerates higher load than linear probing, but on a GPU the warp tail-latency argument pushes us back toward 0.7–0.8 rather than 0.9.\n\n### Where the power-of-two assumption is load-bearing\n\nThe \"capacity is a power of two\" constraint is not just a convenience — it's wired into multiple hot-path operations. Anyone touching these sites should know that breaking the invariant would require switching every `& mask` back to a `% capacity`, which is materially slower on GPU (multi-cycle integer divmod vs. a single AND).\n\nIn `include/gpurhh/hash_table.cuh`:\n\n- **`HashTable` constructor**: `next_pow2(min_capacity)` rounds the user-supplied capacity up; `capacity_mask_ = capacity_ - 1` is cached for use across the hot paths. Capacity can grow by up to 2× over what the user asked for. The trade-off is a one-cycle mask vs. a multi-cycle modulus on every probe step.\n- **Home-slot computation** (insert and get): `home_slot = hash(key) & capacity_mask_` (and `home_bucket = home_slot / bucket_size`). Replacing `&` with `%` would also require capacity_mask_ to be replaced by the raw capacity, doubling the mod cost on every probe.\n- **Probe wrap-around**: `bucket_idx = (home_bucket + probe) & bucket_mask`, where `bucket_mask = num_buckets - 1`. Since `num_buckets = capacity / bucket_size` and both are powers of two, the bucket count is also pow-2 and admits a mask.\n- **Probe-distance derivation** (used inside the Robin Hood swap check): `resident_probe = (bucket_idx - resident_home) & bucket_mask` — this handles wrap-around correctly precisely because `bucket_mask = num_buckets - 1`.\n- **Tile size and warp size**: independently asserted pow-2 via `static_assert((tile_size & (tile_size - 1)) == 0, ...)`. Used by `tiles_per_block = block_size / tile_size` and by `cg::tiled_partition<tile_size>`, which itself requires a pow-2 tile size.\n\nIn benchmarks (`benchmarks/benchmarks.cuh`):\n\n- **Key generation** (`map_to_range`): folds cuRAND bits to `[0, range)` via `x & (range - 1)` when range is a power of two, falling back to `x % range` otherwise. The sweep driver always uses `key_range = capacity`, so the mask path is taken in practice.\n\nA subtler dependency lives in the **default hash function**. With a pow-2 mask, only the low bits of `hash(key)` reach `capacity_mask_` — the high bits are thrown away. If a user supplied an identity hash on structured keys (e.g. dense integer IDs), low-bit clustering would map directly into slot clustering and probe lengths would explode. The default `default_hash<uint32_t>` is `fmix32`, a finalizer-style mixer that spreads bits well, so the low bits of `hash(k)` are pseudo-uniform even when keys are not. Users supplying their own `Hash` should preserve this property; passing a non-mixing hash with a pow-2 capacity is the textbook way to break a fast hash table."
}
```

</details>

## Assistant — 2026-05-19T14:56:11.669Z

Both changes done.

1. **Rename**: `die_usage` → `print_usage` everywhere. The `die_*` prefix was Perl/sh idiom ("die means print + exit") inherited from the original hand-rolled parsers; not idiomatic C++. `[[noreturn]]` already makes the exit visible at the declaration.

2. **Design doc**: added a `### Where the power-of-two assumption is load-bearing` subsection under `## Capacity, hash function, load factor`. It enumerates each hot-path site that depends on the invariant — constructor's `next_pow2`, home-slot computation, probe wrap-around, probe-distance derivation, tile-size assertion, plus the benchmark `map_to_range` kernel — and calls out the subtle interaction with the hash function (with a pow-2 mask only the low bits reach the slot index, so any user-supplied `Hash` must mix bits well or the table degrades, which is why `default_hash` is `fmix32` and not identity).
